| **10-99T9** | Vehicle Collision — D.A.R.P. requested, waited 30 min, second request, resumed patrol |
| **10-90Z** | Gone on Arrival — vehicle not at scene, no information on removal |

## City-Involved Agency Routing

| Condition | Agency |
|-----------|--------|
| Traffic signs, stanchions, signal lights, lampposts | Department of Transportation |
| Parkway, highway, wall, curb, fence, guide rail | DOT Bureau of Highways, Legal Dept |
| Parks Department property; trees on public property | Department of Parks |
| Fire alarm box/post | FDNY Actions and Claims Unit |
| City hydrants | DEP **and** FDNY Actions and Claims |
| School bus collision | DOE Office of Pupil Transportation |
| Transit facility debris (elevated MTA line) | NYC Transit Law Department |
| Ferry, bridge, tunnel, dock, airport, seaplane base | Agency concerned |

## Department Property Forwarding

| Property | Forward To |
|----------|-----------|
| Booths, buildings | Deputy Commissioner — Management & Budget, Facilities Management Division |
| Horses | Mounted Unit |
| Aircraft, launches | Office of the Chief of Special Operations |

## Department Vehicle Collision (217-06) Key Numbers

| Item | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Reports prepared | 3: PAR (MV104AN), MV104-L, CR-PDV |
| Serial numbers | 3: PAR serial, Boro #, FSD # |
| PAR copies | Quadruplicate: 1st to Operator, 3 to Desk Officer |
| Photos | 2 sets showing damage to ALL vehicles; in Jewelry Security Envelope |
| Fax deadline | Within 24 hours to Personnel Safety Desk (tour of occurrence if possible; confirm 0700-1830) |
| Boro # location | Telephone Record |
| FSD # location | On CR-PDV AND top of PAR |
| CR-PDV Parts | A = Investigating Supervisor; B = Review Board recommendations; C = C.O. approval/disapproval |
| Buff copy of CR-PDV | To Patrol Borough — information only, NO photos ("Buff to Boro, No Photo") |
| White copy of CR-PDV | To Personnel Safety Desk with photos and copy of PAR |
| Pink copy of CR-PDV | Operator's Personal Folder (C.O. must review first) |
| Crossover PAR copy | Faxed to Corp Counsel AND Highway District by A-QOL-O |
| Not forwarded to XO | MV104 and/or MV104L (only PAR, CR-PDV, voucher copy, photos go to XO) |

## Serious Traffic Violations (VTL) Referenced in 217-01

| VTL Section | Violation |
|-------------|-----------|
| 511 | Suspended/Revoked License |
| 600 | Leaving the scene |
| 1100-1212 | Traffic signs, signals, and markings |
| 1120-1131 | Driving on right side of roadway, overtaking and passing |
| 1140-1146a | Right of way |
| 1160-1166 | Turning/starting and signals on stopping/turning |
| 1170-1176 | Special stops required |
| 1180-1182b | Speed restrictions |
| 1212 | Reckless driving |

## Who Does NOT Need to Produce Insurance Cards

| Exempt Category |
|----------------|
| Taxis |
| Buses |
| Rented Vehicles |
| Public Service Commission/ICC vehicles |
| GOVT. Owned vehicles |
| Certain Farm Vehicles |
| Diplomats (covered by P.G. 209-03) |

## Key Forms

| Form | Number | Source |
|------|--------|--------|
| MOTOR VEHICLE COLLISION AND MECHANISM REPORT | PD301-151 | P.G. 217-03 |
| WITNESS STATEMENT — VEHICLE COLLISION | PD301-061 | P.G. 217-03 |
| PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE | PD521-141 | P.G. 217-03 |
| PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE WORKSHEET | PD521-141A | P.G. 217-03 |
| Police Accident Report | MV104AN | Multiple |
| Early Notification of Fatal Accident | MV-104EN | P.G. 217-03 |
| Police Report for Fatal Motor Vehicle Accidents | MV-104D | P.G. 217-03 |
| ACCIDENT REPORT — CITY INVOLVED | PD301-155 | P.G. 217-04 |
| COMPLAINT REPORT WORKSHEET | PD313-152A | P.G. 217-05 |
| COLLISION INFORMATION EXCHANGE | PD301-157 | P.G. 217-05 |
| UNUSUAL OCCURRENCE REPORT | PD370-152 | P.G. 217-08 |
| REPORT OF VIOLATION | PD672-151 | P.G. 217-09 |
| FIELD REPORT | 313-1511 | P.G. 217-11 |
| CHEMICAL TEST WARRANT APPLICATION | PD374-041 | P.G. 217-16 |
| Report of Refusal to Submit to Chemical Test | NYSDMV AA-134 | P.G. 217-16 |
| COLLISION REPORT — PD VEHICLE | CR-PDV | P.G. 217-06 |
| Report of Motor Vehicle Accident (civilian form) | MV104 | P.G. 217-06 |
| MOS Driver License Report | MV104L | P.G. 217-06 |
| Truck and Bus Supplemental PAR | MV104S | P.G. 217-01 |
| UF50 (Official Letterhead) | UF50 | P.G. 217-06 (insurance surcharge) |

questions.text: According to P.G. 217-03, who determines whether an injury qualifies as a "critical injury" at a vehicle collision scene?
options: B) The desk officer after reviewing hospital records
C) Emergency Medical Services (EMS) personnel based on on-scene assessment
D) The Highway Unit collision investigator
questions.answerFull: C) Per P.G. 217-03, a critical injury is determined based upon the **on-scene assessment of EMS personnel**.
questions.explanation: Per P.G. 217-03, a critical injury is determined based upon the **on-scene assessment of EMS personnel**.

questions.text: Under P.G. 217-03, within what timeframe must the Early Notification of Fatal Accident (MV-104EN) be faxed to the NYS Department of Motor Vehicles?
questions.answerFull: B) The Early Notification of Fatal Accident must be faxed to NYS DMV within **twenty-four (24) hours**.
questions.explanation: The Early Notification of Fatal Accident must be faxed to NYS DMV within **twenty-four (24) hours**.

questions.text: According to P.G. 217-04, persons involved in a city-involved accident must file claims with which office, and within what timeframe?
options: B) Comptroller of the City of New York within 90 days
C) City Law Department within 30 days
D) Commissioner of Finance within 120 days
questions.answerFull: B) Claims against the City must be filed with the **Comptroller of the City of New York** within **ninety (90) days**.
questions.explanation: Claims against the City must be filed with the **Comptroller of the City of New York** within **ninety (90) days**.

questions.text: Under P.G. 217-04, damage to a city hydrant requires notification to which agency or agencies?
options: A) Department of Environmental Protection only
B) FDNY Actions and Claims Unit only
C) Department of Environmental Protection AND FDNY Actions and Claims Unit
D) Department of Transportation and FDNY
questions.answerFull: C) Hydrant damage requires dual notification: **DEP and FDNY Actions and Claims Unit**.
questions.explanation: Hydrant damage requires dual notification: **DEP and FDNY Actions and Claims Unit**.

questions.text: According to P.G. 217-05, under what authority may a uniformed member issue a summons for leaving the scene of a property-damage-only collision without having observed the offense?
options: B) Vehicle and Traffic Law, Section 602
D) The member may not issue a summons without observing the offense
questions.answerFull: B) VTL **Section 602** authorizes issuing a summons for leaving the scene of a property-damage-only collision even without witnessing the offense.
questions.explanation: VTL **Section 602** authorizes issuing a summons for leaving the scene of a property-damage-only collision even without witnessing the offense.

questions.text: Per P.G. 217-08, within how many hours must a pilot report an aircraft accident to the desk officer, and what is the consequence of failure?
questions.answerFull: B) The pilot must report within **ten (10) hours**; failure to do so is a **misdemeanor**.
questions.explanation: The pilot must report within **ten (10) hours**; failure to do so is a **misdemeanor**.

questions.text: Under P.G. 217-09, who is authorized to make tow company notifications under the Directed Accident Response Program?
options: A) The desk officer at the precinct of occurrence
B) The patrol supervisor at the scene
C) The D.A.R.P. operator at the Communications Section
D) Any uniformed member at the scene
questions.answerFull: C) The D.A.R.P. operator at the **Communications Section** maintains the list and makes notifications on strict rotation. **Precinct commands are NOT authorized** to make notifications.
questions.explanation: The D.A.R.P. operator at the **Communications Section** maintains the list and makes notifications on strict rotation. **Precinct commands are NOT authorized** to make notifications.

questions.text: According to P.G. 217-09, what information about the disabled vehicle must NOT be transmitted over the radio when requesting a D.A.R.P. tow?
questions.answerFull: C) Members must **NOT transmit the make, model, or year** of the disabled vehicle. Only color, body type, and plate number are transmitted.
questions.explanation: Members must **NOT transmit the make, model, or year** of the disabled vehicle. Only color, body type, and plate number are transmitted.

questions.text: Under P.G. 217-09, if a D.A.R.P. tow company does not arrive within the required time, what should the uniformed member do?
options: A) Wait an additional 30 minutes and then leave
B) Request Communications to cancel and assign the next company in rotation; prepare a REPORT OF VIOLATION
C) Contact the tow company directly and demand response
D) Have the desk officer arrange an alternative tow
questions.answerFull: B) After **30 minutes**, the member requests Communications to **cancel** the initial company, assign the **next in rotation**, and prepares a **REPORT OF VIOLATION (PD672-151)** for the non-responding company.
questions.explanation: After **30 minutes**, the member requests Communications to **cancel** the initial company, assign the **next in rotation**, and prepares a **REPORT OF VIOLATION (PD672-151)** for the non-responding company.

questions.text: According to P.G. 217-09, what is the D.A.R.P. disposition code for a vehicle collision where a D.A.R.P. tow was requested, the member waited 30 minutes, made a second request, and resumed patrol?
questions.answerFull: D) Code **10-99T9** indicates: D.A.R.P. requested, waited thirty minutes, made second request, resumed patrol.
questions.explanation: Code **10-99T9** indicates: D.A.R.P. requested, waited thirty minutes, made second request, resumed patrol.

questions.text: Per P.G. 217-09, which of the following vehicles is EXEMPT from D.A.R.P. towing requirements?
options: A) Any vehicle with more than $5,000 in damage
C) Vehicles registered out of state
D) Vehicles less than one year old
questions.answerFull: B) **Government vehicles** are exempt under Administrative Code Section 20-524(c). Their operators may call their own tow service.
questions.explanation: **Government vehicles** are exempt under Administrative Code Section 20-524(c). Their operators may call their own tow service.

questions.text: Under P.G. 217-10, if Department property (other than vehicles) is damaged, who investigates and prepares the report?
options: A) The uniformed member who discovered the damage
questions.answerFull: C) The desk officer directs the **patrol supervisor** to investigate the facts and prepare the report.
questions.explanation: The desk officer directs the **patrol supervisor** to investigate the facts and prepare the report.

questions.text: According to P.G. 217-14, reference markers on limited access highways are what color and size?
questions.answerFull: B) Reference markers are **green**, measuring **9 inches × 8 inches**, placed every 1/10 of a mile.
questions.explanation: Reference markers are **green**, measuring **9 inches × 8 inches**, placed every 1/10 of a mile.

questions.text: Per P.G. 217-16, under what circumstances can a chemical test warrant NOT be applied for?
options: A) When the collision occurred more than 24 hours ago
B) When the operator is the only person who suffered serious physical injury
C) When the operator's BAC is below 0.08
D) When the operator is a juvenile
questions.answerFull: B) If the operator is the **only person** with serious physical injury and **no other person** suffers serious injury or death, a chemical test warrant **cannot** be applied for.
questions.explanation: If the operator is the **only person** with serious physical injury and **no other person** suffers serious injury or death, a chemical test warrant **cannot** be applied for.

questions.text: Under P.G. 217-16, if one hour has passed and the assistant district attorney has not contacted the arresting officer, what is the desk officer's next step?
options: A) Cancel the chemical test warrant request
B) Contact the ADA supervisor and Operations Unit; then contact ADA supervisor every 30 minutes
C) Have the arresting officer proceed without ADA consultation
D) Request a Department attorney from Legal Bureau
questions.answerFull: B) After one hour, the desk officer contacts the **ADA supervisor and Operations Unit**, then contacts the ADA supervisor **every thirty (30) minutes** thereafter until the ADA is reached.
questions.explanation: After one hour, the desk officer contacts the **ADA supervisor and Operations Unit**, then contacts the ADA supervisor **every thirty (30) minutes** thereafter until the ADA is reached.

questions.text: According to P.G. 217-06, when a Department vehicle collision results in Serious Physical Injury AND a Serious Traffic Violation, who is responsible for administering a Breathalyzer (PBT) to the MOS operator?
questions.answerFull: C) Per P.G. 217-06, the **Patrol Supervisor** gives the PBT to the MOS operator when there is SPI AND STV — unless the operator is removed to the hospital (97H). If the Patrol Supervisor is not qualified, they request a qualified **Highway Unit Supervisor** to administer it.
questions.explanation: Per P.G. 217-06, the **Patrol Supervisor** gives the PBT to the MOS operator when there is SPI AND STV — unless the operator is removed to the hospital (97H). If the Patrol Supervisor is not qualified, they request a qualified **Highway Unit Supervisor** to administer it.

questions.text: Under P.G. 217-06, after a Department vehicle collision, who must the Patrol Supervisor notify if the MOS operator is arrested for DWI?
options: B) The Quality of Life Officer
questions.answerFull: C) The Patrol Supervisor notifies **IAB** for a DWI arrest of a MOS operator, and must comply with P.G. 208-40 "Intoxicated or Impaired Driver Arrest." The Patrol Supervisor notifies Operations (NOT the Desk Officer) when the MOS is injured and requires hospital treatment.
questions.explanation: The Patrol Supervisor notifies **IAB** for a DWI arrest of a MOS operator, and must comply with P.G. 208-40 "Intoxicated or Impaired Driver Arrest." The Patrol Supervisor notifies Operations (NOT the Desk Officer) when the MOS is injured and requires hospital treatment.

questions.text: Per P.G. 217-06, a Department vehicle collision occurs. A police officer fails to report on the MV104L. What are the consequences?
options: A) A written reprimand and mandatory retraining
B) Grounds for a CCRB complaint
C) A misdemeanor and grounds for suspension or revocation of the MOS's license
D) An administrative violation requiring the desk officer to prepare the form
questions.answerFull: C) Failure to report on the MV104L is a **misdemeanor** and grounds for **suspension or revocation** of the MOS's license. Note: MV104L is only for UMOS — a CMOS Department vehicle operator prepares the MV104 (civilian form) instead.
questions.explanation: Failure to report on the MV104L is a **misdemeanor** and grounds for **suspension or revocation** of the MOS's license. Note: MV104L is only for UMOS — a CMOS Department vehicle operator prepares the MV104 (civilian form) instead.

questions.text: According to P.G. 217-06, who convenes the Precinct Vehicle Pursuit and Collision Safety Review Board, and how often must it be convened?
options: A) The Quality of Life Sergeant, quarterly
B) The Precinct Executive Officer (XO), monthly
C) The Commanding Officer, within 30 days of each collision
D) The Training Sergeant, within 20 days of each collision
questions.answerFull: B) The **Precinct Executive Officer (XO)** convenes the Precinct Vehicle Pursuit and Collision Safety Review Board **monthly**. The XO is the Chairperson (C.O. if XO unavailable). The board must convene within **20 days**.
questions.explanation: The **Precinct Executive Officer (XO)** convenes the Precinct Vehicle Pursuit and Collision Safety Review Board **monthly**. The XO is the Chairperson (C.O. if XO unavailable). The board must convene within **20 days**.

questions.text: Under P.G. 217-06, how many members are on the Precinct Vehicle Pursuit and Collision Safety Review Board if the MOS operator is from the same precinct?
questions.answerFull: C) There are **6 people on the board** when the operator is from the same precinct: XO + Training Sgt + PEER + Lt PC or SP. OPS + I.C.O. or ASS'T + QOL Sgt. or DESIG. If the operator is from an **outside command**, a supervisor from that command is added for a total of **7**.
questions.explanation: There are **6 people on the board** when the operator is from the same precinct: XO + Training Sgt + PEER + Lt PC or SP. OPS + I.C.O. or ASS'T + QOL Sgt. or DESIG. If the operator is from an **outside command**, a supervisor from that command is added for a total of **7**.

questions.text: Per P.G. 217-06, the Desk Officer faxes the three Department vehicle collision reports to the Personnel Safety Desk. What is the deadline, and what is notably absent from what is faxed?
options: A) Within 48 hours; the photographs are not included
B) Within 24 hours; the final agency determination captions are NOT completed (left blank)
C) Within 30 days; the MV104L is not included
D) By end of tour; the CR-PDV Part C is excluded
questions.answerFull: B) The three reports (PAR, MV104-L, CR-PDV) are faxed to Personnel Safety Desk **within 24 hours**, WITHOUT the "final agency determination" captions completed — the Precinct C.O. makes that determination later. No cover sheet is necessary.
questions.explanation: The three reports (PAR, MV104-L, CR-PDV) are faxed to Personnel Safety Desk **within 24 hours**, WITHOUT the "final agency determination" captions completed — the Precinct C.O. makes that determination later. No cover sheet is necessary.

questions.text: According to P.G. 217-06, when a Department vehicle operator is involved in a collision and a collision occurs during an "emergency operation," what must appear on the Police Accident Report?
options: A) The letter "E" checked in the emergency box on the front of the PAR
B) The letter "P" in a circle next to the UMOS's name at the top of the PAR
C) The notation "Emergency" stamped across the top of the PAR
D) A separate MV104-Emergency form attached to the PAR
questions.answerFull: B) A **"P" in a circle** is placed next to the UMOS's name at the top of the PAR when a collision occurs during emergency operation. This applies only when **responding TO** an emergency — returning FROM an emergency does NOT qualify.
questions.explanation: A **"P" in a circle** is placed next to the UMOS's name at the top of the PAR when a collision occurs during emergency operation. This applies only when **responding TO** an emergency — returning FROM an emergency does NOT qualify.

questions.text: Per P.G. 217-06, what is the distinction between discipline and retraining after a Department vehicle collision?
options: A) Discipline is for any collision; retraining is only for first-time offenders
B) Discipline is for unsafe disregard of speed/lights; retraining is for driving deficiencies such as loss of control or poor mirror usage
C) Discipline applies only if there is a fatality; retraining applies for all other collisions
D) There is no distinction — both are mandatory after any Dept vehicle collision
questions.answerFull: B) **Discipline** is appropriate when a member unsafely disregarded the speed limit or red light. **Retraining** is appropriate when ability was a contributory factor due to a driving deficiency (weather, loss of control, backing/braking/turning, fender judgment, mirror usage, avoiding potholes). VTL allows police to run lights after slowing down and to speed if life/property is not endangered.
questions.explanation: **Discipline** is appropriate when a member unsafely disregarded the speed limit or red light. **Retraining** is appropriate when ability was a contributory factor due to a driving deficiency (weather, loss of control, backing/braking/turning, fender judgment, mirror usage, avoiding potholes). VTL allows police to run lights after slowing down and to speed if life/property is not endangered.

questions.text: Under P.G. 217-06, the A-QOL Officer must enter CR-PDV information into the intranet within what timeframes?
options: A) Parts A and B within 24 hours; Part C within 14 days
B) Parts A and B within 72 hours; Part C within 30 days
D) Parts A and B within 48 hours; Part C within 60 days
questions.answerFull: B) The A-QOL Officer enters information from Parts A and B of the CR-PDV **within 72 hours** from time of collision, and Part C information **within 30 days** (including agency final determination).
questions.explanation: The A-QOL Officer enters information from Parts A and B of the CR-PDV **within 72 hours** from time of collision, and Part C information **within 30 days** (including agency final determination).

questions.text: Per P.G. 217-06, if an MOS operator's private car insurance is unjustly surcharged as a result of a Department vehicle collision, what should the member's Commanding Officer do?
options: A) File a formal grievance with the PBA
B) Notify the Department's Legal Bureau on the member's behalf
C) Prepare a UF50 (Official Letterhead) to the insurance company concerned
D) Contact NYS Department of Financial Services directly
questions.answerFull: C) The C.O. prepares a **UF50 (Official Letterhead)** to the insurance company concerned — the exact text the C.O. should write is specified in the P.G. The member should notify their C.O. AND contact **NYS Department of Financial Services**.
questions.explanation: The C.O. prepares a **UF50 (Official Letterhead)** to the insurance company concerned — the exact text the C.O. should write is specified in the P.G. The member should notify their C.O. AND contact **NYS Department of Financial Services**.

questions.text: According to P.G. 217-16, under what circumstances must a chemical test warrant be obtained for an unconscious DWI suspect?
options: A) Immediately upon the suspect becoming unconscious
B) When the suspect has been unconscious for more than 30 minutes
C) When more than two hours have elapsed since arrest and the suspect has not been tested
D) Only when the collision resulted in a fatality
questions.answerFull: C) A chemical test warrant **must** be obtained when more than **two (2) hours** have elapsed since arrest and the unconscious prisoner has not been subjected to a chemical test. The warrant cannot be applied for if the operator is the only person with serious physical injury.
questions.explanation: A chemical test warrant **must** be obtained when more than **two (2) hours** have elapsed since arrest and the unconscious prisoner has not been subjected to a chemical test. The warrant cannot be applied for if the operator is the only person with serious physical injury.

questions.text: Under P.G. 217-01, which of the following vehicles involved in a collision is classified as a "Qualifying Vehicle" requiring a Truck and Bus Supplemental PAR (MV104S)?
options: A) A motorcycle with a modified engine
B) A school bus with seating for 12 including the driver
C) A commercial truck with a Gross Weight Rating of 10,000 pounds
D) A rental car carrying hazardous household materials
questions.answerFull: B) A **BUS with seating for 9 or more including driver** is a Qualifying Vehicle. A commercial truck must have GWR/CWR of **MORE THAN** 10,000 pounds (not exactly 10,000). A HazMat vehicle is any vehicle with a HAZ MAT placard. The MV104S is prepared when a Condition of Severity also exists (fatal, hospital/medical, or tow/transported).
questions.explanation: A **BUS with seating for 9 or more including driver** is a Qualifying Vehicle. A commercial truck must have GWR/CWR of **MORE THAN** 10,000 pounds (not exactly 10,000). A HazMat vehicle is any vehicle with a HAZ MAT placard. The MV104S is prepared when a Condition of Severity also exists (fatal, hospital/medical, or tow/transported).

questions.text: Per P.G. 217-06, what is the "crossover collision" definition found in P.G. 202-06, and what additional step is required for PARs involving crossover collisions?
options: A) A collision where two vehicles collide head-on; no additional steps required
B) A collision where a vehicle strikes or crosses a median/jersey barrier separating opposing traffic; A-QOL Officer faxes PAR to Corp Counsel AND Highway District
C) A collision where a driver crosses state lines; notify DMV of both states
D) A collision involving a turning vehicle; note in the PAR accident description
questions.answerFull: B) A crossover collision is when a vehicle **strikes OR crosses over** a median, jersey barrier, etc., separating opposing traffic — it does NOT actually have to cross over anything. The A-QOL Officer/Designee faxes a copy of all PARs involving crossover collisions to **Corp Counsel AND Highway District**. *(P.G. 202-06 definition; P.G. 217-06 procedure)*
questions.explanation: A crossover collision is when a vehicle **strikes OR crosses over** a median, jersey barrier, etc., separating opposing traffic — it does NOT actually have to cross over anything. The A-QOL Officer/Designee faxes a copy of all PARs involving crossover collisions to **Corp Counsel AND Highway District**. *(P.G. 202-06 definition; P.G. 217-06 procedure)*

sergeantFocus.text: It is City policy to **pursue claims in its favor**. Members must detail circumstances supporting City claims and obtain witness information. **Full cooperation** with the involved agency or Law Department is **mandatory**.
The supervisor at the Department facility must ensure the vehicle is not released prematurely — evidence preservation and defect correction are both gates to release.
The patrol supervisor has direct responsibility for supervising vehicle removal, summons issuance, and ensuring the IMPOUND screen is updated for towed vehicles.
The Quality of Life Officer reviews all Police Accident Reports — supervisors should verify this review is happening consistently.
The patrol supervisor initiates the Highway District request — this is the first step in the chemical test warrant process.
The patrol supervisor should weigh these six factors when deciding disposition. Intent and witness availability are the most critical factors favoring detective referral.
The supervising officer must ensure the unusual occurrence report includes the two additional copies for the Chief of Special Operations.
The **patrol supervisor** investigates and prepares the report for Department property damage — this is a direct supervisory responsibility.

chapters.title: Property: General
chapters.readme:
# Section 218 — Property: General

## Overview

Section 218 governs all aspects of invoicing, processing, safeguarding, and disposing of property coming into police custody. This is one of the largest sections in the Patrol Guide, covering 44 procedures that address everything from general invoicing to specialized evidence handling (firearms, DNA, computers, fireworks), stolen vehicle processing, currency handling, vendor/peddler enforcement, and handgun license surrenders.

## Source

NYPD Patrol Guide, Section 218 (Property — General)
Source PDF: `public-pguide4-ocr.pdf`

## Learning Objectives

1. Understand general invoicing procedures and property categories (P.G. 218-01)
2. Know the process for returning property at command and voiding invoices (P.G. 218-02)
3. Understand vehicle safeguarding, inventory search rules, and the 48-hour limit (P.G. 218-12, 218-13)
4. Know stolen vehicle processing for NYC-recovered, out-of-city, and Port Authority scenarios (P.G. 218-14, 218-15, 218-16)
5. Master Rotation Tow Program procedures and the 30-day tow operator timeline (P.G. 218-21)
6. Understand firearms evidence marking, packaging, and chain of custody (P.G. 218-23)
7. Know found property, decedent's property, and currency processing rules (P.G. 218-26, 218-28, 218-35, 218-36)
8. Understand vendor/peddler property seizure and forfeiture procedures (P.G. 218-39, 218-40, 218-41)
9. Know handgun license surrender investigation requirements (P.G. 218-45)
10. Understand vehicle seizure at arrest and the VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM requirement (P.G. 218-48)
11. Know DNA evidence immediate delivery chain: Command → Police Lab → OCME (P.G. 218-49)
12. Understand property retention timelines — 1 year for property, 15 days for vehicles (P.G. 218-55)

## Study Tips

- **Property Categories** — Memorize all categories: Arrest Evidence, DNA Arrest Evidence, Investigatory, DNA Investigatory, Forfeiture, Decedent's Property, Found Property, Safekeeping, Peddler Property, Amnesty, Determine True Owner, Parking Enforcement, Photo Release, Rotation Tow, Other
- **48-hour rule** appears twice: vehicles at command (218-12/218-20) and VIN check delivery to Auto Pound (218-21)
- **Retention deadlines**: 1 year for property, 15 days for vehicles — failure to submit retention request = disposal
- **Currency handling** differs based on type: evidence vs. non-evidence vs. numismatic/sentimental vs. forfeiture vs. red dye
- **Inventory search consent**: If owner doesn't consent to opening locked items, a search warrant is needed (218-13)
- **FORMS Property** is the central system — know the FORMS failure procedures (218-57)
chapters.keyTerms:
# Section 218 — Key Terms, Timeframes, Fees, and Forms

## Property Categories

| Category | Definition |
|----------|-----------|
| **Arrest Evidence** | Property seized for court presentation |
| **DNA Arrest Evidence** | Property seized for court presentation containing potentially probative DNA evidence |
| **Investigatory** | Property seized for investigation with COMPLAINT REPORT and no ARREST WORKSHEET |
| **DNA Investigatory** | Investigatory property containing potentially probative DNA evidence |
| **Forfeiture** | Property used to facilitate a crime or representing proceeds/substituted proceeds |
| **Decedent's Property** | Personal property of a deceased person |
| **Found Property** | Non-contraband property found by a member or civilian |
| **Peddler's Property** | Items removed from vendors (stands, carts) |
| **Amnesty** | Firearms surrendered via Cash for Guns Program |
| **Safekeeping** | Property to be returned to owner |
| **Determine True Owner** | Property where true owner cannot be determined and no immediate arrest warranted |
| **Photo Release** | Recovered stolen vehicles where arrest made and vehicle not used in other crime |
| **Rotation Tow** | Non-evidence stolen or apparently abandoned vehicles/motorcycles recovered in NYC |

## Firearms Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Firearm** | Includes rifles, shotguns, pistols, revolvers, starter's pistols, zip guns, air/CO2 guns, homemade/modified guns, simulated/toy firearms, ghost guns, 3D-printed firearms, stabilizing brace firearms |
| **Cartridge** | Live ammunition — bullet + shell casing as one unit |
| **Fired Bullet** | Part of cartridge fired through barrel |
| **Shell Casing** | Metal casing remaining in/ejecting from firearm after bullet leaves |

## Vehicle Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Apparently Abandoned Vehicle** | Unoccupied vehicle abandoned per VTL 1224 OR observed under circumstances indicating it may be stolen but no alarm transmitted |
| **Double Tow** | Additional tow truck for unusual recovery (embankment, overturned, wedged) — NOT authorized solely because vehicle has no wheels |

## Other Key Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Regulated Waste** | Blood/infectious materials in liquid/semi-liquid state; contaminated sharps; pathological wastes |
| **Contraband** | Property whose mere possession is prohibited by law (not property merely suspected stolen) |
| **Numismatic/Sentimental Value** | All gold coins, all U.S. silver coins dated 1964 or prior, extremely old bills |
| **Negotiable Instrument** | Cash equivalents: money orders, traveler's checks, bank checks |
| **Co-Occupant** | Person residing on a permanent basis in same premises with deceased |
| **General Vendor** | Person selling goods other than food at retail in a public area |
| **Food Vendor** | Person selling food at retail in any public place |
| **Distributor** | Person/organization engaged in sale/distribution of goods for resale by a general vendor |
| **Handgun** | Any pistol or revolver listed on a Handgun License |

## Critical Timeframes

| Timeframe | Rule | Source |
|-----------|------|--------|
| **48 hours** | Maximum time vehicle may be safeguarded at command (non-evidence) | 218-12 |
| **48 hours** | Maximum time for delivery of seized vehicle to Property Clerk Auto Pound | 218-20 |
| **30 days** | Tow operator MUST deliver vehicle to Auto Pound | 218-21 |
| **15 days** | After 15 days at Auto Pound, vehicle disposed of according to law | 218-21 |
| **15 days** | Retention deadline for investigatory motor vehicles/boats | 218-55 |
| **1 year** | Retention deadline for investigatory property (non-vehicle) | 218-55 |
| **6 years** | Filed INVOICES maintained (each July, previous year boxed to Property Clerk warehouse) | 218-02 |
| **10 days** | Desk officer must authorize release or denial of investigatory evidence | 218-02 |
| **5 days** | Claimant may request CO/designee review after denial of investigatory evidence | 218-02 |
| **30 days** | Handgun license surrender investigation must be completed | 218-45 |
| **60 days** | Investigation if found property not returned by interstate company/airline/bank | 218-27 |
| **24 hours** | Report prepared after disposal of perishable vendor food | 218-39 |
| **120 hours** | Sexual Offense Evidence Collection Kit used within this period (or medical discretion) | 218-33 |
| **0100 hours** | Daily deadline for firearms requiring lab analysis to be delivered to patrol borough office | 218-23 |
| **1 minute** | Time for vendor to begin moving in exigent circumstances | 218-39 |
| **2 hours** | Minimum time vendor must stay away from location in exigent circumstances | 218-39 |
| **3 business days** | Property Clerk issues check for federal/state forfeiture upon written request | 218-38 |
| **6 hours** | FORMS outage threshold — under 6 hours: prepare WORKSHEET and wait | 218-57 |

## Fee Schedule

| Fee Type | Amount | Source |
|----------|--------|--------|
| Vendor removal — by Department vehicle | $65.00 | 218-43 |
| Vendor removal — not by Department vehicle | $20.00 | 218-43 |
| Vendor goods seized with vehicle/cart/stand | $10.00 | 218-43 |
| Vendor goods only seized | $20.00 | 218-43 |
| Vendor storage (per day or part) | $5.00 | 218-43 |
| Vehicle/boat towing (Department tow) | $25.00 | 218-44 |
| Vehicle/boat storage (per day or part) | $5.00 | 218-44 |

## Key Forms

| Form | Number | Used For |
|------|--------|----------|
| PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE | PD521-141 | All invoiced property |
| PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE WORKSHEET | PD521-141A | Draft preparation of INVOICE |
| PROPERTY TRANSFER REPORT | PD521-1412 | Transferring property between locations |
| PROPERTY TRANSFER REPORT ACCEPTANCE DOCUMENT | PD521-172 | Receipt upon delivery of transferred property |
| REQUEST FOR LABORATORY EXAMINATION REPORT | PD521-168 | Requesting lab analysis of evidence |
| ON-LINE BOOKING SYSTEM ARREST WORKSHEET | PD244-159 | Arrest processing |
| ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF FOUND PROPERTY | PD542-012 | Receipt for found property delivered on patrol |
| REPORT OF UNCLAIMED PROPERTY | PD542-122 | Reporting found property not claimed |
| WARNING NOTICE — VEHICLE IN POLICE CUSTODY | PD571-1211 | Affixed to windshield of vehicles in custody |
| ROTATION TOW — OWNER NOTIFICATION | PD571-1210 | Notifying owner of rotation tow |
| VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM | PD571-1218 | Served on arrestee when vehicle seized |
| COMMAND VEHICLE SEIZURE INDEX | PD571-091 | Tracking vehicle seizure numbers at command |
| FEE RECEIPT | PD122-017 | Collecting removal/storage fees |
| SEAL FOR DOOR OF D.O.A. PREMISES | PD517-120 | Sealing deceased person's premises |
| IDENTIFICATION TAG | PD317-091 | Receipt for body removed by morgue/undertaker |
| NOTICE TO PERSONS FROM WHOM PROPERTY HAS BEEN REMOVED | PD521-124 | Notice of rights to reclaim property |
| HANDGUN SURRENDER NOTICE | PD641-123 | Directing surrender of handgun license |
| HANDGUN SURRENDER NOTICE RESPONSE | PD641-1234A | Documenting investigation results |
| PEDDLER FORFEITURE QUESTIONNAIRE | PD260-155 | Documenting claimant information for forfeiture |
| CONSENT TO SUSPECT EVIDENCE COLLECTION KIT | PD321-031 | Suspect consent for forensic evidence exam |
| CERTIFICATION OF PROPERTY CLERK DIVISION SEALS | PD521-061 | Tracking peddler enforcement seals |
| PROPERTY CLERK DIVISION SEAL DISTRIBUTION CARD | PD521-1411 | Signing for issued seals |
| REQUEST FOR RETENTION | PD521-169 | Requesting retention of invoiced property |
| REQUEST FOR INVOICE CHANGE OF CATEGORY | PD521-167 | Changing property category |
| OFFICIAL LETTERHEAD | PD158-151 | Various notifications |

## Key Acronyms (from The Key Lessons 21–22)

| Acronym | Full Term | Context |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| **FAS** | Firearms Analysis Section | Section of the Police Lab that analyzes firearms evidence |
| **JFTF** | Joint Firearms Task Force | Contact for emergency firearm trace requests |
| **SECK** | Sexual Offense Evidence Collection Kit | DNA evidence category for lab delivery |
| **PTR** | Property Transfer Report | Report used when transferring property between locations |
| **BATF** | Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives | Contacted for routine or emergency firearm traces |
| **DCWP** | Dept. of Consumer/Worker Protection | Receives LD6 complaints about tow operator violations |
| **DSNY** | Dept. of Sanitation New York | Tags vehicles for potential Ro-Tow (but UMOS makes final determination) |
| **EPCS** | Evidence Property Control Specialist | Obtains "Ro Tow" copy of PCI and updates FORMS-P |

## Ro-Tow Disqualifiers — "CHASED FIT DOM"

The mnemonic **CHASED FIT DOM** identifies what is NOT a Rotation Tow situation:

| Letter | Situation | Notes |
|--------|-----------|-------|
| **C** | Crime | Dept. Tow |
| **H** | Highway | Authorized Tow or Dept. Tow |
| **A** | Arrest made | Except photo release |
| **S** | Safeguard | Aided/Collision |
| **E** | Evidence | Dept. Tow |
| **D** | Determine True Owner | — |
| **F** | Forfeiture | — |
| **I** | Investigation | — |
| **T** | Truck (large) | — |
| **D** | Derelict | Sanitation guidelines on inside back cover of Ro-Tow Log |
| **O** | Owner on scene AND recovered prior to police | — |
| **M** | Moped (limited use motorcycle) | — |

## VTL 1224 — Abandoned Vehicle Thresholds

| Time | Condition |
|------|-----------|
| More than 6 hours | No plates |
| More than 24 hours | Illegally parked |
| More than 48 hours | Legally parked initially → becomes abandoned |
| More than 96 hours | On private property without owner permission |

## Four Transparent Lab Bags (218-04)

| Bag | Contents |
|-----|---------|
| 1 — GUNS | Firearms and firearm-related evidence |
| 2 — DRUGS | Controlled substances / marijuana (PCI only — no Request for Lab Exam, no 61, no OLBS, no zFINEST) |
| 3 — DNA | DNA evidence including SECKs |
| 4 — CRIMINALISTICS | Trace evidence (hair, fiber, paint, GSR, etc.) |

## Additional Critical Timeframes (from Lessons 21–22)

| Timeframe | Rule | Source |
|-----------|------|--------|
| **0030** | Desk Officer must notify Patrol Boro that evidence is going to Lab | 218-04 |
| **0100** | Lab delivery must occur on 1st platoon prior to this time | 218-04 |
| **0100** | If boro not called by 0030, Borough Clerical Officer calls command | 218-04 |
| **2 weeks** | Call BATF Regional Crime Gun Center for routine trace (after receipt by FAS) | 218-23 |
| **Day 8–30** | Ro-Tow operator may deliver vehicle to Property Clerk Auto Pound on weekdays | 218-21 |
| **Day 30** | Ro-Tow operator MUST deliver vehicle to Auto Pound | 218-21 |
| **After 15 days at Auto Pound** | Unclaimed vehicles disposed of according to law | 218-21 |

## Key Contact Units

| Unit | When to Contact |
|------|----------------|
| **Auto Crime Unit** (0800–2300) | Questions about vehicle immobilization |
| **Stolen Property Inquiry Unit** | Cancel/transmit alarms; ONLY unit that cancels firearms alarms |
| **Inter-City Correspondence Unit** | Vehicle stolen outside NYC recovered inside NYC |
| **Computer Crimes Squad** (Mon–Fri 0600–2000) | Computer evidence seizure |
| **Office of Chief of Detectives** | Computer evidence (after hours) |
| **Asset Forfeiture Unit** (24/7) | Currency/instruments $1,000+ for forfeiture |
| **Bomb Squad Range — Rodman's Neck** (Mon–Fri 0700–1500) | Fireworks delivery (Mon–Fri 7x3) |
| **Bomb Squad Office** | Fireworks delivery at all other times or if Range personnel unavailable |
| **License Division** | Handgun license surrender issues |
| **Legal Bureau** | Suspect evidence consent refusal; civil enforcement questions |
| **Internal Affairs Bureau Command Center** | Property discrepancies; currency deposit discrepancies |
| **Property Clerk Division** | Large seizures (2,000+ bills); large vendor property |
| **Harbor Unit** | Boat removal to storage facility |
| **Fleet Services Division, Department Tow** | Vehicles unable to be driven |
| **Information Technology Bureau Service Desk** | FORMS Property outages |
| **NYPD OCME Liaison Unit** | Unfounded sexual offense cases |
| **FIU OCME Liaison Unit** | Immediate DNA delivery decisions |
| **NYS Tax Office** | Lottery property recovery |
| **Audits and Accounts Unit** | Fee receipts forwarded daily |
| **Police Lab Evidence Control Supervisor** (24/7) | Unusual property before Lab delivery (large seizures, hypos, etc.) |
| **Joint Firearms Task Force (JFTF)** | Emergency firearm trace requests |
| **BATF Regional Crime Gun Center** | Routine firearm trace (2 weeks after receipt by FAS) |
| **Dept. of Consumer/Worker Protection (DCWP)** | Tow operator violations/overcharges (LD6); tow company theft (61 dup.) |

questions.text: Under P.G. 218-12, which of the following is NOT an authorized method for immobilizing a vehicle in police custody?
options: A) Removing rotor or coil wire and attaching to INVOICE printout
D) Removing starter relay/fuse or fuel pump relay/fuse
questions.answerFull: B) Members should NOT disconnect the battery due to the potential risk of damaging some vehicles. Contact the Auto Crime Unit (0800–2300) with questions. (P.G. 218-12)
questions.explanation: Members should NOT disconnect the battery due to the potential risk of damaging some vehicles. Contact the Auto Crime Unit (0800–2300) with questions. (P.G. 218-12)

questions.text: A vehicle may be safeguarded at the command for how long when not required as evidence or forfeiture?
questions.answerFull: B) After 48 hours, the vehicle must be removed to a Property Clerk storage facility. (P.G. 218-12)
questions.explanation: After 48 hours, the vehicle must be removed to a Property Clerk storage facility. (P.G. 218-12)

questions.text: During an inventory search of an automobile, a locked trunk cannot be forced open UNLESS one of several exceptions applies. If the owner does not consent and no exception exists, what is required?
options: B) A court order from the DA's office
D) Notification to the Auto Crime Unit
questions.answerFull: C) No consent = no inventory of locked items (absent exigent circumstances). A search warrant is required. (P.G. 218-13)
questions.explanation: No consent = no inventory of locked items (absent exigent circumstances). A search warrant is required. (P.G. 218-13)

questions.text: Under the Rotation Tow program, when must the tow operator deliver a vehicle to the Property Clerk Auto Pound?
questions.answerFull: C) Vehicles remaining at the tow facility MUST BE DELIVERED to the Auto Pound by the tow operator by day 30. (P.G. 218-21)
questions.explanation: Vehicles remaining at the tow facility MUST BE DELIVERED to the Auto Pound by the tow operator by day 30. (P.G. 218-21)

questions.text: When serving the VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM on a defendant, the arresting officer must:
options: A) Have a supervisor present as witness
C) Obtain written consent from the ADA
questions.answerFull: B) The arresting officer must activate the BWC when serving the VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM. (P.G. 218-48)
questions.explanation: The arresting officer must activate the BWC when serving the VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM. (P.G. 218-48)

questions.text: When marking firearm-related evidence, where should initials be scratched on a shell casing recovered from a crime scene?
options: A) The exterior of the casing
B) The bottom/base of the casing
C) The inside of the casing
D) Nowhere — shell casings are not individually marked
questions.answerFull: C) Shell casings are marked on the INSIDE. DO NOT mark the exterior or bottom. (P.G. 218-23)
questions.explanation: Shell casings are marked on the INSIDE. DO NOT mark the exterior or bottom. (P.G. 218-23)

questions.text: Firearms requiring laboratory analysis must be delivered to the patrol borough office by what time daily?
questions.answerFull: C) All invoiced firearms requiring Firearms Analysis Section examination must be delivered by 0100 hours daily. Delay requires exigent circumstances AND permission from the precinct CO/duty captain. (P.G. 218-23)
questions.explanation: All invoiced firearms requiring Firearms Analysis Section examination must be delivered by 0100 hours daily. Delay requires exigent circumstances AND permission from the precinct CO/duty captain. (P.G. 218-23)

questions.text: A PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE being sent to the Police Laboratory will not exceed how many line items?
questions.answerFull: C) INVOICES sent or potentially sent to the Police Laboratory will not exceed twenty line items. (P.G. 218-01)
questions.explanation: INVOICES sent or potentially sent to the Police Laboratory will not exceed twenty line items. (P.G. 218-01)

questions.text: Which unit is the ONLY unit authorized to cancel alarms for firearms?
questions.answerFull: C) Cancellation of firearms alarms will be made ONLY by the Stolen Property Inquiry Unit. The recovering command will NOT cancel. (P.G. 218-23)
questions.explanation: Cancellation of firearms alarms will be made ONLY by the Stolen Property Inquiry Unit. The recovering command will NOT cancel. (P.G. 218-23)

questions.text: When seizing computer evidence, what should a member do if the computer needs to be powered off?
options: B) Hold the power button for 10 seconds
C) Unplug the computer from the wall outlet
questions.answerFull: C) Do NOT use the on/off switch. Unplug from the wall outlet only. Prior to removing the plug, photograph the monitor screen. (P.G. 218-31)
questions.explanation: Do NOT use the on/off switch. Unplug from the wall outlet only. Prior to removing the plug, photograph the monitor screen. (P.G. 218-31)

questions.text: Invoiced property categorized as Investigatory (non-vehicle) will be disposed of by the Property Clerk after what period if no retention request is submitted?
questions.answerFull: B) The Property Clerk will dispose of investigatory property (non-vehicle) after one year from the date of invoice if no retention request is submitted. Vehicles/boats are disposed of after 15 days. (P.G. 218-55)
questions.explanation: The Property Clerk will dispose of investigatory property (non-vehicle) after one year from the date of invoice if no retention request is submitted. Vehicles/boats are disposed of after 15 days. (P.G. 218-55)

questions.text: When processing non-evidence currency, the command messenger must be accompanied by whom when delivering the deposit bag to the bank?
options: C) Another uniformed member of the service or Evidence/Property Control Specialist
questions.answerFull: C) Currency is delivered to the bank's night deposit vault by the command messenger accompanied by another uniformed member or Evidence/Property Control Specialist. (P.G. 218-36)
questions.explanation: Currency is delivered to the bank's night deposit vault by the command messenger accompanied by another uniformed member or Evidence/Property Control Specialist. (P.G. 218-36)

questions.text: Under P.G. 218-38, what is the minimum value of currency/negotiable instruments that requires notification to the Asset Forfeiture Unit for forfeiture processing?
questions.answerFull: B) Seized U.S. currency and/or negotiable instruments valued at $1,000 or more that are deemed forfeitable require notification to the Asset Forfeiture Unit (24/7). (P.G. 218-38)
questions.explanation: Seized U.S. currency and/or negotiable instruments valued at $1,000 or more that are deemed forfeitable require notification to the Asset Forfeiture Unit (24/7). (P.G. 218-38)

questions.text: When processing a deceased person's property, what must the patrol supervisor obtain before directing a body search?
options: A) Permission from the commanding officer
B) Conferral with the Medical Examiner
D) Written consent from next of kin
questions.answerFull: B) The patrol supervisor must confer with the Medical Examiner or assistant Medical Examiner PRIOR to directing a member to search the body for valuables and identification documents. (P.G. 218-28)
questions.explanation: The patrol supervisor must confer with the Medical Examiner or assistant Medical Examiner PRIOR to directing a member to search the body for valuables and identification documents. (P.G. 218-28)

questions.text: What is the maximum number of fireworks bags that should be transported in an RMP?
questions.answerFull: B) As a general rule, no more than four bags of fireworks should be transported in an RMP, placed in the rear seat away from heat/spark sources. (P.G. 218-53)
questions.explanation: As a general rule, no more than four bags of fireworks should be transported in an RMP, placed in the rear seat away from heat/spark sources. (P.G. 218-53)

questions.text: A handgun license surrender investigation must be completed and forms forwarded to the License Division within how many days?
questions.answerFull: C) The investigation must be completed and all related forms forwarded to the License Division, through channels, within thirty days of date the HANDGUN SURRENDER NOTICE was received at command. (P.G. 218-45)
questions.explanation: The investigation must be completed and all related forms forwarded to the License Division, through channels, within thirty days of date the HANDGUN SURRENDER NOTICE was received at command. (P.G. 218-45)

questions.text: Under the vendor enforcement procedures, what is the supervisory span of control for peddler enforcement?
options: A) One supervisor for three officers
B) One supervisor for five officers
C) One supervisor for eight officers
D) One supervisor for ten officers
questions.answerFull: B) Supervisory span of control for peddler enforcement shall not exceed one supervisor for five police officers. (P.G. 218-39)
questions.explanation: Supervisory span of control for peddler enforcement shall not exceed one supervisor for five police officers. (P.G. 218-39)

questions.text: When the desk officer receives a request for the return of investigatory evidence, the release must be authorized or denied within how many days?
questions.answerFull: B) The desk officer must authorize release no more than ten days after receipt of the request, if property is no longer required as evidence. If denied, claimant may request CO/designee review within 5 days. (P.G. 218-02)
questions.explanation: The desk officer must authorize release no more than ten days after receipt of the request, if property is no longer required as evidence. If denied, claimant may request CO/designee review within 5 days. (P.G. 218-02)

questions.text: What is the removal fee when a licensed food vendor's vehicle, cart, or stand is removed by a Department vehicle?
questions.answerFull: D) The removal fee when property is removed by a Department vehicle is $65.00. If NOT removed by Department vehicle, the fee is $20.00. (P.G. 218-43)
questions.explanation: The removal fee when property is removed by a Department vehicle is $65.00. If NOT removed by Department vehicle, the fee is $20.00. (P.G. 218-43)

questions.text: When FORMS Property becomes inoperable for more than six hours and the problem is isolated to the invoicing command only, what should the desk officer instruct?
options: B) Contact the Property Clerk Division for manual processing
C) Remove property to an adjoining command and invoice there
D) Wait for the system to be restored
questions.answerFull: C) When the outage exceeds six hours and is isolated to the command, the invoicing member removes property to an adjoining command and invoices in compliance with current procedures. The invoicing command (where the incident occurred) is selected as the invoicing command in FORMS. (P.G. 218-57)
questions.explanation: When the outage exceeds six hours and is isolated to the command, the invoicing member removes property to an adjoining command and invoices in compliance with current procedures. The invoicing command (where the incident occurred) is selected as the invoicing command in FORMS. (P.G. 218-57)

questions.text: Under P.G. 218-04, a found revolver with no connection to an arrest or criminal investigation would be:
options: A) Delivered to the Police Laboratory for analysis
B) Delivered to the Firearms Analysis Section (FAS) for tracing
C) Delivered direct to the Property Clerk Division (no lab analysis required)
D) Held at the command until a detective supervisor directs otherwise
questions.answerFull: C) Found **revolvers** not associated with an arrest or criminal investigation do NOT require laboratory analysis and go directly to the Property Clerk. By contrast, found **firearms** (semi-automatic handguns, etc.) DO go to the Lab. (P.G. 218-04)
questions.explanation: Found **revolvers** not associated with an arrest or criminal investigation do NOT require laboratory analysis and go directly to the Property Clerk. By contrast, found **firearms** (semi-automatic handguns, etc.) DO go to the Lab. (P.G. 218-04)

questions.text: Under P.G. 218-04, the Desk Officer must notify the Patrol Boro prior to what time that evidence is going to the Laboratory?
questions.answerFull: B) The Desk Officer notifies the Patrol Boro PRIOR to 0030 that there is evidence going to the Lab for delivery. The actual delivery must then occur on the 1st platoon PRIOR to 0100. If the desk officer fails to call the boro by 0030, the Borough Member/Clerical Officer will call command at 0100. (P.G. 218-04)
questions.explanation: The Desk Officer notifies the Patrol Boro PRIOR to 0030 that there is evidence going to the Lab for delivery. The actual delivery must then occur on the 1st platoon PRIOR to 0100. If the desk officer fails to call the boro by 0030, the Borough Member/Clerical Officer will call command at 0100. (P.G. 218-04)

questions.text: Under P.G. 218-04, which of the following forms is NOT attached to the Drugs transparent bag going to the Police Laboratory?
options: D) All of the above are NOT attached to the Drugs bag
questions.answerFull: D) The Drugs bag does NOT get a Request for Laboratory Examination, does NOT get a 61/Omni printout, and does NOT get a zFINEST printout. The ONLY paperwork attached to the Drugs bag is the PCI. (P.G. 218-04)
questions.explanation: The Drugs bag does NOT get a Request for Laboratory Examination, does NOT get a 61/Omni printout, and does NOT get a zFINEST printout. The ONLY paperwork attached to the Drugs bag is the PCI. (P.G. 218-04)

questions.text: A member fires their service weapon during an officer-involved shooting. The recovered shell casings from the scene should be marked:
options: A) On the exterior of the casing
B) On the inside of the casing only
C) On the bottom of the casing
D) Not marked — shell casings from officer-involved shootings are never marked
questions.answerFull: B) Discharged shell casings are marked on the INSIDE only — never on the exterior or bottom. This applies to all shell casings regardless of the circumstances. (P.G. 218-23)
questions.explanation: Discharged shell casings are marked on the INSIDE only — never on the exterior or bottom. This applies to all shell casings regardless of the circumstances. (P.G. 218-23)

questions.text: When a member wishes to have a firearm sent to the Lab but does NOT want it traced, what must they write and where?
options: A) "NO TRACE" in the remarks section of the INVOICE only
B) "DO NOT TRACE" in block letters in the remarks section of BOTH the PCI AND the Request for Lab Exam
C) "HOLD FOR TRACE" in the arrest paperwork
D) Nothing — tracing is automatic and cannot be declined
questions.answerFull: B) The phrase "DO NOT TRACE" must be written in block letters in the remarks section of both documents. If just one document is marked, the firearm may still be traced. (P.G. 218-23)
questions.explanation: The phrase "DO NOT TRACE" must be written in block letters in the remarks section of both documents. If just one document is marked, the firearm may still be traced. (P.G. 218-23)

questions.text: Officers recover an abandoned vehicle with no plates on a highway. It has been unattended for 7 hours. Under VTL 1224, how should this vehicle be classified?
options: A) Not abandoned — must be unattended 24 hours
B) Abandoned — unattended more than 6 hours with no plates
C) Derelict — subject to Sanitation disposal
D) Evidence — subject to Dept. Tow only
questions.answerFull: B) Under VTL 1224, a vehicle left unattended for more than 6 hours with no plates is deemed abandoned. This is the shortest of the four time thresholds. (P.G. 218-21)
questions.explanation: Under VTL 1224, a vehicle left unattended for more than 6 hours with no plates is deemed abandoned. This is the shortest of the four time thresholds. (P.G. 218-21)

questions.text: A tow operator, after being told the type and condition of a vehicle, refuses to accept a Rotation Tow assignment. The Desk Officer should:
options: A) Call the next tow operator without any documentation
B) Prepare a Report of Violation (LD6) and forward to DCWP; then call next operator in sequence
C) Notify the commanding officer and await further instructions
D) Prepare a Complaint Report (61) and forward to DCWP
questions.answerFull: B) When a tow operator refuses AFTER being informed of vehicle type and condition, the Desk Officer prepares a Report of Violation (LD6) and forwards it to the Dept. of Consumer/Worker Protection (DCWP). The next tow operator in sequence is then notified. (P.G. 218-21)
questions.explanation: When a tow operator refuses AFTER being informed of vehicle type and condition, the Desk Officer prepares a Report of Violation (LD6) and forwards it to the Dept. of Consumer/Worker Protection (DCWP). The next tow operator in sequence is then notified. (P.G. 218-21)

questions.text: A complaint is received that a Rotation Tow company stole items from a towed vehicle. The correct form and routing is:
options: A) LD6 (Report of Violation) forwarded to DCWP
B) Complaint Report (61) forwarded to DCWP (duplicate, informational only)
C) LD6 forwarded to Internal Affairs Bureau
D) Complaint Report (61) forwarded directly to the tow company's license bureau
questions.answerFull: B) Theft and other unlawful acts by tow companies are recorded on the Complaint Report (61) and the duplicate is forwarded to DCWP for informational purposes only. Overcharges use LD6. Know the distinction. (P.G. 218-21)
questions.explanation: Theft and other unlawful acts by tow companies are recorded on the Complaint Report (61) and the duplicate is forwarded to DCWP for informational purposes only. Overcharges use LD6. Know the distinction. (P.G. 218-21)

questions.text: A patrol supervisor responds to a scene where a person has died alone in their apartment. The supervisor wants to search the body. What must be done FIRST?
options: A) Request Operations to notify the Public Administrator
B) Confer with the Medical Examiner or assistant Medical Examiner
C) Obtain a search warrant from the DA
D) Seal the door with a SEAL FOR DOOR OF DOA PREMISES
questions.answerFull: B) The patrol supervisor must confer with the ME PRIOR to directing any member to search the body. This is a mandatory first step. (P.G. 218-28)
questions.explanation: The patrol supervisor must confer with the ME PRIOR to directing any member to search the body. This is a mandatory first step. (P.G. 218-28)

questions.text: When securing fireworks for transport in an RMP, members should:
options: A) Place bags in the trunk, sealed, with windows closed
B) Place up to 4 bags in the rear seat with all windows opened minimum 2 inches
C) Place bags in the front passenger seat with air conditioning running
D) Call Bomb Squad for escort before any transport of fireworks
questions.answerFull: B) Fireworks are transported in the REAR SEAT of the RMP (NOT the trunk). All windows must be opened a minimum of 2 inches. Maximum 4 bags per RMP. For a significant amount, call FDNY through 311 for escort. (P.G. 218-53)
questions.explanation: Fireworks are transported in the REAR SEAT of the RMP (NOT the trunk). All windows must be opened a minimum of 2 inches. Maximum 4 bags per RMP. For a significant amount, call FDNY through 311 for escort. (P.G. 218-53)

questions.text: A Sanitation Department worker places a tag on an abandoned-looking vehicle and notifies NYPD. This automatically qualifies the vehicle for Rotation Tow.
options: A) True — the DSNY notification triggers the Ro-Tow program
B) False — the UMOS must make the final determination by visual inspection
C) True — but only if the vehicle has been tagged for more than 24 hours
D) False — only the Desk Officer can authorize Ro-Tow after DSNY notification
questions.answerFull: B) A DSNY sticker or notification does NOT automatically make a vehicle eligible for Ro-Tow. The Desk Officer directs a UMOS to inspect the vehicle and decide if it qualifies based on criteria found on the rear inside cover of the Ro-Tow Log. (P.G. 218-21)
questions.explanation: A DSNY sticker or notification does NOT automatically make a vehicle eligible for Ro-Tow. The Desk Officer directs a UMOS to inspect the vehicle and decide if it qualifies based on criteria found on the rear inside cover of the Ro-Tow Log. (P.G. 218-21)

sergeantFocus.text: Detective supervisors must ensure ECMS entry documenting date/time of delivery, Police Laboratory Number, and OCME Evidence Control Number for all DNA evidence.
Detective supervisors must ensure Biohazard Labels are properly utilized, review each INVOICE and REQUEST, and ensure ECMS entries documenting delivery are prepared.
Any discrepancy in property against INVOICE requires immediate IAB notification — this is a critical integrity safeguard.
The desk officer must verify identity, ensure proper documentation, and make Command Log entries for every temporary property removal.
CO should assign a **supervisor** to conduct the investigation.
COs must ensure adequate supply of pre-printed WORKSHEETS, INVOICES, and associated forms are stored in the property room for use during FORMS outages.
The Traffic Safety Sergeant has direct responsibility for coordinating timely vehicle delivery to Property Clerk Auto Pounds.
Commanding officers must ensure compliance is part of the command's **monthly self-inspection program** monitored by Quality Assurance Section.
CO must designate sufficient supervisors to frequently inspect perishable foods so items can be condemned and disposed of when necessary.

chapters.title: Department Property
chapters.readme:
# Section 219 — Department Property

## Source
- **Patrol Guide Section:** 219
- **Source PDF:** public-pguide4.pdf (OCR version)
- **Priority:** Low

## Learning Objectives
- Know the vehicle inspection requirements (daily and weekly) and required equipment
- Understand fuel dispensing card types and delivery supervision procedures
- Know the reporting chain and notifications for lost/stolen Department property, firearms, shields, and ID cards
- Identify rules for IT systems security, portable radios, LPR devices, and mobile digital devices
- Understand the eligibility requirements and restrictions for Restricted Parking Permits
- Know the rules governing OMNY Police Passes, E-Z Pass tags, and radiation detection pagers
- Recognize emergency power outage procedures and fuel tank responsibilities

## Chapter Files

| File | Procedures | Topics |
|------|-----------|--------|
|  | 219-01 through 219-10 | Vehicle inspection, maintenance, car wash, tires, tire chains, Department tow, unmarked vehicles |
|  | 219-11, 219-12, 219-13, 219-28 | Fuel dispensing (card types), fuel/heating oil delivery, emergency power outages |
|  | 219-14, 219-15, 219-23, 219-24, 219-31, 219-32 | Computer security, portable radios, callboxes, cell phones, LPR devices, mobile digital devices |
|  | 219-17, 219-18, 219-19, 219-25, 219-26, 219-27, 219-29, 219-30 | Patrol kits (PPE), supply inspection, lockers, E-Z Pass, OMNY passes, radiation pagers, parking permits |
|  | 219-20, 219-21 | Loss/theft of Department property, lost/stolen firearm/shield/ID card |
|  | — | Definitions, timeframes, forms, contact units, fuel card colors |
|  | — | 15 multiple-choice practice questions |

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| P.G. 219-01 | Department Vehicles — Inspection at Start of Tour | completed |
| P.G. 219-02 | Weekly Vehicle Inspections | completed |
| P.G. 219-03 | Vehicle Maintenance | completed |
| P.G. 219-04 | Car Wash | completed |
| P.G. 219-05 | Tire Repair | completed |
| P.G. 219-06 | Tire Replacement | completed |
| P.G. 219-07 | Tire Station Removal | completed |
| P.G. 219-08 | Tire Chains | completed |
| P.G. 219-09 | Department Tow | completed |
| P.G. 219-10 | Unmarked Vehicle Utilization Record | completed |
| P.G. 219-11 | Computerized Fuel Dispensing | completed |
| P.G. 219-12 | Fuel Delivery Supervision | completed |
| P.G. 219-13 | Heating Oil Delivery | completed |
| P.G. 219-14 | Computer Systems — Security and Integrity | completed |
| P.G. 219-15 | Portable Radios | completed |
| P.G. 219-17 | Patrol Kits (PPE) | completed |
| P.G. 219-18 | Inspection of Supplies | completed |
| P.G. 219-19 | Department Locker Inspections | completed |
| P.G. 219-20 | Loss or Theft of Department Property | completed |
| P.G. 219-21 | Lost/Stolen Firearm, Shield, or ID Card | completed |
| P.G. 219-23 | Solar Cellular Callboxes | completed |
| P.G. 219-24 | Cellular Telephones — Patrol Supervisor | completed |
| P.G. 219-25 | E-Z Pass Tags | completed |
| P.G. 219-26 | NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Passes | completed |
| P.G. 219-27 | Radiation Detection Pagers — Command-Assigned | completed |
| P.G. 219-28 | Emergency Power Outages | completed |
| P.G. 219-29 | NYPD Restricted Parking Permits | completed |
| P.G. 219-30 | Radiation Detection Pagers — Personally Assigned | completed |
| P.G. 219-31 | License Plate Reader Devices | completed |
| P.G. 219-32 | Department Mobile Digital Devices | completed |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- Vehicle inspection accountability (daily tour checks + weekly Sunday inspections)
- Notification chain for lost/stolen property: Desk Officer → CO/Ops Coordinator → IAB + Stolen Property Inquiry Unit
- Disciplinary consultation with Department Advocate's Office for lost/stolen firearms
- ICO duties: monthly DAS audits (5 members), E-Z Pass monitoring, OMNY pass inspections, parking permit background checks
- LPR alarm response: ALWAYS verify through NYSPIN before law enforcement action
- Emergency power: disconnect non-essential equipment from red-plate receptacles; quarterly room surveys
- Restricted Parking Permit: CO has final authority; 5+ red light or 15+ school speed violations = ineligible
chapters.keyTerms:
# Section 219 — Key Terms, Timeframes, and Forms

## Key Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Category I Vehicle** | Unmarked vehicle not required to be stored at Department facility; permitted to be removed from the City regularly; designated by the Police Commissioner |
| **Category II Vehicle** | Unmarked vehicle with specific designations (exempt from OPP garage parking ineligibility) |
| **General Purpose Vehicle** | Standard unmarked vehicle for routine operational use |
| **Special Purpose Vehicle** | Unmarked vehicle assigned for specific operational needs |
| **License Plate Reader (LPR)** | Specialized camera/computer that captures license plate numbers, converts to text, compares against "hot list"; NOT connected to NYSPIN; activates on partial matches |
| **Mobile Digital Device** | Any smartphone, tablet, or portable computer issued by the Department |
| **Vehicle Identification Box (VIB)** | In-vehicle transponder for automated fuel dispensing |
| **NYPD Restricted Parking Permit (Misc. 23-N)** | Cardboard parking credential (~4"×10") with serial number, command code, license plate number, and expiration date |
| **NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Pass** | Transit pass for subway/bus access (not express bus); valid ~7 years; assigned to individual member by unique serial number |
| **Radiation Detection Pager** | Device assigned to commands or individual members for detecting radiological threats; requires specific training |
| **Ullage** | Remaining capacity in a fuel tank; tanks filled to 90% ullage |
| **Regulated Infectious Waste** | Biohazard materials picked up by Medical Division messenger |
| **OLBS** | Online Booking System — one of the systems ICO issues passwords for through Omniform |
| **BADS** | Booking and Arraignment Desk System — ICO issues passwords through Omniform |
| **LAPS** | LAPS system — ICO issues passwords through Omniform |
| **VIB** | Vehicle Identification Box — in-vehicle transponder for automated fuel dispensing |
| **CRALO** | Command Radio Auto Liaison Officer — holder of yellow fuel card |
| **ERS** | Employee Resources Section — where member reports for replacement shield/ID after loss |
| **SPIU / Stolen Property Inquiry Unit** | Unit notified to transmit alarm for lost/stolen Department property with identifying marks |
| **ITB** | Information Technology Bureau — provides Mobility Tablet tech support 24/7; handles LPR repairs; telecommunications unit handles lost/stolen mobile devices |
| **COG** | "Change of Gear" — the Digital Activity Log entry documenting vehicle inspection results (condition, odometer, gas gauge) |
| **DAS** | Domain Awareness System — ICO audits 5 random members monthly for compliance |
| **Focused Audit** | An audit of computer activity during a specific time period, requested by ICO or C.O. via 49 to Deputy Commissioner of IT |

## Fuel Card Colors

| Card | Color |
|------|-------|
| Master | **Green** |
| CRALO | **Yellow** |
| Non-Fleet | **Orange** |
| Private | **Red** |
| Operator | **White** |

## Critical Timeframes

| Timeframe | Rule | Source |
|-----------|------|--------|
| **Each tour** | Inspect Department vehicle; account for radiation detection pagers; account for LPR devices | 219-01, 219-27, 219-31 |
| **Each Sunday** | Weekly vehicle inspection after second platoon posted | 219-02 |
| **10th of month** | Car wash expense reports due to Audits and Accounts | 219-04 |
| **Nov 1 – Mar 15** | Tire chains available | 219-08 |
| **>4 inches snow** | Threshold for installing tire chains | 219-08 |
| **24 hours** | LPR hot list must be updated within this period to use device | 219-31 |
| **5 business days** | Request ITB permission before taking mobile device to foreign country | 219-32 |
| **30 days** | Notify CO of vehicle change for Restricted Parking Permit; max time to use substitute vehicle | 219-29 |
| **7 years** | General validity period for NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Pass | 219-26 |
| **Monthly** | ICO audits DAS usage for 5 random members | 219-14 |
| **Quarterly** | CO surveys generator/mechanical/electrical rooms | 219-28 |
| **50%** | Minimum fuel tank level for commands with single tank (heating + generator) | 219-28 |
| **90%** | Maximum fill level (ullage) for fuel deliveries | 219-12 |
| **12 months** | Period for counting red light (5+) or school speed (15+) camera violations for permit ineligibility | 219-29 |

## Restricted Parking Permit Ineligibility Triggers

| Trigger |
|---------|
| Assigned Category I vehicle |
| OPP garage access (except Cat II) |
| Military & Extended Leave / vacation-terminal leave pending retirement |
| Under suspension |
| Auxiliary Police |
| 5+ red light camera violations in 12 months |
| 15+ school speed camera violations in 12 months |
| Vehicle in judgment for outstanding fines |
| Invalid license, registration, or insurance |

## Parking Always Prohibited (Even With Permit)

| Violation |
|-----------|
| Double parking |
| Fire hydrant/zone |
| Bus stop/lane |
| Sidewalk |
| Crosswalks/driveways |
| Obstructing traffic |
| No standing zones |
| Snow emergency |
| Taxi stand |
| Bicycle lane |
| No Permit Zone |

## E-Z Pass — Authorized vs. Unauthorized Toll Crossings

| Authorized | Unauthorized |
|-----------|-------------|
| Bronx-Whitestone Bridge | NJ Turnpike |
| George Washington Bridge | NY State Thruway |
| Bayonne Bridge | Garden State Parkway |
| Henry Hudson Bridge | New England Thruway |
| Holland/Lincoln Tunnels | Tappan Zee Bridge |
| Queens Midtown Tunnel | _(list not all-inclusive)_ |
| Throgs Neck Bridge | |
| Verrazano-Narrows Bridge | |
| Hugh L. Carey Tunnel | |
| RFK Bridge | |

## Key Forms

| Form | Number | Used For |
|------|--------|----------|
| VEHICLE INSPECTION REPORT | PD571-153 | Weekly vehicle inspection |
| CAR WASH AUTHORIZATION | PD171-051 | Car wash services |
| RADIO ASSIGNMENT LOG | — | Tracking portable radio assignments |
| LOST/STOLEN FIREARM/SHIELD/I.D. CARD REPORT | PD520-150 | Parts A–D for reporting loss/theft |
| SUPERVISOR'S COMPLAINT REPORT/COMMAND DISCIPLINE ELECTION REPORT | PD468-123 | Disciplinary findings |
| COMPLAINT REPORT | PD313-152 | Lost/stolen/damaged property |
| FORCE RECORD | PD406-143 | Member records (shield number, serial numbers) |
| PROPERTY TRANSFER RECEIPT | PD521-145 | Personally assigned radiation pagers |
| RESTRICTED PARKING PERMIT APPLICATION | PD671-041 | Parking permit request |
| NYPD PHONE STICKER | PD589-090 | Affixed to Department phone case |
| DEPARTMENT MOBILE DIGITAL DEVICE REPAIR/REPLACEMENT/RETURN REQUEST | PD589-163 | Phone repair/replacement |
| PROPERTY RECEIPT — DISCONTINUANCE OF SERVICE | PD520-013 | Separation from service property return |

## Lost/Stolen Firearm — County Notification Rules

| Location of Loss | Who to Notify First |
|-----------------|-------------------|
| Within NYC | Desk Officer, Pct of occurrence |
| Nassau or Suffolk County | Desk Officer **105 Pct** |
| Westchester, Putnam, Rockland, or Orange | Desk Officer **50 Pct** |
| Outside residence counties | **Operations Unit DIRECT** |

## Lost/Stolen Firearm/Shield/ID — Report Parts

| Part | Prepared By | Distribution |
|------|------------|-------------|
| Part A | MOS reporting loss | — |
| Part B | D/O Pct of occurrence | — |
| Part C | C.O. / Duty Captain | Per **BOTTOM** of form |
| Part D | C.O. of MOS concerned | Per **REAR** of form |

## Key Contact Units

| Unit | When to Contact |
|------|----------------|
| **Fleet Services Division** | Vehicle maintenance, E-Z Pass reassignment, defective tags; loss of tire/tool from dept auto (3 copies of 49) |
| **Building Maintenance Section** (24/7) | Emergency power outages — immediately |
| **ITB Service Desk** (24/7) | LPR repairs, Mobility Tablet support |
| **ITB Telecommunications Unit** | Lost/stolen/damaged mobile devices; callbox issues (Mon–Fri 0800–1600) |
| **ITB Information Security Desk** | Foreign travel with Department phone (5 business days prior; list every country) |
| **ITB Wheel** | Callbox issues after hours |
| **Operations Unit** | Lost/stolen portable radio; also notified immediately for lost/stolen firearm/shield/ID card |
| **Electronics Section** | Lost/stolen portable radio |
| **Internal Affairs Bureau** | Lost/stolen Department property; lost/stolen firearm/shield/ID |
| **Stolen Property Inquiry Unit (SPIU)** | Lost/stolen Department property alarms; property with identifying marks |
| **Transit Bureau Wheel** | Lost/stolen OMNY pass; also for lost/stolen MetroCard (NYCT Police Pass) |
| **Employee Resources Section (ERS)** | Lost/stolen MetroCard (NYCT Police Pass) — forward 49 and Complaint Report; replacement shield/ID card |
| **Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit** | OMNY pass issues; damaged/defective/lost/stolen passes |
| **Patrol Borough Counterterrorism Coordinator** | Radiation pager repair/replacement instructions |
| **Citywide Counterterrorism Office** | Lost/stolen radiation pager — notify immediately |
| **Chief of Department's Vehicle Identification Unit** | Restricted Parking Permit applications/returns |
| **Department Advocate's Office** | Consultation required before disciplinary action for lost/stolen FIREARM |
| **Employee Resources Section** | Replacement shield/ID card after loss; also for LIRR/Metro North pass issues |
| **Medical Division** | Regulated infectious waste pickup |
| **Quartermaster Section** | AAA batteries for radiation pagers |
| **DCAS Inspector** | Must sign heating oil delivery ticket |
| **HQ Security Unit** | Loss occurs in 5th Precinct confines — reports loss here (5th records the 61) |
| **PA Integrity Unit** | Loss occurs in 13th Precinct confines — reports loss here (13th records the 61) |
| **Legal Bureau** | ICO contacts for CCRB smartphone requests; also handles Rosario/Discovery requests |
| **Real Time Crime Center** | Can search past records of license plates queried by LPR |
| **Telecommunications Unit** | Callbox notification Mon–Fri 0800–1600 |

questions.text: Under P.G. 219-08, tire chains should NOT be installed on Department vehicles until there is more than how many inches of snow?
questions.answerFull: C) Tire chains are NOT installed until there is more than 4 inches of snow. Additionally, tire chains are NOT installed on front-wheel drive vehicles. (P.G. 219-08)
questions.explanation: Tire chains are NOT installed until there is more than 4 inches of snow. Additionally, tire chains are NOT installed on front-wheel drive vehicles. (P.G. 219-08)

questions.text: Which color fuel card is designated for the CRALO?
questions.answerFull: C) Card colors: Green = Master, Yellow = CRALO, Orange = Non-Fleet, Red = Private, White = Operator. (P.G. 219-11)
questions.explanation: Card colors: Green = Master, Yellow = CRALO, Orange = Non-Fleet, Red = Private, White = Operator. (P.G. 219-11)

questions.text: How often must the Integrity Control Officer audit the Domain Awareness System (DAS) and how many members must be reviewed?
questions.answerFull: B) The ICO conducts a monthly DAS audit reviewing 5 random members. (P.G. 219-14)
questions.explanation: The ICO conducts a monthly DAS audit reviewing 5 random members. (P.G. 219-14)

questions.text: If a portable radio is lost and the member was NOT carrying it in an authorized carrying case, the finding will be:
questions.answerFull: C) If a portable radio is lost and was not in an authorized carrying case at the time, the member is deemed negligent. (P.G. 219-15)
questions.explanation: If a portable radio is lost and was not in an authorized carrying case at the time, the member is deemed negligent. (P.G. 219-15)

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a required notification when a portable radio is lost or stolen?
