Section 303 of the Administrative Guide defines the duties and responsibilities of borough-level command staff. These five procedures establish the chain of command from Borough Commander down through the executive officers, adjutant, and operations commander. For the sergeant exam, the key takeaways are the reporting relationships, the distinction between operational and administrative oversight, and the specific timeframes for inspections/briefings.

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Study File | Status |
|-----------|-------|-----------|--------|
| A.G. 303-01 | Borough Commander | borough-command | completed |
| A.G. 303-02 | Borough Executive Officer, Operations | borough-command | completed |
| A.G. 303-03 | Borough Executive Officer, Administration | borough-command | completed |
| A.G. 303-04 | Borough Adjutant | borough-command | completed |
| A.G. 303-05 | Borough Operations Commander | borough-command | completed |
| A.G. 303-06 | Borough Administrator | extended | completed |
| A.G. 303-07 | Borough Special Projects Coordinator | extended | completed |
| A.G. 303-08 | Borough Counterterrorism Coordinator | extended | completed |
| A.G. 303-09 | Patrol Borough Traffic Safety Coordinator | extended | completed |
| A.G. 303-10 | Borough Auxiliary Police Coordinator | extended | completed |

## Priority Topics for Sergeant Exam

1. **Borough Commander (303-01)** — Semi-annual briefings, annual CPP inspections, quarterly meetings
2. **Operations vs. Administration split** — Operations exec handles crime/QOL/traffic; Administration exec handles discipline/personnel/scheduling/QA
3. **Borough Adjutant (303-04)** — Early Intervention Committee, Chronic Absent monitoring, CCRB conferral reviews, self-inspection program
4. **Reporting relationships** — Who reports to whom in the borough hierarchy
5. **Borough Operations Commander (303-05)** — 9 crime categories, youth coordination officer selection

## Study Tips

- Know the **two executive officer tracks**: Operations (303-02) handles tactical/enforcement matters; Administration (303-03) handles personnel/discipline/scheduling
- The Borough Adjutant (303-04) has the most sergeant-relevant duties — Early Intervention, Chronic Absent, CCRB reviews
- Memorize the Borough Commander's timeframes: **semi-annual** (IAB/CAB briefings), **annual** (CPP inspections), **quarterly** (executive meetings)
- The Operations Commander oversees **9 specific crime categories** — the exam may test whether you can identify which ones
chapters.keyTerms:
# Section 303 — Key Terms & Critical Numbers

## Borough Command Hierarchy

| Position | Reports To | Oversight Type |
|----------|-----------|----------------|
| Borough Commander | Chief of Patrol | All operations |
| Borough Exec Officer, Operations | Borough Commander | Tactical/enforcement |
| Borough Exec Officer, Administration | Borough Commander | Personnel/discipline/QA |
| Borough Adjutant | Exec Officer, Administration | Risk management/discipline |
| Borough Operations Commander | Exec Officer, Operations | Crime reduction/specialty units |

## Critical Timeframes

| Timeframe | Action | Procedure |
|-----------|--------|-----------|
| Semi-annually | Confer with IAB, CAB, QAS, OEEO, Personnel Orders | 303-01 |
| Annually | Inspect subordinate command's Confidential Performance Profile | 303-01 |
| Quarterly (or as needed) | Meeting with executive staff and line organizations | 303-01 |

## Key Responsibilities by Position

### Borough Adjutant (303-04) — Most Sergeant-Relevant
- Early Intervention Committee participation
- Performance monitoring programs
- Data integrity audits
- Disciplinary system equity
- Self-inspection and auditing program
- Chronic Absent sick monitoring
- Firearms discharge review
- Staff Performance Evaluations
- CCRB conferral report review

### Borough Operations Commander (303-05) — 9 Crime Categories
1. Sex crimes
2. Robberies
3. Assaults
4. Burglaries
5. Larcenies/thefts (including Grand Larceny)
6. Vehicle theft and related crimes
7. Domestic violence
8. Firearms violence/weapons possession/use
9. Other crimes and conditions as they emerge

## Memory Aids

> **Memory Aid — B.C. SCHEDULE:** **S**emi-annual = **S**taff briefings (IAB, CAB, QAS, OEEO, Personnel Orders). **A**nnual = **A**nnual CPP inspection. **Q**uarterly = **Q**uality meetings with executive staff and line orgs. The S-A-Q pattern matches the first letters of each timeframe.

> **Memory Aid — OPS vs. ADMIN:** **OPS** = **O**utward-facing, **P**atrol/crime/ **S**treet work. **ADMIN** = **A**gency-internal, **D**ocuments/**I**nternal/**M**onitoring. Think: Ops = field/visible, Admin = desk/records.

> **Memory Aid — 9 CRIME CATEGORIES:** **S.R.A.B.L.V.D.F.O.** — **S**ex crimes, **R**obberies, **A**ssaults, **B**urglaries, **L**arcenies, **V**ehicle theft, **D**omestic violence, **F**irearms/weapons, **O**ther emerging crimes.

## Key Distinctions

| Operations Track (303-02) | Administration Track (303-03) |
|---------------------------|-------------------------------|
| Crime control strategies | Community affairs |
| QOL improvement | Communications (written) |
| Traffic management/safety | Discipline |
| Graffiti enforcement | Facilities/logistics/supply |
| Counterterrorism liaison | Personnel issues (budget, evals, training, sick) |
| | Property in Dept custody |
| | Scheduling |
| | Quality assurance (inspections, audits, data) |

questions.text: How often must a Borough Commander confer with the Internal Affairs Bureau, Community Affairs Bureau, Quality Assurance Section, Office of Equal Employment Opportunity, and Personnel Orders Division?
questions.explanation: **Semi-annually** (after the initial briefing upon assignment to command). (A.G. 303-01, duty #5a)

questions.text: How often must a Borough Commander inspect each subordinate command's "Confidential Performance Profile"?
questions.explanation: **Annually.** (A.G. 303-01, duty #6)

questions.text: The Borough Executive Officer, Operations supervises which of the following?
options: A) Borough Administrator and Borough Adjutant
B) Borough Operations Commander, Borough Special Projects Coordinator, and Borough specialty units
C) Borough Adjutant and Borough Operations Commander
D) Borough Administrator and Borough Operations Commander
questions.answerFull: B) The Borough Executive Officer, Operations supervises the Borough Operations Commander, Borough Special Projects Coordinator, and Borough specialty units. (A.G. 303-02, duty #1)
questions.explanation: The Borough Executive Officer, Operations supervises the Borough Operations Commander, Borough Special Projects Coordinator, and Borough specialty units. (A.G. 303-02, duty #1)

questions.text: Which borough executive officer is responsible for oversight of discipline and the evaluation process?
questions.answerFull: C) The Borough Executive Officer, Administration provides operational and administrative oversight for discipline and personnel issues including the evaluation process. (A.G. 303-03, duty #2c, #2e(2))
questions.explanation: The Borough Executive Officer, Administration provides operational and administrative oversight for discipline and personnel issues including the evaluation process. (A.G. 303-03, duty #2c, #2e(2))

questions.text: The Borough Adjutant reports to which position?
questions.answerFull: C) The Borough Adjutant reports to the Borough Executive Officer, Administration. (A.G. 303-04, duty #1)
questions.explanation: The Borough Adjutant reports to the Borough Executive Officer, Administration. (A.G. 303-04, duty #1)

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a responsibility of the Borough Adjutant?
options: A) Participate in Early Intervention Committee
B) Monitor sick records with attention to Chronic Absent members
D) Review CCRB conferral reports for accuracy and completeness
questions.answerFull: C) Supervising special event preparation is a duty of the Borough Operations Commander (303-05), not the Borough Adjutant. (A.G. 303-04 and 303-05)
questions.explanation: Supervising special event preparation is a duty of the Borough Operations Commander (303-05), not the Borough Adjutant. (A.G. 303-04 and 303-05)

questions.text: The Borough Operations Commander develops crime reduction strategies for how many specific crime categories?
questions.answerFull: C) Nine categories: sex crimes, robberies, assaults, burglaries, larcenies/thefts, vehicle theft, domestic violence, firearms violence, and other crimes/conditions as they emerge. (A.G. 303-05, duty #3)
questions.explanation: Nine categories: sex crimes, robberies, assaults, burglaries, larcenies/thefts, vehicle theft, domestic violence, firearms violence, and other crimes/conditions as they emerge. (A.G. 303-05, duty #3)

questions.text: The Borough Executive Officer, Operations must liaison with which coordinator regarding emergency preparedness and mobilizations?
questions.answerFull: B) The Borough Executive Officer, Operations must liaison with the Borough Counterterrorism Coordinator to ensure operational readiness of emergency preparedness and mobilizations. (A.G. 303-02, duty #4)
questions.explanation: The Borough Executive Officer, Operations must liaison with the Borough Counterterrorism Coordinator to ensure operational readiness of emergency preparedness and mobilizations. (A.G. 303-02, duty #4)

questions.text: How often must the Borough Commander convene meetings with members of the borough's executive staff and the Department's line organizations?
questions.answerFull: C) Quarterly (or as needed) to discuss command level management issues and resolve challenges faced by members on patrol. (A.G. 303-01, duty #9)
questions.explanation: Quarterly (or as needed) to discuss command level management issues and resolve challenges faced by members on patrol. (A.G. 303-01, duty #9)

questions.text: The Borough Operations Commander oversees the selection of command youth coordination officers after conferral with whom?
questions.answerFull: C) The Borough Operations Commander oversees selection of command youth coordination officers after conferral with precinct/PSA commanding officers. (A.G. 303-05, duty #5)
questions.explanation: The Borough Operations Commander oversees selection of command youth coordination officers after conferral with precinct/PSA commanding officers. (A.G. 303-05, duty #5)

chapters.title: General Regulations
chapters.readme:
# Section 304 — General Regulations

## Source
- **Administrative Guide Section:** 304
- **Source PDF:** A.G. 304-04 Fitness For Duty.pdf
- **Priority:** High

## Learning Objectives
- Understand fitness for duty requirements and alcohol-related misconduct consequences
- Know the mandatory termination triggers for firearm misuse while intoxicated
- Recognize the "Unfit For Duty While Armed" charge and Dismissal Probation requirements
- Know the three counseling resources: HELPLINE, POPPA, Counseling Services Unit
- Understand salute protocols, courtesies, and rank-based etiquette rules
- Know the Department Manual composition and driver's license requirements
- Identify prohibited conduct categories, political restrictions, and relationship restrictions
- Know personal appearance standards, locker sticker rules, and 8-Point Cap requirements
- Understand facial hair policy exceptions and religious head covering accommodations
- Know the three types of prohibited statements: false, misleading, and inaccurate
- Understand gift acceptance rules, pre-approval requirements, and reporting thresholds
- Know uniform wear rules for parades, funerals, and law enforcement events

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| A.G. 304-02 | Courtesies | completed |
| A.G. 304-03 | Compliance With Orders | completed |
| A.G. 304-04 | Fitness For Duty | completed |
| A.G. 304-06 | Prohibited Conduct | completed |
| A.G. 304-07 | Personal Appearance | completed |
| A.G. 304-08 | Facial Hair Policy | completed |
| A.G. 304-09 | Religious Head Covering | completed |
| A.G. 304-10 | Making False Statements | completed |
| A.G. 304-13 | Attendance at Parades, Funerals, Other | completed |
| A.G. 304-14 | Acceptance of Gifts & Other Compensation | completed |
| A.G. 325-35 | Department Computer Use Policy | completed |

## Chapter Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
|  | A.G. 304-04: Fitness for duty, alcohol misconduct, termination triggers, counseling programs, breathalyzer rules, PG conflict notes |
|  | A.G. 304-02: Salute rules, flag/anthem protocols, desk salute, rank-based office etiquette |
|  | A.G. 304-03: Department Manual, punctuality, obeying orders, driver's license requirements, IAB notification |
|  | A.G. 304-06: General prohibitions, relationships, political restrictions, Community Board rules, off-duty restrictions, operational rules |
|  | A.G. 304-07/08/09: Appearance standards, 8-Point Cap, locker stickers, hair/jewelry/tattoo rules, facial hair policy, religious head covering |
|  | A.G. 325-35: Department computer use policy, monitoring notice, prohibited uses |
|  | A.G. 304-10: False/misleading/inaccurate statements, material facts, impeding investigations, separation penalties |
|  | A.G. 304-13: Uniform wear rules, alcohol/firearms restrictions, event monitoring, out-of-state permissions |
|  | A.G. 304-14: Gift acceptance policy, pre-approval requirements, Commissioner vs. CO determinations, IAB reporting |
|  | Definitions, disciplinary consequences, counseling protections, courtesies, compliance, prohibited conduct, appearance terms, statements, gifts, events |
|  | 16 multiple-choice questions covering all AG 304 sections |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- **Mandatory termination:** Firearm misuse while unfit due to alcohol; serious physical injury via motor vehicle while intoxicated
- **Counseling referral:** Know HELPLINE, POPPA, and Counseling Services Unit pathways
- **Dismissal Probation:** Required in negotiated penalties for alcohol-related motor vehicle incidents
- **Counseling protections:** Participation does NOT jeopardize promotions; does NOT exempt from discipline for specific misconduct
- **Chemical testing refusal:** Charged under A.G. 304-06 "Prohibited Conduct"
- **Breathalyzer threshold:** .04 or greater = unfit for duty (A.G. 318-20)
- **Qualifier trap:** "Be fit for duty at all times EXCEPT while out sick" — without "EXCEPT" it is wrong
- **PG Conflict:** A.G. 304-06 vs. A.G. 304-13 on off-duty alcohol in uniform
- **Courtesies:** Salute the Desk (not Desk Officer); salute P.C. and ANY Deputy Commissioner even in civilian clothes
- **Community Board:** CAN serve on board, CANNOT serve on Public Safety Committee
- **10-day rule:** Decline elected office within 10 days or vacate Department position
- **Driver's license:** Notify C.O. directly (breaks chain of command); semi-annual DMV inquiry
- **Locker stickers:** 2 outside (Police Don't Move, Proper Tactics Save Lives), 3 inside (Gun Safety, Seat Belt, Health and Wellness)
- **Facial hair exemption:** Medical = 180 days only; religious and medical both require written approval
- **RAPNET:** Reasonable Accommodation Photo Net — supervisors can query tax numbers
chapters.keyTerms:
# Section 304 — Key Terms and Definitions

## Fitness For Duty

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Fitness For Duty** | Being fit for duty at all times, except when on sick report; not consuming intoxicants to the extent of becoming unfit |
| **Unfit For Duty While Armed** | Disciplinary charge for misconduct involving member found unfit due to alcohol while armed with a firearm |
| **Dismissal Probation** | Period included in negotiated penalties for alcohol-related motor vehicle incidents |

## Counseling Programs

| Program | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **HELPLINE** | Confidential assistance for alcohol-related problems |
| **POPPA** | Police Organization Providing Peer Assistance — joint labor-management, union-operated employee assistance |
| **Counseling Services Unit** | 24/7 assessment and referral; maintains Title 42 Federal confidentiality; not part of disciplinary system |

## Disciplinary Consequences

| Violation | Consequence |
|-----------|-------------|
| Firearm misuse while unfit (alcohol) | **Termination** (exceptional cases: Police Commissioner) |
| Serious physical injury via motor vehicle + alcohol | **Termination** absent exigent circumstances |
| Refusal of chemical testing (VTL §1192) | Charged under A.G. 304-06 "Prohibited Conduct" |
| Found unfit or refuses breath testing during probation | Additional discipline, may include termination |

## Counseling Protections

| Protection | Detail |
|-----------|--------|
| Confidentiality | Title 42 Federal guidelines |
| Promotions | Participation does NOT jeopardize promotional opportunities |
| Assignments | Current assignments NOT ordinarily changed |
| Discipline | Participation does NOT exempt from discipline for specific misconduct |
| Availability | Services available to members, families, and retirees |

## Courtesies (A.G. 304-02)

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Salute** | Rendered as per U.S. Army regulations; required for P.C. and ANY Deputy Commissioner (even in civilian clothes) |
| **The Desk** | What members salute when entering a command (not the Desk Officer) |
| **Attention** | Required when Deputy Inspector or above enters the room, unless otherwise directed |

## Compliance With Orders (A.G. 304-03)

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Department Manual** | Consists of 4 books: Patrol Guide, Administrative Guide, Organizational Guide, Detective Guide |
| **Semi-Annual DMV Inquiry** | C.O. responsibility to verify driver's license status and conduct warrant check for all UMOS and CMOS |
| **RAPNET** | Reasonable Accommodation Photo Net — accessible through LAN and MDTs; contains facial hair and head covering accommodations |

## Prohibited Conduct (A.G. 304-06)

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Conduct Prejudicial** | Conduct prejudicial to the good order, efficiency, or discipline of the Department — the overarching prohibited conduct standard |
| **Schedule "B" CD** | Command Discipline imposed under A.G. 318-01 for certain violations of A.G. 304-06 (e.g., bringing alcohol into Dept. facility) |
| **10-Day Rule** | Member nominated/elected to office has 10 days to decline or automatically vacates Department position |
| **Public Safety Committee** | Committee of Community Board — members CANNOT serve on this committee if it deals with PD and law enforcement matters |
| **Step 7 Relationships** | Prohibited relationships with CIs, witnesses, victims, youth in Dept. programs, and youth at assigned schools |
| **Outside Wire** | Reporting for duty/EOT via outside wire on a regular basis is prohibited; within precinct of residence requires C.O. approval |

## Personal Appearance (A.G. 304-07)

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **8-Point Cap** | Must be worn on foot post, traffic posts, details; optional May 1-Sept 30 for P.O.s/Detectives on subway patrol |
| **Locker Stickers (Outside)** | "Police Don't Move" and "Proper Tactics Save Lives" — 2 stickers on OUTSIDE |
| **Locker Stickers (Inside)** | Gun Safety, Seat Belt, Health and Wellness — 3 stickers on INSIDE |
| **Combination Lock** | Locker must be secured with combination lock WITHOUT serial number |

## Facial Hair and Religious Head Covering (A.G. 304-08, 304-09)

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Reasonable Accommodation Request** | Form used for both facial hair (religious) and religious head covering requests; submitted to EEOD |
| **Shaving Restriction Form** | Form completed by District Surgeon for medical facial hair exemption; valid for 180 days only |
| **EEOD** | Equal Employment Opportunity Division — reviews and approves religious accommodation requests on case-by-case basis |
| **APR Mask** | Air Purifying Respirator — required in specialized units (SOD, CT, etc.); facial hair incompatible due to OSHA regulations |

## Alcohol-Related Terms

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Breathalyzer .04** | Result of .04 or greater = unfit for duty (A.G. 318-20, note after step 1) |
| **Off-Duty Breathalyzer OT** | Department pays overtime to member when administering breathalyzer off duty |
| **VTL Section 1192** | Vehicle and Traffic Law section for DWI; refusal to submit to chemical testing = charged under A.G. 304-06 |

questions.text: A uniformed member of the service who misuses a firearm while unfit for duty due to excessive alcohol consumption will be:
questions.answerFull: C) Any misconduct involving a member's misuse of a firearm while unfit for duty due to excessive consumption of and intoxication from alcohol will result in termination. Exceptional cases are determined by the Police Commissioner on a case-by-case basis. (A.G. 304-04)
questions.explanation: Any misconduct involving a member's misuse of a firearm while unfit for duty due to excessive consumption of and intoxication from alcohol will result in termination. Exceptional cases are determined by the Police Commissioner on a case-by-case basis. (A.G. 304-04)

questions.text: A UMOS causes serious physical injury to another person while operating a motor vehicle and is determined to be unfit for duty due to alcohol. What is the consequence?
questions.answerFull: C) The Department policy states that any UMOS who causes serious physical injury while operating a motor vehicle and is determined unfit for duty due to alcohol consumption will be terminated, absent exigent circumstances. (A.G. 304-04)
questions.explanation: The Department policy states that any UMOS who causes serious physical injury while operating a motor vehicle and is determined unfit for duty due to alcohol consumption will be terminated, absent exigent circumstances. (A.G. 304-04)

questions.text: A UMOS refuses to submit to chemical testing in connection with an alleged violation of VTL §1192 (DWI). The member will be charged with violating:
questions.answerFull: B) A UMOS who refuses chemical testing for alleged DWI will be charged with violating A.G. 304-06, "Prohibited Conduct" — engaging in conduct prejudicial to the good order, efficiency, or discipline of the Department. (A.G. 304-04)
questions.explanation: A UMOS who refuses chemical testing for alleged DWI will be charged with violating A.G. 304-06, "Prohibited Conduct" — engaging in conduct prejudicial to the good order, efficiency, or discipline of the Department. (A.G. 304-04)

questions.text: The Counseling Services Unit maintains confidentiality standards under:
options: A) New York State Civil Service Law
B) Title 42 of Federal guidelines governing alcoholism programs
questions.answerFull: B) The Counseling Services Unit maintains strict standards of confidentiality as set forth in Title 42 of Federal guidelines governing alcoholism programs. (A.G. 304-04)
questions.explanation: The Counseling Services Unit maintains strict standards of confidentiality as set forth in Title 42 of Federal guidelines governing alcoholism programs. (A.G. 304-04)

questions.text: A member participating in counseling programs for alcohol-related problems should be aware that:
options: A) Their promotional opportunities will be jeopardized
B) They will automatically be transferred
C) Participation will NOT jeopardize promotional opportunities
D) They will be placed on restricted duty
questions.answerFull: C) Members participating in these programs will not jeopardize their promotional opportunities, and current assignments will not ordinarily be changed. However, participation does not exempt a member from disciplinary action for specific acts of misconduct. (A.G. 304-04)
questions.explanation: Members participating in these programs will not jeopardize their promotional opportunities, and current assignments will not ordinarily be changed. However, participation does not exempt a member from disciplinary action for specific acts of misconduct. (A.G. 304-04)

questions.text: Any negotiated penalty for a UMOS determined to have been operating a motor vehicle while unfit due to alcohol shall include:
options: B) A period of Dismissal Probation
questions.answerFull: B) Any negotiated penalty involving a UMOS operating a motor vehicle while unfit due to alcohol shall include a period of Dismissal Probation. The member must also agree to submit to ordered breath testing during the probation period. (A.G. 304-04)
questions.explanation: Any negotiated penalty involving a UMOS operating a motor vehicle while unfit due to alcohol shall include a period of Dismissal Probation. The member must also agree to submit to ordered breath testing during the probation period. (A.G. 304-04)

questions.text: POPPA is best described as:
options: B) A joint labor-management, union-operated employee assistance program
D) An Internal Affairs Bureau division
questions.answerFull: B) POPPA (Police Organization Providing Peer Assistance) is a joint labor-management cooperative effort involving the implementation of a union-operated, alternative employee assistance program. Both HELPLINE and POPPA offer confidential services. (A.G. 304-04)
questions.explanation: POPPA (Police Organization Providing Peer Assistance) is a joint labor-management cooperative effort involving the implementation of a union-operated, alternative employee assistance program. Both HELPLINE and POPPA offer confidential services. (A.G. 304-04)

questions.text: The Counseling Services Unit operates:
options: B) 24 hours a day, seven days a week
questions.answerFull: B) The Counseling Services Unit operates 24 hours a day, seven days a week, and will help assess the extent of the problem and make appropriate referrals for treatment. (A.G. 304-04)
questions.explanation: The Counseling Services Unit operates 24 hours a day, seven days a week, and will help assess the extent of the problem and make appropriate referrals for treatment. (A.G. 304-04)

questions.text: Which of the following correctly states the fitness-for-duty requirement?
options: A) Be fit for duty at all times
B) Be fit for duty at all times, except when on sick report
C) Be fit for duty at all times, except when off duty
D) Be fit for duty while on patrol only
questions.answerFull: B) The qualifier "except when on sick report" is critical. Answer choice A is a trap — it omits the qualifier and is therefore incorrect. Always look for the complete statement with the "EXCEPT" qualifier. (A.G. 304-04)
questions.explanation: The qualifier "except when on sick report" is critical. Answer choice A is a trap — it omits the qualifier and is therefore incorrect. Always look for the complete statement with the "EXCEPT" qualifier. (A.G. 304-04)

questions.text: A member of the service who serves on a Community Board may:
options: A) Serve on the Public Safety Committee
B) Vote on PD activities in the district the board serves
C) Serve on the board but NOT on the Public Safety Committee
D) Not serve on a Community Board at all
questions.answerFull: C) Members CAN serve on a Community Board, but CANNOT serve on the Community Board's Public Safety Committee if it deals with PD and other law enforcement matters. They also cannot vote on Community Board matters regarding PD activities in the district the board serves. (A.G. 304-06)
questions.explanation: Members CAN serve on a Community Board, but CANNOT serve on the Community Board's Public Safety Committee if it deals with PD and other law enforcement matters. They also cannot vote on Community Board matters regarding PD activities in the district the board serves. (A.G. 304-06)

questions.text: When entering a command, a uniformed member should salute:
questions.answerFull: C) When entering the command, members salute The DESK — not the Desk Officer. The salute is rendered to the desk itself as a symbol of command authority. (A.G. 304-02)
questions.explanation: When entering the command, members salute The DESK — not the Desk Officer. The salute is rendered to the desk itself as a symbol of command authority. (A.G. 304-02)

questions.text: A breathalyzer result of what level or greater constitutes "unfit for duty"?
questions.answerFull: B) A breathalyzer result of .04 or greater means the member is unfit for duty. This is lower than the legal DWI limit of .08 — the Department holds members to a stricter standard. (A.G. 318-20, note after step 1)
questions.explanation: A breathalyzer result of .04 or greater means the member is unfit for duty. This is lower than the legal DWI limit of .08 — the Department holds members to a stricter standard. (A.G. 318-20, note after step 1)

questions.text: A member's driver's license is suspended. Who must the member notify?
questions.answerFull: C) The member must notify the Commanding Officer directly if their license is suspended, revoked, or not renewed. This breaks the normal chain of command — the notification goes straight to the C.O. (A.G. 304-03)
questions.explanation: The member must notify the Commanding Officer directly if their license is suspended, revoked, or not renewed. This breaks the normal chain of command — the notification goes straight to the C.O. (A.G. 304-03)

questions.text: Locker stickers on the OUTSIDE of a member's locker include:
options: A) Gun Safety and Seat Belt
B) Police Don't Move and Health and Wellness
C) Police Don't Move and Proper Tactics Save Lives
D) Proper Tactics Save Lives and Gun Safety
questions.answerFull: C) Two stickers go on the OUTSIDE: "Police Don't Move" (with rank, name, shield, squad) and "Proper Tactics Save Lives." Three go on the INSIDE: Gun Safety, Seat Belt, and Health and Wellness. (A.G. 304-07)
questions.explanation: Two stickers go on the OUTSIDE: "Police Don't Move" (with rank, name, shield, squad) and "Proper Tactics Save Lives." Three go on the INSIDE: Gun Safety, Seat Belt, and Health and Wellness. (A.G. 304-07)

questions.text: A member is nominated to an elected office. How many days does the member have to decline before automatically vacating their Department position?
questions.answerFull: B) If a member accepts or is nominated to elected office, the MOS has 10 days to turn it down or automatically vacate their position with the Department. Exceptions include Community Board, Board of Education outside the city, or unpaid leave with the Mayor's permission. (A.G. 304-06)
questions.explanation: If a member accepts or is nominated to elected office, the MOS has 10 days to turn it down or automatically vacate their position with the Department. Exceptions include Community Board, Board of Education outside the city, or unpaid leave with the Mayor's permission. (A.G. 304-06)

questions.text: The medical exemption for facial hair (Shaving Restriction Form) is valid for:
questions.answerFull: C) The medical exemption for facial hair is ONLY good for 180 days. Reevaluation is required to extend it. Members should schedule an appointment to renew the Shaving Restriction Form 1 month prior to expiration. (A.G. 304-08)
questions.explanation: The medical exemption for facial hair is ONLY good for 180 days. Reevaluation is required to extend it. Members should schedule an appointment to renew the Shaving Restriction Form 1 month prior to expiration. (A.G. 304-08)

sergeantFocus.text: Know the **three types of prohibited statements**: (1) False — intentional untruth, (2) Misleading — intended to misdirect/omit material facts, (3) Inaccurate — grossly negligent incorrect information. The **discipline differs** — false statements result in separation absent extraordinary circumstances.
**Extraordinary circumstances** for avoiding separation on false statement charges can **only** be determined by the **Police Commissioner** on a case-by-case basis. This is heavily tested.
Members must **review documents before signing**. Claiming "I didn't read it" is not a defense to an inaccurate statement charge.

**Supervisory responsibilities are never abrogated** — ranking officers must take affirmative action when observing corruption or misconduct, including **immediate IAB notification**. This applies whether on or off duty, inside or outside NYC.
Know the **uniform permission hierarchy**: (1) Outside NYC = Police Commissioner/First Deputy/Chief of Department, (2) Social events = Commanding Officer or designee.

**Out-of-state firearms** require permission from Police Commissioner/First Deputy/Chief of Department **even if** the state allows out-of-jurisdiction officers to carry. State law does not override Department policy.
Members must notify commanding officer **PRIOR to acceptance** — not after. Pre-approval is required.
Know the **two-track process**: (1) Reward for police service = Police Commissioner decides, (2) Gift from city business dealer = Commanding Officer decides after reviewing City Charter § 2604(B)(5).
**Two or more gifts** in a calendar year = report to **Deputy Commissioner, Internal Affairs** regardless of value or source.

Commanding Officers must conduct semi-annual DMV inquiries and warrant checks for all UMOS and CMOS. When IAB flags a license issue, the member is immediately prohibited from driving Department vehicles until the matter is resolved.
Ensure members are informed of this policy. Personal use and dissemination of derogatory/discriminatory material are strictly prohibited and subject to disciplinary action.

As a supervisor, return salutes promptly. Ensure subordinates understand salute protocols, especially the distinction between saluting the desk vs. the Desk Officer, and the different protocols for Captain vs. Deputy Inspector rank thresholds.

Supervisors should be familiar with HELPLINE, POPPA, and Counseling Services Unit referral pathways. Early detection and referral is the Department's objective. Participation in counseling does NOT jeopardize promotions. Counseling is confidential and NOT part of the disciplinary system.

Know the distinction between the head covering and facial hair accommodation processes. Both use the same form and apply to the same groups, but the ID update mechanisms differ — head coverings go through the Shield/ID Unit for a new ID card, while facial hair goes into RAPNET.

Supervisors must be vigilant about prohibited conduct, particularly unauthorized relationships with subordinates, CIs, witnesses, victims, and youth. The political restrictions and Community Board rules are frequently tested areas.

chapters.title: Uniforms and Equipment
chapters.readme:
# Section 305 — Uniforms and Equipment

## Source
- **Administrative Guide Section:** 305
- **Source PDFs:** A.G. 305-05, A.G. 305-07
- **Priority:** Low

## Learning Objectives
- Know reimbursement procedures for lost or damaged uniforms and personal clothing
- Understand firearms carrying requirements — when armed/unarmed, holster requirements
- Identify prohibited holster types and carrying methods
- Know firearm modification, storage, and court entry rules

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| A.G. 305-05 | Lost or Damaged Uniform | completed |
| A.G. 305-07 | Firearms General Regulations | completed |

## Chapter Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
|  | A.G. 305-05: Reimbursement procedures, filing deadlines, reimbursement limits |
|  | A.G. 305-07: Carrying requirements, holster rules, prohibited modifications, court rules, foreign travel |
|  | Authorized firearm definitions, holster requirements, reimbursement terms |
|  | 10 multiple-choice questions covering uniforms and firearms regulations |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- **Holster compliance:** Verify retention device, trigger guard coverage; no clip-on/belly band
- **Modification rules:** Only Firearms and Tactics Section armorers; qualification required after modification
- **Reimbursement:** 30-day filing deadline; direct forwarding for < $100, through channels for ≥ $100
- **Unarmed exceptions:** Know the 5 off-duty circumstances allowing member to be unarmed
- **Court entry:** Members unarmed at Family Court/Supreme Court Matrimonial unless arresting officer
chapters.keyTerms:
# Section 305 — Key Terms and Definitions

## Firearms Terminology

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Authorized Firearm** | Service pistols and off-duty pistols/revolvers conforming to Firearms and Tactics Section specifications |
| **Service Pistol** | Primary on-duty firearm |
| **Off-Duty Pistol/Revolver** | Authorized secondary firearm; may be carried in addition to service pistol on duty (not visible) |
| **Special Weapons** | Require CO permission before carrying on duty (see A.G. 305-11) |
| **FORCE RECORD (PD406-143)** | Record of all revolvers/pistols owned, permitted modifications |
| **Tritium Night Sights** | Green night sights required on service pistols (optional for pre-9/12/94 members and off-duty pistols) |
| **Firearms Safety Station** | Used when loading/unloading firearms |

## Holster Requirements

| Requirement | Detail |
|------------|--------|
| Retention | Reliable retention device other than friction alone |
| Coverage | Must cover trigger guard, muzzle, and front sight |
| Inside-waistband | Must have reinforced mouth for one-handed re-holstering |
| Prohibited types | "One size fits all," "clip-on," "belly band" |

## Prohibited Modifications

All aftermarket/non-Department parts; polishing that alters firearm; machining; attaching anything (slip-on grips, grip plugs, rail attachments, belt clips); engraving, decals, painting, etching.

## Uniform Reimbursement

| Term | Detail |
|------|--------|
| **Eligible items** | Uniforms/equipment damaged/lost in performance of duty; personal clothing/eyeglasses during extraordinary police action |
| **Filing deadline** | 30 days from incident |
| **Settlement deadline** | 180 days from approval |
| **Direct forwarding** | Cost < $100 |
| **Through channels** | Cost ≥ $100 |
| **Personal articles NOT reimbursed** | Jewelry, rings, bracelets (watches excepted at $75 max) |

## Key Timeframes

| Item | Timeframe |
|------|-----------|
| Report for lost/damaged uniform | Within **30 days** |
| Settlement execution | Within **180 days** of approval |
| Vest return if it does not fit | Within **2 days** |

## Equipment Terminology

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **BWC** | Body Worn Camera — carried when issued, requires Department training |
| **CEW** | Conducted Electrical Weapon (Taser) — carried on support side if assigned and trained |
| **OC Spray** | Oleoresin Capsicum pepper spray device — carried on support side, opposite firearm |
| **RTKBCs** | Right To Know Business Cards — carried on person in appropriate amounts |
| **Color of the Day** | Head/arm band carried by civilian clothes enforcement members (not undercovers) |
| **Support Side** | Non-shooting side of the duty belt (items 1-5) |
| **Strong Side** | Shooting/gun side of the duty belt (items 6-10) |
| **Double Magazine Pouch** | Flap and snap closure, black, affixed vertically; holds 2 extra magazines (15 rounds each) |
| **ES Spec #** | Equipment Section Specification Number — label inside authorized nylon duty belts |

## Uniform Terminology

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **The 32 Club** | Four items authorized when temp drops below 32 degrees: Balaclava, Winter Knit Hat (both require Duty Jacket), Winter Hat, and Earmuffs |
| **Point Blank Enterprises Inc.** | Sole authorized manufacturer of uniform shirt style outer vest covers |
| **Outer Vest Cover** | Uniform shirt style cover for body armor; must match uniform shirt color (blue or white); worn as outermost garment |
| **Nylon Windbreaker** | Specialized unit jacket; 1" reflective tape divides front and back; "POLICE" on front, "NYPD" and "POLICE" on rear |

## Breast Bar and Insignia Terminology

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Service Stripes (Hash Marks)** | Each mark = 5 years; left sleeve only; Lt, Sgt, Det, P.O. only; may wear 6 months early |
| **Collar Brass** | Yellow metal insignia on both sides of collar (except raincoat); 1" from forward edge on shirt, 1-1/4" on Summer Blouse |
| **Longevity Bar** | Worn over right breast pocket by UMOS below Captain in short sleeves (no 6-month early rule like hash marks) |
| **WTC Memorial Breast Bar** | Worn above all bars except US Flag; does NOT require being on the job during 9/11 |
| **Firearms Proficiency Bar** | Bottom-most bar; requires 100% at range; removed if not repeated; no promotional points |
| **EPD** | Excellent Police Duty — breast bar with numeral for multiples; max 2 bars |
| **Gold Leaf** | Signifies a 2nd Medal of Honor, Combat Cross, or Medal for Valor on the same bar |
| **NCO Breast Bar** | Worn above shield, immediately below Unit Citation or individual recognition awards (per P.G. 202-11) |

## Mourning Band Terminology

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Mourning Band** | Black, 1/2" wide; covers seal of the city (NOT shield # or rank) |
| **CMOS Mourning Pin** | Worn on left lapel by civilian members (who have no shield) per A.G. 305-14 |
| **Police Memorial Day** | Mourning band mandatory if attending services at 1PP in uniform; optional for all others |
| **National P.O.'s Memorial Day** | Every May 15th — mourning band optional for all |

## Protective Vest Terminology

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **NIJ** | National Institute of Justice — certifies protective vest threat levels |
| **Threat Level III+** | Minimum required vest level (must EXCEED Level II) |
| **Zylon** | Vest material that is specifically NOT authorized |
| **PA Vest Unit** | Police Academy Vest Unit — inspects vests, provides guidance on serviceability |
| **Typed Letterhead (49)** | Report format used when presenting unserviceable vest to PA Vest Unit |

## Mnemonics

| Mnemonic | Meaning |
|----------|---------|
| **A FAVOR** | When to be unarmed off duty: **A**lcohol, **F**amily/Supreme Court Matrimonial, **A**dvisable not to carry, **V**acation, **O**ff-Duty Employment, **R**isk of Loss/Theft |
| **By The Numbers (305)** | 1/4" (shield-to-bar distance, fingernail length), 1/2" (mourning band, cop's braid, facial hair), 1" (windbreaker tape, collar brass on shirt), 1-1/4" (boss's braid, collar brass on Summer Blouse), 3" (min sock height Transit), 5" (max sock height Transit) |

questions.text: A UMOS may be unarmed at their own discretion while off duty in all of the following situations EXCEPT:
options: B) Engaged in authorized off-duty employment
C) Walking through a NYC neighborhood
D) There is a likelihood of consuming alcohol
questions.answerFull: C) Members must be armed at all times when in NYC, unless specific exceptions apply: risk of loss/theft (sporting activities, beach), vacation, authorized off-duty employment, activity where it would be inadvisable to carry, or likelihood of consuming alcohol. Walking through NYC does not qualify. (A.G. 305-07)
questions.explanation: Members must be armed at all times when in NYC, unless specific exceptions apply: risk of loss/theft (sporting activities, beach), vacation, authorized off-duty employment, activity where it would be inadvisable to carry, or likelihood of consuming alcohol. Walking through NYC does not qualify. (A.G. 305-07)

questions.text: Which type of holster is prohibited under A.G. 305-07?
questions.answerFull: C) "Belly band" holsters, "clip-on" holsters, and "one size fits all" holsters are prohibited. Shoulder and ankle holsters are permitted if they meet retention requirements. (A.G. 305-07)
questions.explanation: "Belly band" holsters, "clip-on" holsters, and "one size fits all" holsters are prohibited. Shoulder and ankle holsters are permitted if they meet retention requirements. (A.G. 305-07)

questions.text: Firearms may NOT be stored or left in:
options: A) A locked firearms safe at home
questions.answerFull: B) Members shall not store or leave firearms in an unattended motor vehicle, except as authorized (e.g., P.G. 221-10). (A.G. 305-07)
questions.explanation: Members shall not store or leave firearms in an unattended motor vehicle, except as authorized (e.g., P.G. 221-10). (A.G. 305-07)

questions.text: All firearm repairs and modifications must be performed by:
options: C) Firearms and Tactics Section armorers solely
D) The member with CO approval
questions.answerFull: C) All repairs and modifications to authorized firearms must be performed solely by Firearms and Tactics Section armorers. The member must pass a qualification course before carrying a modified firearm. (A.G. 305-07)
questions.explanation: All repairs and modifications to authorized firearms must be performed solely by Firearms and Tactics Section armorers. The member must pass a qualification course before carrying a modified firearm. (A.G. 305-07)

questions.text: A report for lost or damaged uniform must be filed within:
questions.answerFull: B) The report must be prepared on Typed Letterhead addressed to the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget, within 30 days from the date of incident. (A.G. 305-05)
questions.explanation: The report must be prepared on Typed Letterhead addressed to the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget, within 30 days from the date of incident. (A.G. 305-05)

questions.text: Reimbursement for lost or damaged uniform items costing less than $100 should be forwarded to the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget:
options: D) Through the Chief of Department
questions.answerFull: B) Items costing less than $100 are forwarded direct to the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget. Items costing $100 or more go through channels. (A.G. 305-05)
questions.explanation: Items costing less than $100 are forwarded direct to the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget. Items costing $100 or more go through channels. (A.G. 305-05)

questions.text: The maximum reimbursement amount for a lost or damaged watch is:
questions.answerFull: B) A maximum of $75.00 has been established as a reasonable amount for watch reimbursement. Members are reminded they must be equipped with a serviceable watch while performing patrol duty. (A.G. 305-05)
questions.explanation: A maximum of $75.00 has been established as a reasonable amount for watch reimbursement. Members are reminded they must be equipped with a serviceable watch while performing patrol duty. (A.G. 305-05)

questions.text: A UMOS entering Family Court must be:
options: A) Armed with service pistol only
B) Unarmed, unless present as arresting officer or on official business
C) Armed with both service and off-duty weapons
D) Armed but with weapon concealed
questions.answerFull: B) Members must be unarmed when entering Family Court or Supreme Court Matrimonial Parts, unless involved as arresting officer or on official business in a case. If armed as arresting officer, report to court officer supervisor and sign the Law Enforcement Officer log. (A.G. 305-07)
questions.explanation: Members must be unarmed when entering Family Court or Supreme Court Matrimonial Parts, unless involved as arresting officer or on official business in a case. If armed as arresting officer, report to court officer supervisor and sign the Law Enforcement Officer log. (A.G. 305-07)

questions.text: All settlements for lost or damaged uniform reimbursement are non-transferable and must be executed within:
questions.answerFull: C) All settlements are non-transferable and MUST be executed within 180 days of approval by the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget. (A.G. 305-05)
questions.explanation: All settlements are non-transferable and MUST be executed within 180 days of approval by the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget. (A.G. 305-05)

questions.text: A UMOS carrying an authorized firearm into a foreign country (not on official business or sporting event) will face:
options: B) No consequences if firearm is properly secured
C) Disciplinary action and possibly criminal penalties and/or weapon confiscation
questions.answerFull: C) Members are prohibited from carrying firearms into a foreign country unless on official business or personally participating in an event requiring use of a firearm. Failure to comply will result in disciplinary action and possibly subject the member to criminal penalties and/or weapon confiscation. (A.G. 305-07)
questions.explanation: Members are prohibited from carrying firearms into a foreign country unless on official business or personally participating in an event requiring use of a firearm. Failure to comply will result in disciplinary action and possibly subject the member to criminal penalties and/or weapon confiscation. (A.G. 305-07)

questions.text: A Sergeant's nylon windbreaker displays rank insignia in which location?
options: C) Chevrons on the left sleeve only
D) Gold letters across the back
questions.answerFull: B) On the nylon windbreaker, Sergeants have insignia on the collar only — NO chevrons on the sleeves. This is different from the V-neck sweater, where Sergeants DO wear chevrons on the sleeves. (A.G. 305-04)
questions.explanation: On the nylon windbreaker, Sergeants have insignia on the collar only — NO chevrons on the sleeves. This is different from the V-neck sweater, where Sergeants DO wear chevrons on the sleeves. (A.G. 305-04)

questions.text: The WTC Memorial breast bar may be worn by:
options: A) Only UMOS who were on the job on 9/11/2001
B) Only UMOS assigned to WTC recovery operations
C) All UMOS, regardless of whether they were on the job on 9/11
D) Only UMOS who received a commendation for 9/11 service
questions.answerFull: C) The WTC Memorial breast bar may be worn by all UMOS above all other bars except the US Flag bar. Unlike anniversary bars, members did NOT have to be on the job during 9/11 to wear it. (A.G. 305-13)
questions.explanation: The WTC Memorial breast bar may be worn by all UMOS above all other bars except the US Flag bar. Unlike anniversary bars, members did NOT have to be on the job during 9/11 to wear it. (A.G. 305-13)

questions.text: A protective vest must meet what minimum threat level certification?
options: C) Exceeding Threat Level II (Level III or higher)
questions.answerFull: C) The vest must be certified by the NIJ as EXCEEDING Threat Level II — meaning Level II itself is NOT acceptable. The vest must be Level III or higher. Vests made of Zylon are also NOT authorized. (A.G. 305-15)
questions.explanation: The vest must be certified by the NIJ as EXCEEDING Threat Level II — meaning Level II itself is NOT acceptable. The vest must be Level III or higher. Vests made of Zylon are also NOT authorized. (A.G. 305-15)

questions.text: When a member dies in the line of duty, members of his/her command wear the mourning band until:
options: A) The day of the funeral
B) 2400 hrs on the 10th day after death
D) 2400 hrs on the day of the funeral
questions.answerFull: B) For LOD death, members of the deceased's command wear the mourning band from time of death until 2400 hrs on the 10th day after death. Other members wear it from time of death until 2400 hours on the day of the funeral. Note that the flag at half-mast period uses "10 days after funeral" as its reference — a different timeline. (A.G. 305-14 / A.G. 304-02)
questions.explanation: For LOD death, members of the deceased's command wear the mourning band from time of death until 2400 hrs on the 10th day after death. Other members wear it from time of death until 2400 hours on the day of the funeral. Note that the flag at half-mast period uses "10 days after funeral" as its reference — a different timeline. (A.G. 305-14 / A.G. 304-02)

questions.text: On the duty belt, OC pepper spray is carried on which side?
options: A) Strong side, next to firearm
B) Support side, opposite the firearm
C) Center line of the belt
D) Either side at the member's discretion
questions.answerFull: B) OC pepper spray device and holder are carried on the support side (non-shooting side), opposite the firearm. It is item #4 on the support side of the belt. (A.G. 305-08)
questions.explanation: OC pepper spray device and holder are carried on the support side (non-shooting side), opposite the firearm. It is item #4 on the support side of the belt. (A.G. 305-08)

questions.text: Who must a supervisor contact for a reserve protective vest supply?
questions.answerFull: C) Regardless of the member's bureau (Housing, Transit, Detective Bureau, etc.), the supervisor directs the requesting MOS to call the local Patrol Borough for reserve vest supply. If the Patrol Borough does not have any, contact the PA Vest Unit. (A.G. 305-15)
questions.explanation: Regardless of the member's bureau (Housing, Transit, Detective Bureau, etc.), the supervisor directs the requesting MOS to call the local Patrol Borough for reserve vest supply. If the Patrol Borough does not have any, contact the PA Vest Unit. (A.G. 305-15)

questions.text: The mnemonic "A FAVOR" helps remember when a member may be unarmed off duty. What does the "F" stand for?
options: B) Family Court or Supreme Court Matrimonial
questions.answerFull: B) A FAVOR: A = Alcohol likely consumed; F = Family or Supreme Court Matrimonial (don't enter armed except as arresting officer or on official business); A = Advisable not to carry; V = Vacation; O = Off-Duty Employment; R = Risk of Loss or Theft (sports, beach, etc.). (A.G. 305-07)
questions.explanation: A FAVOR: A = Alcohol likely consumed; F = Family or Supreme Court Matrimonial (don't enter armed except as arresting officer or on official business); A = Advisable not to carry; V = Vacation; O = Off-Duty Employment; R = Risk of Loss or Theft (sports, beach, etc.). (A.G. 305-07)

sergeantFocus.text: Know the complete breast bar hierarchy from top (US Flag) to bottom (Firearms Proficiency). Verify members wear bars in correct order during inspections. Watch for unauthorized cloth flags or fraternal pins — only the American Flag breast bar is authorized at the top position. (A.G. 305-13)

Verify holster compliance (retention device, trigger guard coverage, no clip-on/belly band). Ensure members have passed qualification before carrying modified firearms. Know the Family Court/Supreme Court unarmed requirement.

If member is incapacitated, the supervisory officer must sign and submit the report within 30 days. Verify that damaged article is presented unrepaired and that the member was performing police duty (or extraordinary police action for civilian clothing).

Ensure members of the command wear mourning bands during the correct time periods. For LOD deaths, verify compliance through the 10th day after death for command members. Know that the flag period and mourning band period have different reference dates (funeral vs death). (A.G. 305-14)

As a supervisor, inspect vests when serviceability concerns are raised. Know the approval chain for not wearing: Bureau Chief/Deputy Commissioner for uniform, Immediate Supervisor for civilian enforcement. Direct members to the Patrol Borough for reserve supply regardless of their assigned bureau. Ensure members who are shot while wearing a vest — even if apparently uninjured — visit a doctor. (A.G. 305-15)

As a supervisor, ensure compliance with unauthorized adornment prohibitions during roll call and throughout the tour. Verify belt equipment order at annual pistol qualification. Confirm civilian clothes members carry the Color of the Day band. (A.G. 305-08)

chapters.title: Disciplinary Matters
chapters.readme:
# Section 318 — Disciplinary Matters

## Source
- **Administrative Guide Section:** 318
- **Source PDFs:** A.G. 318-01, 318-02, 318-05, 318-09, 318-10, 318-11, 318-12, 318-14
- **Priority:** High

## Learning Objectives
- Know the three types of CDs (A, B, C), their penalty maximums, and who issues each
- Distinguish Schedule "A" vs "B" violations using the FOUL FRAP mnemonic
- Master the CD issuance process: investigation, interview, three election options, Review Panel powers
- Know grounds for mandatory suspension (A DOG CAR) and which actors must vs. may suspend
- Know the procedure for removing firearms from an intoxicated UMOS
- Understand the definition of intoxication and "common sense" determination standard
- Master interrogation rights and protections for members under investigation
- Know the Garrity warning elements and their implications
- Understand MOS arrested procedures (in-city vs. outside city differences)
- Know CD record clearing and sealing timeframes
- Understand the Citywide CD System entry responsibilities and deadlines

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| A.G. 318-01 | Types of CDs and Their Penalties | completed |
| A.G. 318-02 | Issuance of Schedule "A" and "B" CDs | completed |
| A.G. 318-05 | Cause for Suspension or Modified | completed |
| A.G. 318-09 | MOS Arrested (Includes Civilians) | completed |
| A.G. 318-10 | Removal of Firearms / Unfit for Duty | completed |
| A.G. 318-11 | Interrogation of Members of the Service | completed |
| A.G. 318-12 | Sealing of "B" CDs | completed |
| A.G. 318-14 | Citywide CD System | completed |
| A.G. 318-06 | Suspension of UMOS | completed (covered in 318-10 file) |
| A.G. 318-07 | Suspension of CMOS | completed (covered in 318-10 file) |
| A.G. 318-08 | Cause for Modified Assignment | completed (covered in 318-10 file) |

## Chapter Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
|  | A.G. 318-01: Three CD types (A/B/C), penalty maximums, Schedule A vs B violations, FOUL FRAP mnemonic, loss of property rules, 3rd CD in 6-month rule |
|  | A.G. 318-02: CD process from initiation through adjudication, three election options, Review Panel, transfer rules, IAB-originated CDs, day-type distinctions |
|  | A.G. 318-05: Must vs. authorized to suspend, A DOG CAR mnemonic, G.D.A.S.S. clubs, Sgt vs. TM/SSDD authority, re-suspension |
|  | A.G. 318-09: MOS arrested in-city vs. outside city, notification chains, Ranking Officer investigation, 30-day reporting, court appearance updates |
|  | A.G. 318-10: Unfit for duty procedures, 49 distribution comparison table across 318-06/07/08/10; also covers 318-06 (Suspend UMOS), 318-07 (Suspend CMOS), 318-08 (Modified) distribution rules |
|  | A.G. 318-10: PD469-150 preparation, investigation standard, firearms removal, report distribution (8 copies), mandatory counseling referral |
|  | A.G. 318-14: Database entry deadlines, access management, record removal/destruction timeframes, sealing of "B" CDs |
|  | A.G. 318-11: PD339-121, counsel rights, BWC viewing, prohibited tactics, Garrity warning, tape/transcript deadlines, member obligations |
|  | Intoxication definition, key forms, CD types, FOUL FRAP, A DOG CAR, election options, clearing timeframes, all key timeframes |
|  | 20 multiple-choice questions covering all AG 318 procedures |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- **CD Types:** A = 5 days (CO/XO), B = 10 days (CO/XO), C = 20 days (Dept Advocate only)
- **FOUL FRAP:** Mnemonic for Schedule "B" violations
- **A DOG CAR:** Mnemonic for mandatory suspension grounds; impaired (under .08) is NOT on the list
- **Must vs. Authorized:** Sgt+ must suspend any MOS; TM/SSDD authorized to suspend CMOS only
- **IAB-originated CDs:** Findings = investigating entity approval; Discipline = DC Dept Advocate approval
- **CD Review Panel:** Can increase penalty up to double the CO's proposal
- **3 working days:** Member election of CD option
- **60 days:** CD adjudication deadline
- **PD469-150:** Both the referring supervisor AND CO/XO/duty captain prepare their own reports immediately
- **Determination standard:** Totality of circumstances, common sense, consider time elapsed
- **Mandatory actions:** Remove firearms, modified assignment/suspension, contact Counseling Services Unit (mandated)
- **IAB notification:** Obtain log number from IAB Command Center
- **Interrogation:** PD339-121 before questioning; no "off the record" questions; captain+ determines reasonable time for counsel
- **Garrity protection:** Statements cannot be used criminally but CAN be used departmentally
- **MOS arrested:** In-city = desk officer + Boro CO direct; outside city = no desk officer, no duty captain, Chief designates investigator
- **Record clearing:** Unsub = 1 yr (always); "A" = 1 yr (clean record); "B" = 3 yrs from disposition (must request sealing in writing)
chapters.keyTerms:
# Section 318 — Key Terms and Definitions

## Intoxication

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Intoxication** | Unfitness for duty due to influence of alcohol, narcotics, or other drug, or circumstances indicating member may have been intoxicated during earlier period related to the incident |

## Key Forms

| Form | Used For |
|------|---------|
| **SUPERVISOR'S FITNESS FOR DUTY REPORT (PD469-150)** | Prepared by both referring supervisor AND CO/XO/duty captain based on observations |
| **NOTIFICATION OF ALLEGATION(S) AND EMPLOYEE'S RIGHTS (PD339-121)** | Provided to member prior to interrogation for review and endorsement |
| **CHARGES AND SPECIFICATIONS (PD468-121)** | Formal disciplinary charges served on member |

## Interrogation Rights

| Right | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **PD339-121** | Must be provided before questioning |
| **Counsel** | Permitted for serious violations or sufficient justification |
| **BWC Review** | Member may view own BWC recording related to incident prior to questioning |
| **Line Organization** | Representative present at all times during interrogation |
| **Reasonable Time** | Captain or above determines what constitutes "reasonable time" for counsel |

## Garrity Warning Elements

| Element |
|---------|
| Official investigation by Police Department |
| Questions specifically directed and narrowly related to official duties |
| All rights under NY State law, State Constitution, US Constitution |
| Right not to be compelled to incriminate yourself |
| Right to legal counsel at each stage |
| Refusal to testify on official duties = departmental charges → possible dismissal |
| Statements CANNOT be used in criminal proceedings |
| Statements CAN be used in departmental charges |

## Key Timeframes

| Item | Timeframe |
|------|-----------|
| Post-charge interrogation completion | At least **10 days** before Department trial |
| Tape provided after charge service | Within **20 days** |
| Tape provided after post-charge interrogation | Within **5 days** |
| Transcript to member | By **1000 hours** on trial date |

## Report Distribution — Intoxicated UMOS (8 Copies)

| Recipient | Method |
|-----------|--------|
| First Deputy Commissioner | Original (DIRECT) + Through channels |
| Chief of Department | DIRECT |
| Chief of Personnel | DIRECT |
| Chief of Internal Affairs | DIRECT |
| Deputy Commissioner, Trials | DIRECT |
| Department Advocate's Office | DIRECT |
| Member's CO | DIRECT |

## Prohibited Interrogation Tactics

| Prohibited |
|-----------|
| "Off the record" questions |
| Offensive language or threats (transfer, dismissal, disciplinary punishment) |
| Promises of reward for answering questions |

## Command Discipline Types

| Type | Issued By | Max Penalty |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| **Schedule "A"** | CO/XO | 5 days |
| **Schedule "B"** | CO/XO | 10 days |
| **Schedule "C"** | Department Advocate only | 20 days |

## Schedule "B" CD Mnemonic — FOUL FRAP

| Letter | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| **F** | FADO (Force, Abuse of authority, Discourtesy, Offensive Language) |
| **O** | Other appropriate by CO/XO (after notifying Adjutant and consulting Advocate) |
| **U** | Unauthorized use of a Department Vehicle |
| **L** | Loss of Shield, Activity Log, or Department property |
| **F** | Failure to give name and shield to person requesting |
| **R** | Radio: fail to respond, acknowledge, give disposition, or unauthorized transmission |
| **A** | Alcohol in dept. facility or vehicle unless within scope of assignment |
| **P** | Prisoner — failure to safeguard |

## Suspension Mnemonic — A DOG CAR

| Letter | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| **A** | Arrest for PL crime (Misd or Fel) or DWI (VTL 1192 subs 2-4a) |
| **D** | Dole Test — Refuse to take |
| **O** | Order — Refuse a lawful order |
| **G** | G.O. 15 — Refuse to answer |
| **C** | Club — Patronize a "Good Ass Club" (G.D.A.S.S.: Gambling, Drugs, After hours, Social club, Smoke) |
| **A** | AWOL — 5 consecutive tours |
| **R** | Roll Call / during the tour — Refuse assignment |

## Suspension Authority

| Actor | Authority Level | Who They Suspend |
|-------|----------------|-----------------|
| Uniformed Sergeant (or above) | **Must** suspend | Any MOS (uniformed or civilian) |
| TM/SSDD | **Authorized to** suspend | CMOS (civilians under their command) |

## Member's Three CD Election Options

| Option | Result |
|--------|--------|
| Accept both finding and penalty | Adjudicated at command level |
| Accept finding but not penalty | CD Review Panel decides |
| Accept neither — opt for Charges | Goes to Department Advocate |

## CD Record Clearing Timeframes

| CD Type | Time | Measured From | Condition |
|---------|------|--------------|-----------|
| Unsubstantiated (A or B) | 1 year | Issuance | None — always removed |
| Schedule "A" | 1 year | Issuance | No subsequent discipline of any kind |
| Schedule "B" (sealing) | 3 years | Disposition | No subsequent "B" or Charges; member must request in writing |

## Key Timeframes — CD Process

| Item | Timeframe |
|------|-----------|
| Member election of CD option | **3 working days** from CO/XO's decision |
| CD adjudication deadline | **60 days** from issuance |
| Citywide system entry (issuance) | **5 working days** from issuance |
| Citywide system entry (adjudication) | **5 working days** from adjudication |
| CO sign-off on Citywide entries | **5 working days** from completion of adjudication entries |
| 49 to Chief of Dept (arrested MOS) | **24 hours** |
| MOS updates CO on case status | Every **30 days** |

## Law vs. Procedure Questions

| Question Type | Who to Ask |
|---------------|-----------|
| Questions on **Law** | Legal Bureau |
| Questions on **Procedure** | Department Advocate |

questions.text: Upon observing a UMOS who appears unfit for duty due to intoxication, the supervisory member must prepare the SUPERVISOR'S FITNESS FOR DUTY REPORT (PD469-150):
questions.answerFull: C) The supervisory member must prepare the PD469-150 immediately based upon observations of the member. The CO/XO/duty captain must also prepare their own PD469-150 immediately. (A.G. 318-10)
questions.explanation: The supervisory member must prepare the PD469-150 immediately based upon observations of the member. The CO/XO/duty captain must also prepare their own PD469-150 immediately. (A.G. 318-10)

questions.text: When a UMOS is determined to be intoxicated, the CO/XO/duty captain should:
options: A) Issue a verbal warning and send member home
B) Remove firearms, place on modified assignment or suspend, and advise of counseling
C) Notify Internal Affairs Bureau only
D) Contact the Police Commissioner directly
questions.answerFull: B) The CO/XO/duty captain removes firearms (per A.G. 318-13), places member on modified assignment or suspends, advises of Counseling Service Programs, and mandates supervisory officer contact to Counseling Services Unit. (A.G. 318-10)
questions.explanation: The CO/XO/duty captain removes firearms (per A.G. 318-13), places member on modified assignment or suspends, advises of Counseling Service Programs, and mandates supervisory officer contact to Counseling Services Unit. (A.G. 318-10)

questions.text: The Typed Letterhead report for an intoxicated UMOS must be prepared in how many copies and forwarded to how many recipients?
questions.answerFull: C) Eight copies are prepared and forwarded to: First Deputy Commissioner (original, DIRECT + through channels), Chief of Department, Chief of Personnel, Chief of Internal Affairs, Deputy Commissioner Trials, Department Advocate's Office, and member's CO — all DIRECT. (A.G. 318-10)
questions.explanation: Eight copies are prepared and forwarded to: First Deputy Commissioner (original, DIRECT + through channels), Chief of Department, Chief of Personnel, Chief of Internal Affairs, Deputy Commissioner Trials, Department Advocate's Office, and member's CO — all DIRECT. (A.G. 318-10)

questions.text: Prior to questioning a member who is the subject of an official investigation, the interrogating officer must provide:
options: B) NOTIFICATION OF ALLEGATION(S) AND EMPLOYEE'S RIGHTS (PD339-121)
C) SUPERVISOR'S FITNESS FOR DUTY REPORT (PD469-150)
questions.answerFull: B) Prior to questioning, the interrogating officer must provide the member a completed PD339-121 for review and endorsement, allowing reasonable time for review. (A.G. 318-11)
questions.explanation: Prior to questioning, the interrogating officer must provide the member a completed PD339-121 for review and endorsement, allowing reasonable time for review. (A.G. 318-11)

questions.text: A member who is the subject of an official investigation has the right to view which recording prior to questioning?
options: A) Any CCTV footage from the precinct
B) Their own Body-Worn Camera recording related to the incident
C) All BWC recordings from all officers at the scene
D) No recordings may be viewed
questions.answerFull: B) The member will be permitted to view their own BWC recording related to the incident(s) under investigation prior to questioning at a time and place deemed appropriate by the supervisor in charge of the investigation. (A.G. 318-11)
questions.explanation: The member will be permitted to view their own BWC recording related to the incident(s) under investigation prior to questioning at a time and place deemed appropriate by the supervisor in charge of the investigation. (A.G. 318-11)

questions.text: A UMOS who refuses to answer questions specifically directed and narrowly related to official duties will be:
options: C) Subject to suspension from duty
questions.answerFull: C) Members must answer questions specifically directed and narrowly related to official duties. Refusal shall result in suspension from duty. (A.G. 318-11)
questions.explanation: Members must answer questions specifically directed and narrowly related to official duties. Refusal shall result in suspension from duty. (A.G. 318-11)

questions.text: Under the Garrity warning, a member's statements during an official investigation:
options: A) Can be used in both criminal proceedings and departmental charges
B) Cannot be used in criminal proceedings but CAN be used in departmental charges
C) Cannot be used in either criminal proceedings or departmental charges
D) Can be used in criminal proceedings but NOT departmental charges
questions.answerFull: B) If the member answers, neither their statements nor any information gained by reason of such statements can be used against them in subsequent criminal proceedings. However, the statements may be used against them in relation to subsequent departmental charges. (A.G. 318-11)
questions.explanation: If the member answers, neither their statements nor any information gained by reason of such statements can be used against them in subsequent criminal proceedings. However, the statements may be used against them in relation to subsequent departmental charges. (A.G. 318-11)

questions.text: An interrogation conducted after CHARGES AND SPECIFICATIONS have been served must be completed at least how many days prior to the Department trial date?
questions.answerFull: B) An interrogation conducted after service of charges must be completed at least 10 days prior to the date of Department trial, except as directed by the Deputy Commissioner - Trials. (A.G. 318-11)
questions.explanation: An interrogation conducted after service of charges must be completed at least 10 days prior to the date of Department trial, except as directed by the Deputy Commissioner - Trials. (A.G. 318-11)

questions.text: The determination of what constitutes a "reasonable time" for a member to obtain counsel before interrogation is made by:
options: C) The captain (or above) in charge of the investigation
questions.answerFull: C) In all cases, the determination as to what is a reasonable time will be made by the captain (or above) in charge of the investigation, considering the nature of the investigation, timeliness, and stage of the investigation. (A.G. 318-11)
questions.explanation: In all cases, the determination as to what is a reasonable time will be made by the captain (or above) in charge of the investigation, considering the nature of the investigation, timeliness, and stage of the investigation. (A.G. 318-11)

questions.text: A supervisory officer is mandated to contact the Counseling Services Unit when a member is:
options: A) Placed on modified assignment, suspended, or has firearms removed due to unfitness
questions.answerFull: A) A supervisory officer is mandated in all cases to contact the Counseling Services Unit on behalf of a member who is placed on modified assignment, suspended, or has their firearms removed due to being unfit for duty. (A.G. 318-10)
questions.explanation: A supervisory officer is mandated in all cases to contact the Counseling Services Unit on behalf of a member who is placed on modified assignment, suspended, or has their firearms removed due to being unfit for duty. (A.G. 318-10)

questions.text: Which of the following is a prohibited interrogation tactic?
options: B) Having a line organization representative present
C) Promises of reward for answering questions
D) Scheduling interrogation during business hours
questions.answerFull: C) Prohibited tactics include: "off the record" questions, offensive language or threats (transfer, dismissal, disciplinary punishment), and promises of reward for answering questions. (A.G. 318-11)
questions.explanation: Prohibited tactics include: "off the record" questions, offensive language or threats (transfer, dismissal, disciplinary punishment), and promises of reward for answering questions. (A.G. 318-11)

questions.text: Questions and answers from an official investigation interrogation may be released outside the Department only with written approval of:
options: C) The Deputy Commissioner - Legal Matters
D) The Chief of Internal Affairs
questions.answerFull: C) Questions and answers resulting from interrogation are confidential and not to be revealed or released outside the Department without prior written approval of the Deputy Commissioner - Legal Matters. If a subpoena duces tecum is received, contact the Legal Bureau immediately. (A.G. 318-11)
questions.explanation: Questions and answers resulting from interrogation are confidential and not to be revealed or released outside the Department without prior written approval of the Deputy Commissioner - Legal Matters. If a subpoena duces tecum is received, contact the Legal Bureau immediately. (A.G. 318-11)

questions.text: The maximum penalty for a Schedule "A" Command Discipline is:
questions.answerFull: B) The three types of CDs have different maximums: "A" = 5 days, "B" = 10 days, "C" = 20 days. "C" CDs are issued only by the Department Advocate. (A.G. 318-01)
questions.explanation: The three types of CDs have different maximums: "A" = 5 days, "B" = 10 days, "C" = 20 days. "C" CDs are issued only by the Department Advocate. (A.G. 318-01)

questions.text: When a CD results from an IAB investigation and the CO wants to change the recommended disciplinary action, the CO must obtain conferral and approval from:
options: B) The Deputy Commissioner, Department Advocate
questions.answerFull: B) When a CD comes from IAB: to change **findings**, you need approval of the **investigating entity**. To change **discipline**, you need approval of the **DC Department Advocate**. The exam often switches these two. (A.G. 318-02)
questions.explanation: When a CD comes from IAB: to change **findings**, you need approval of the **investigating entity**. To change **discipline**, you need approval of the **DC Department Advocate**. The exam often switches these two. (A.G. 318-02)

questions.text: The CD Review Panel may increase the penalty proposed by the CO to not more than:
options: B) One and a half times the proposed penalty
questions.answerFull: C) The CD Review Panel may approve, reduce, or increase the penalty — but the increase cannot exceed double what the CO proposed. (A.G. 318-02)
questions.explanation: The CD Review Panel may approve, reduce, or increase the penalty — but the increase cannot exceed double what the CO proposed. (A.G. 318-02)

questions.text: A Uniformed Sergeant must suspend an MOS for all of the following EXCEPT:
options: B) Refusal to take a Dole Test
questions.answerFull: C) The mandatory suspension grounds (A DOG CAR) include DWI (VTL 1192 subs 2-4a) but NOT impairment below .08. By its absence from the list, impaired is not a must-suspend. (A.G. 318-05)
questions.explanation: The mandatory suspension grounds (A DOG CAR) include DWI (VTL 1192 subs 2-4a) but NOT impairment below .08. By its absence from the list, impaired is not a must-suspend. (A.G. 318-05)

questions.text: A member must elect one of the three CD options within what timeframe of receiving the CO/XO's decision?
questions.answerFull: B) The member must notify the CO in writing on the CD of the option elected within 3 **working** days of receiving the CO/XO's decision. When the AG does not specify "working," it means calendar days. (A.G. 318-02)
questions.explanation: The member must notify the CO in writing on the CD of the option elected within 3 **working** days of receiving the CO/XO's decision. When the AG does not specify "working," it means calendar days. (A.G. 318-02)

questions.text: Records and dispositions of substantiated Schedule "B" CDs may be sealed after:
options: A) 1 year from issuance with no subsequent discipline
B) 3 years from issuance with no subsequent "B" or Charges
C) 3 years from disposition, upon written request, with no subsequent "B" or Charges
D) They are automatically sealed after 3 years
questions.answerFull: C) Sealing of "B" CDs is NOT automatic — the member must request in writing via a 49 to their CO on the 3rd anniversary of the date of disposition. If no subsequent "B" CDs or Charges during those 3 years, the CO expunges from the command folder. (A.G. 318-12/318-14)
questions.explanation: Sealing of "B" CDs is NOT automatic — the member must request in writing via a 49 to their CO on the 3rd anniversary of the date of disposition. If no subsequent "B" CDs or Charges during those 3 years, the CO expunges from the command folder. (A.G. 318-12/318-14)

questions.text: When an MOS is arrested outside the city, who designates the Captain or above to conduct the investigation?
questions.answerFull: C) When arrested outside the city, there is no "Duty Captain" — the Chief designates a Captain or above to conduct the investigation. Inside the city, the Borough CO assigns the Ranking Officer. (A.G. 318-09)
questions.explanation: When arrested outside the city, there is no "Duty Captain" — the Chief designates a Captain or above to conduct the investigation. Inside the city, the Borough CO assigns the Ranking Officer. (A.G. 318-09)

questions.text: Loss of a Department-issued smartphone results in which type of Command Discipline?
questions.answerFull: A) Loss of an ID Card, Summons, Summons Book, or Department-issued smartphone are all Schedule "A" CDs. Loss of shield, Activity Log, prisoner (failure to safeguard), or other Department property (except the "A" items) are Schedule "B" CDs. (A.G. 318-01)
questions.explanation: Loss of an ID Card, Summons, Summons Book, or Department-issued smartphone are all Schedule "A" CDs. Loss of shield, Activity Log, prisoner (failure to safeguard), or other Department property (except the "A" items) are Schedule "B" CDs. (A.G. 318-01)
