sergeantFocus.text: Know the 8 items the interrogating officer must inform the member about. No "off the record" questions, threats, or reward promises. Interrogation must be recorded. Post-charge interrogation must be completed **10 days** before trial. Assign member to **2nd Platoon**.

The first supervisor on scene must (1) direct member to remain, (2) prepare PD469-150 immediately, (3) notify CO/XO/duty captain. Supervisory officer is **mandated** to contact Counseling Services Unit. IAB Command Center must be notified for log number.

chapters.title: Civilian Personnel
chapters.readme:
# Section 319 — Civilian Personnel

## Source
- **Administrative Guide Section:** 319
- **Source PDF:** A.G. 319-08
- **Priority:** Low

## Learning Objectives
- Know the procedure for reporting and documenting civilian member on-duty injuries
- Identify the 5 required forms for civilian injury reporting
- Understand notification timelines for Civilian Employee Services Unit

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| A.G. 319-08 | Civilian Member - Injury | completed |
| A.G. 319-15 | (Not available) | blocked |

## Chapter Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
|  | A.G. 319-08: Injury reporting, 5 required forms, notification timelines, Workers Compensation |
|  | Forms list, key timeframes, Chief of Personnel authority |
|  | 4 multiple-choice questions covering civilian injury reporting |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- **7-day deadline:** Civilian member must report injury within 7 days
- **5 forms:** WCS-110, WCS-100, WCS-120, DP2002, Time Usage 49
- **Notification:** Civilian Employee Services Unit immediately (business hours) or 0900 next day; copies within 24 hours
- **Leave authority:** Chief of Personnel grants leave with pay for first week (Workers Compensation)
chapters.keyTerms:
# Section 319 — Key Terms and Definitions

## Forms

| Form | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| **Employee Statement Form (FISA WCS-110)** | Civilian member's statement of injury |
| **Supervisor's/Agency Form - Report of Injury (FISA WCS-100)** | Supervisor's report of civilian injury |
| **Witness Statement Form (FISA WCS-120)** | Witness account of civilian injury |
| **Employee Option Form (DP2002)** | Civilian member's option selection |
| **Time Usage 49** | Time Used Due to Job Injury - Workers Compensation |

## Key Timeframes

| Item | Timeframe |
|------|-----------|
| Civilian member reports injury | No later than **7 days** after incident |
| Supervisor notifies Civilian Employee Services Unit | **Immediately** (business hours) or **0900** following business day |
| Forward copies to Civilian Employee Services Unit | Within **24 hours** of notification |

## Key Authority

| Authority | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| **Chief of Personnel** | Empowered to grant leave with pay for first week's absence (Workers Compensation, disabled in line of duty) |

## Memory Aids

> **Memory Aid — 7-24-NOTIFY:** **7** days to report injury, **24** hours to forward copies, **Notify immediately** during business hours (or 0900 next business day if after hours).

> **Memory Aid — FIVE FORMS:** **WCS-110, WCS-100, WCS-120, DP2002, TIME USAGE 49.** Think: **"S-T-A-W-T"** — **S**upervisor **T**ransmits **A**ll **W**ork-related injury **T**ransmittal forms.

questions.text: A civilian member must report an on-duty injury no later than:
questions.answerFull: C) The civilian member must report injury to supervisor at time of incident, and the injury must be reported no later than 7 days after the incident. (A.G. 319-08)
questions.explanation: The civilian member must report injury to supervisor at time of incident, and the injury must be reported no later than 7 days after the incident. (A.G. 319-08)

questions.text: The investigating supervisor must notify the Civilian Employee Services Unit and forward copies within:
options: A) Immediately by telephone; copies within 12 hours
B) Immediately by telephone during business hours; copies within 24 hours
C) By end of tour; copies within 48 hours
D) Within 24 hours; copies within 72 hours
questions.answerFull: B) The supervisor must notify the Civilian Employee Services Unit immediately by telephone during business hours (or at 0900 following business day) and forward copies within 24 hours of notification. (A.G. 319-08)
questions.explanation: The supervisor must notify the Civilian Employee Services Unit immediately by telephone during business hours (or at 0900 following business day) and forward copies within 24 hours of notification. (A.G. 319-08)

questions.text: Who is empowered to grant leave with pay for the first week's absence of a civilian member covered by Workers Compensation?
options: D) The Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget
questions.answerFull: C) The Chief of Personnel is empowered to grant leave with pay for the first week's absence of a member covered by Workers Compensation who is physically disabled in the performance of official duties. (A.G. 319-08)
questions.explanation: The Chief of Personnel is empowered to grant leave with pay for the first week's absence of a member covered by Workers Compensation who is physically disabled in the performance of official duties. (A.G. 319-08)

questions.text: How many forms must the investigating supervisor ensure are prepared when a civilian member is injured on duty?
questions.answerFull: C) Five forms: Employee Statement Form (FISA WCS-110), Supervisor's Report of Injury (FISA WCS-100), Witness Statement Form (FISA WCS-120), Employee Option Form (DP2002), and Time Usage 49. (A.G. 319-08)
questions.explanation: Five forms: Employee Statement Form (FISA WCS-110), Supervisor's Report of Injury (FISA WCS-100), Witness Statement Form (FISA WCS-120), Employee Option Form (DP2002), and Time Usage 49. (A.G. 319-08)

sergeantFocus.text: Know the 5 forms required: WCS-110 (Employee Statement), WCS-100 (Supervisor Report of Injury), WCS-120 (Witness Statement), DP2002 (Employee Option), and Time Usage 49. Chief of Personnel grants leave with pay for first week.

chapters.title: Personnel Matters — General
chapters.readme:
# Section 320 — Personnel Matters — General

## Source
- **Administrative Guide Section:** 320
- **Source PDF:** Public Admin Guide2.pdf
- **Priority:** Medium

## Overview

Section 320 of the Administrative Guide covers general personnel matters including tour exchanges ("mutuals"), monitoring off-duty visits to correctional facilities, and maintenance/transfer of command-level personal records. These procedures contain specific numeric thresholds and timeframes that are frequently tested on the sergeant exam.

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Study File | Status |
|-----------|-------|-----------|--------|
| A.G. 320-01 | Exchange of Tours for Uniformed Members of the Service | personnel-general | completed |
| A.G. 320-02 | Monitoring Off-Duty Visits to Inmates at Correctional Facilities | personnel-general | completed |
| A.G. 320-03 | Maintenance and Transfer of Command-Level Personal Records | personnel-general | completed |

## Priority Topics for Sergeant Exam

1. **Exchange of Tours (320-01)** — 28-day FLSA cycle, same rank/command/below captain, no consecutive tours, 5-day advance submission, PD433-160, Regular Sick only
2. **Correctional Facility Visits (320-02)** — 7-day advance notice, separate notification per visit, IAB notification, copy to DC Internal Affairs
3. **Personal Records (320-03)** — Jan 1/Jul 1 purge dates, 6-month threshold, sealed "Confidential" envelope for transfers, NYS driver's license check

## Study Tips

- The exchange of tours procedure is packed with testable restrictions — memorize: **same rank, same command, below captain, 28-day FLSA cycle, no consecutive tours, 5 days advance, Regular Sick only**
- Know that **PD433-160** is the Exchange of Tour Request form
- The **7-day** advance notice for correctional facility visits and **5-day** advance for tour exchanges are both common exam questions
- For personal records: the **January 1 and July 1** purge dates and **6-month** threshold are classic number questions
- Remember: overtime records go in the **Time Record Folder (TRF)**, NOT the personal folder
chapters.keyTerms:
# Section 320 — Key Terms & Critical Numbers

## Definitions

| Term | Definition | Procedure |
|------|-----------|-----------|
| Exchange of Tour | Voluntary performance of another MOS's tour; also known as a "mutual" | 320-01 |
| FLSA Cycle | 28-day Fair Labor Standards Act work cycle | 320-01 |
| Personal Folder | 11" x 14" folder inscribed with name, tax registry, shield, date of appointment | 320-03 |
| Time Record Folder (TRF) | Separate folder for overtime reports — NOT in personal folder | 320-03 |

## Critical Numbers

| Number | What It Means | Procedure |
|--------|--------------|-----------|
| 5 days | Minimum advance submission for Exchange of Tour Request | 320-01 |
| 7 days | Minimum advance notice for off-duty correctional facility visit | 320-02 |
| 24 hours | Department can reschedule any same-rank MOS without this notice if mutual fails | 320-01 |
| 28 days | FLSA cycle — mutuals must be within the same cycle | 320-01 |
| 6 months | Reports older than this are purged from personal folder | 320-03 |
| Jan 1 / Jul 1 | Dates when old reports are purged from personal folders | 320-03 |

## Exchange of Tour Requirements (320-01)

| Requirement | Detail |
|-------------|--------|
| Rank | Same rank, below captain |
| Assignment | Same command |
| Qualification | Must be qualified to perform each other's duties |
| Timing | Within same 28-day FLSA cycle |
| Consecutive tours | NOT permitted (no 3rd platoon followed by 1st platoon) |
| Sick reporting | Must report Regular Sick (not Administrative Sick); requires Dept surgeon visit |
| Overtime | No FLSA or contractual overtime generated |
| Form | PD433-160 (Exchange of Tour Request) |

## Forms

| Form Number | Name | Procedure |
|-------------|------|-----------|
| PD433-160 | Exchange of Tour Request | 320-01 |
| PD406-143 | Force Record | 320-03 |
| PD406-143a | Force Record Supplement | 320-03 |
| PD446-141 | Source Document for Personnel Data Card | 320-03 |
| PD138-061 | Employee's Withholding Exemption Certificate | 320-03 |
| PD429-122A | Sick Report Worksheet | 320-03 |
| PD429-131 | Sick Report Return | 320-03 |
| PD468-123 | Supervisor's Complaint Report/Command Discipline Election Report | 320-03 |
| PD120-060 | Annual Uniform & Equipment Inspection Checklist | 320-03 |
| PD449-151 | Personnel Records Transmittal | 320-03 |
| PD439-142 | Individual Conduct Summary | 320-03 |
| PD138-064 | Overtime Report | 320-03 |

## Correctional Facility Visit Requirements (320-02)

| Step | Who | Action |
|------|-----|--------|
| 1 | MOS | Prepare typed letterhead with 6 required data points, submit 7 days in advance |
| 2 | CO | Notify IAB, Command Center |
| 3 | CO | Approve/disapprove |
| 4 | CO | Notify member of determination |
| 5 | CO | Forward copy to Deputy Commissioner, Internal Affairs; file in personal folder |

## Personal Folder — Reports Purged on Jan 1 and Jul 1

1. Officer Profile Report (if > 6 months old)
2. FINEST messages re: sick reports (if > 6 months old)
3. Sick Report Return (if > 6 months old)

> Purged reports are filed and safeguarded until an Operations Order for destruction is received.

## Memory Aids

> **Memory Aid — EXCHANGE OF TOUR RULES (mutual):** **S.S.S.F.F.C.** = **S**ame rank, **S**ame command, **S**ame FLSA cycle, **F**it for duty (no Admin Sick), **F**ive days advance notice, **C**annot be two consecutive tours. All five rules apply to every mutual.

> **Memory Aid — PERSONAL FOLDER PURGE (Jan 1 / July 1):** **J-Jan** and **J-Jul** = **J**anuary 1st and **J**uly 1st. Reports purged if more than 6 months old: **O**fficer Profile, **F**INEST messages, **S**ick Report Return = **OFS** — think "O.F.S. = Old Files Shred."

> **Memory Aid — CORRECTIONAL FACILITY VISIT:** **7 days** notice, notify **IAB** and **Command Center**, forward to **Deputy Commissioner, IAB**, file in **personal folder**. Think: **I.C.D.C.** — **I**AB, **C**ommand, **D**eputy **C**ommissioner IAB = the three notifications.

questions.text: An exchange of tour (mutual) must be performed within the same:
questions.answerFull: B) Exchange of tours must be performed in the same twenty-eight day Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) cycle. (A.G. 320-01)
questions.explanation: Exchange of tours must be performed in the same twenty-eight day Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) cycle. (A.G. 320-01)

questions.text: A uniformed member of the service requesting an exchange of tours must submit the EXCHANGE OF TOUR REQUEST to the operations coordinator at least how many days in advance?
questions.answerFull: B) The request must be submitted at least five days in advance. (A.G. 320-01, step #5)
questions.explanation: The request must be submitted at least five days in advance. (A.G. 320-01, step #5)

questions.text: During an exchange of tours, a uniformed member of the service who becomes ill must report:
options: B) Regular Sick, which requires a visit to the Department surgeon
C) Either Administrative or Regular Sick, at the member's discretion
questions.answerFull: B) Members are NOT permitted to report Administrative Sick during a mutual and must report Regular Sick, which requires a visit to the Department surgeon. (A.G. 320-01, step #2b)
questions.explanation: Members are NOT permitted to report Administrative Sick during a mutual and must report Regular Sick, which requires a visit to the Department surgeon. (A.G. 320-01, step #2b)

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a requirement for an exchange of tours?
options: A) Members must be the same rank
B) Members must be assigned to the same command
C) Members must be below the rank of captain
D) Members must be assigned to the same platoon
questions.answerFull: D) Members do not need to be on the same platoon — they must be same rank, same command, below captain, and qualified to perform each other's duties. (A.G. 320-01)
questions.explanation: Members do not need to be on the same platoon — they must be same rank, same command, below captain, and qualified to perform each other's duties. (A.G. 320-01)

questions.text: If a uniformed member of the service fails to perform an exchanged tour, the Department may reschedule any same-rank MOS without how much notice?
questions.answerFull: B) The Department may reschedule any uniformed member of the service of the same rank without twenty-four hour notice. (A.G. 320-01, Additional Data)
questions.explanation: The Department may reschedule any uniformed member of the service of the same rank without twenty-four hour notice. (A.G. 320-01, Additional Data)

questions.text: A member of the service intending to visit an inmate at a correctional facility while off-duty must forward a typed letterhead to the commanding officer at least how many days prior?
questions.answerFull: C) At least seven days prior to the intended visit. (A.G. 320-02, step #1)
questions.explanation: At least seven days prior to the intended visit. (A.G. 320-02, step #1)

questions.text: When a member of the service requests to visit an inmate at a correctional facility, the commanding officer must notify which of the following?
options: A) Patrol Borough Commander and Operations Unit
C) Chief of Department and Personnel Bureau
D) Community Affairs Bureau and Legal Bureau
questions.answerFull: B) The commanding officer must notify IAB and the Command Center of details. (A.G. 320-02, step #2)
questions.explanation: The commanding officer must notify IAB and the Command Center of details. (A.G. 320-02, step #2)

questions.text: Reports more than six months old are removed from personal folders on which dates?
options: C) The first Monday of each quarter
questions.answerFull: B) Reports more than six months old are removed on January 1 and July 1 each year. (A.G. 320-03, step #4)
questions.explanation: Reports more than six months old are removed on January 1 and July 1 each year. (A.G. 320-03, step #4)

questions.text: When a member of command is transferred, the personal folder jacket is forwarded to the new command in what manner?
options: C) In a sealed envelope marked "Confidential"
D) Hand-delivered by the transferred member
questions.answerFull: C) The folder jacket is forwarded in a sealed envelope marked "Confidential." (A.G. 320-03, step #7)
questions.explanation: The folder jacket is forwarded in a sealed envelope marked "Confidential." (A.G. 320-03, step #7)

questions.text: The OVERTIME REPORT (PD138-064) is maintained in which location?
options: B) A separate Time Record Folder (TRF)
C) The command's central filing system
questions.answerFull: B) The Overtime Report is to be kept in a separate Time Record Folder (TRF), not in the personal folder. (A.G. 320-03, Additional Data)
questions.explanation: The Overtime Report is to be kept in a separate Time Record Folder (TRF), not in the personal folder. (A.G. 320-03, Additional Data)

sergeantFocus.text: As a supervisor, you are responsible for ensuring personal folders are properly maintained, documents are filed correctly, and purge schedules are followed. When members transfer out, verify the sealed confidential envelope and photocopy of Force Record are provided. Check NYS driver's license on assignment.

While sergeants are not typically the primary grant administrators, you may serve as programmatic or fiscal contacts. Know the key rules: grant funds cannot supplant existing funding, overtime-in-time does not expend grant funds, equipment bidding takes 12-18 months, and HUD grants go through Housing Bureau. Timely reporting and compliance are your direct responsibility as a command contact.

chapters.title: Leave, Payroll & Timekeeping
chapters.readme:
# Section 324 — Leave, Payroll & Timekeeping

## Source
- **Patrol Guide Section:** 324
- **Source PDF:** public-pguide4.pdf
- **Priority:** Medium

## Learning Objectives
- Understand the various leave categories (annual, sick, military, personal, etc.) and eligibility requirements
- Know the procedures for requesting, approving, and documenting leave
- Identify the timekeeping and payroll procedures including overtime documentation
- Recognize the Sergeant's responsibilities for monitoring attendance and leave abuse
- Know the AWOL escalation timeline: 2 hours (D.O. inquiries) vs. 4 hours (major notifications including IAB)
- Understand the report routing for AWOL investigations (Typed Letterhead to Chief of Department)

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| A.G. 324-01 | Vacation Policy | completed |
| A.G. 324-02 | Working During Vacation | completed |
| A.G. 324-03 | Vacation Lost Due to Sick Leave | completed |
| A.G. 324-04 | Ranking Officers Vacations | completed |
| A.G. 324-05 | Authorized Leave | completed |
| A.G. 324-06 | Bereavement Leave | completed |
| P.G. 324-17 | AWOL | completed |

## Chapter Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
|  | P.G. 324-17: AWOL escalation timeline, D.O. duties at 2 and 4 hours, C.O. investigation, report routing, return-to-duty restriction |
|  | AWOL definitions, key roles, timeframes, reports, applicability |
|  | 7 multiple-choice questions covering AWOL procedures |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- Approving and tracking subordinate leave requests and attendance patterns
- Identifying and addressing potential leave abuse or chronic absenteeism
- Ensuring accurate timekeeping and overtime documentation for the platoon
- Understanding the sick leave reporting and monitoring procedures
- **AWOL escalation:** At 2 hours, D.O. sends a supervisor to member's residence (in city); at 4 hours, C.O./Duty Captain, Boro, Operations, and IAB are all notified
- **Entire-tour AWOL:** Member cannot return to duty without prior permission of Chief of Department
- **When member located:** Notify Boro, Operations, IAB, and Sick Desk Supervisor
chapters.keyTerms:
# Section 324 — Key Terms and Definitions

## AWOL

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **AWOL** | Absent Without Official Leave — member fails to report for duty without authorization |
| **2-Hour AWOL** | First threshold at beginning of tour; Desk Officer makes telephone inquiries and responds to member's residence |
| **4-Hour AWOL** | Escalation threshold; notifications expand to C.O./Duty Captain, Boro, Operations, and IAB Command Center |
| **Entire Tour AWOL** | Member absent for full tour; cannot return to duty without **prior permission of Chief of Department** |
| **ICCU** | Inter-City Correspondence Unit — contacted to notify local PD when member's residence is outside the city |

## Key Roles

| Role | AWOL Responsibility |
|------|-------------------|
| **Desk Officer** | Makes all initial inquiries at 2 hours; escalates notifications at 4 hours |
| **C.O. / Duty Captain** | Conducts immediate investigation at 4 hours; interviews member when located |
| **IAB Command Center** | Notified at 4-hour mark and when member is located |
| **Sick Desk Supervisor** | Notified when AWOL member is located |
| **Chief of Department** | Receives Typed Letterhead (49) report; must authorize return to duty after entire-tour AWOL |

## Key Timeframes

| Item | Timeframe |
|------|-----------|
| AWOL threshold — D.O. inquiries begin | **2 hours** after beginning of tour |
| AWOL threshold — major notifications | **4 hours** after start of tour |
| Report to Chief of Department | Typed Letterhead (49) with results and action taken |

## Key Reports

| Report | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| **Typed Letterhead (49)** | To Chief of Department — results and action taken or contemplated |
| **Supplemental Report (49)** | When member is located — facts and discipline not previously reported |
| **Command Log** | Entry of facts, notifications, and investigation results |

## Applicability Note

| Applies To | Does NOT Apply To |
|-----------|------------------|
| Uniformed Members of the Service (UMOS) | Civilian Members of the Service (CMOS) |

## Memory Aids

> **Memory Aid — AWOL 2 vs 4 HOURS:** **2** hours = **T**elephone and **T**ower (residence response by **T**he D.O.'s **T**eam sending a **S**upervisor). **4** hours = **I**AB, **C**.O., **O**ps, **B**oro — **I** = Internal Affairs, all others starting with letters in **ICOB** = **I**-**C**-**O**-**B** = **I**AB, **C**.O., **O**ps, **B**oro.

> **Memory Aid — WHO RESPONDS TO RESIDENCE:** For city-dwelling AWOL members, a **supervisor** (boss) is sent — not the member's own supervisor, not just any officer. For out-of-city members, ICCU notifies **local PD**.

> **Memory Aid — ENTIRE TOUR AWOL:** **C**hief **O**f **D**epartment **P**ermission required before member can return to duty. Think: **C.O.D.P.** = **C**hief's **O**rder **D**aily **P**ermission. Or: **N**o **D**uty **W**ithout **C**hief = **NDWC** — can't go back to work without the Chief's say-so.

questions.text: When a UMOS is AWOL for 2 hours at the beginning of the tour and lives within the city, the desk officer should request the D.O. of the member's resident precinct to have whom respond to the residence?
questions.answerFull: C) When a member is AWOL for 2 hours and lives in the city, the D.O. requests the member's resident precinct D.O. to send a **boss** (supervisor) to the residence. If outside the city, ICCU notifies local PD. (P.G. 324-17)
questions.explanation: When a member is AWOL for 2 hours and lives in the city, the D.O. requests the member's resident precinct D.O. to send a **boss** (supervisor) to the residence. If outside the city, ICCU notifies local PD. (P.G. 324-17)

questions.text: At the 4-hour AWOL mark, the desk officer must notify all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.answerFull: C) At 4 hours, the D.O. notifies the C.O./Duty Captain, Boro/Counterpart, Operations, and IAB Command Center. The Sick Desk Supervisor is notified later — when the member is **located**. (P.G. 324-17)
questions.explanation: At 4 hours, the D.O. notifies the C.O./Duty Captain, Boro/Counterpart, Operations, and IAB Command Center. The Sick Desk Supervisor is notified later — when the member is **located**. (P.G. 324-17)

questions.text: A UMOS has been AWOL for an entire tour. The next day, the member reports to the command for duty. The member:
options: A) May be assigned to duty at the desk officer's discretion
B) May be assigned to duty with the platoon commander's approval
C) Will not be assigned to duty without prior permission of the Chief of Department
D) Will be automatically suspended pending investigation
questions.answerFull: C) A member AWOL for an entire tour will NOT be assigned to duty without **prior** permission of the Chief of Department. (P.G. 324-17)
questions.explanation: A member AWOL for an entire tour will NOT be assigned to duty without **prior** permission of the Chief of Department. (P.G. 324-17)

questions.text: When an AWOL member is located, who among the following must be notified?
options: B) Boro Command/Counterpart, Operations, IAB, and Sick Desk Supervisor
C) Only the C.O. and IAB
D) Only the Boro Commander and Operations
questions.answerFull: B) When the AWOL member is located, the C.O./Duty Captain notifies the Boro Command/Counterpart, Operations, IAB, AND the Sick Desk Supervisor. (P.G. 324-17)
questions.explanation: When the AWOL member is located, the C.O./Duty Captain notifies the Boro Command/Counterpart, Operations, IAB, AND the Sick Desk Supervisor. (P.G. 324-17)

questions.text: The AWOL procedure (P.G. 324-17) applies to:
options: A) All members of the service
B) Uniformed members only — it does NOT apply to CMOS
questions.answerFull: B) The AWOL procedure does NOT apply to civilian members of the service (CMOS). (P.G. 324-17)
questions.explanation: The AWOL procedure does NOT apply to civilian members of the service (CMOS). (P.G. 324-17)

questions.text: When the C.O. investigates an AWOL and prepares the Typed Letterhead (49), the routing is:
options: A) 1 copy direct to Chief of Department, 1 through channels (boro)
B) 2 copies direct to Chief of Department
C) 1 copy to IAB, 1 to Chief of Department
D) 1 copy to the member's file, 1 to Operations
questions.answerFull: A) If the C.O. investigated, 1 copy goes direct to the Chief of Department and 1 goes through channels (boro). If the Duty Captain investigated, 1 direct to COD and 2 copies to the MOS's C.O. (P.G. 324-17)
questions.explanation: If the C.O. investigated, 1 copy goes direct to the Chief of Department and 1 goes through channels (boro). If the Duty Captain investigated, 1 direct to COD and 2 copies to the MOS's C.O. (P.G. 324-17)

questions.text: When a member is AWOL for 2 hours and lives outside the city, the desk officer should:
options: A) Request the member's resident precinct D.O. to send a supervisor
B) Request ICCU to notify local PD to respond to the residence
C) Notify IAB Command Center immediately
questions.answerFull: B) If the member lives outside the city, the D.O. requests ICCU to notify local PD to respond. If in the city, the resident precinct D.O. sends a supervisor. (P.G. 324-17)
questions.explanation: If the member lives outside the city, the D.O. requests ICCU to notify local PD to respond. If in the city, the resident precinct D.O. sends a supervisor. (P.G. 324-17)

chapters.title: Career Development
chapters.readme:
# Section 329 — Career Development

## Source
- **Administrative Guide Section:** 329
- **Source PDFs:** A.G. 329-06, A.G. 329-07
- **Priority:** Low

## Learning Objectives
- Know the retirement process including terminal leave computation and PPF reporting
- Understand LEOSA certification requirements for retired members
- Know the property return matrix for retiring and resigning members
- Understand resignation procedures, reinstatement rules, and vacation accrual impact

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| A.G. 329-06 | Discontinuance of Police Service — Retirement or Vested Interest | completed |
| A.G. 329-07 | Discontinuance of Police Service — Resignation | completed |

## Chapter Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
|  | A.G. 329-06: Terminal leave computation, Tier 2/3 reporting, LEOSA, property return; A.G. 329-07: Resignation procedures, reinstatement rules, Operations Unit notifications |
|  | Terminal leave, LEOSA, key forms, timeframes, terminal leave restrictions, resignation rules |
|  | 12 multiple-choice questions covering retirement and resignation procedures |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- **Terminal leave:** 1 month per 10 years + 3 days per year beyond 20; no leaves after terminal leave begins
- **Tier 2 vs Tier 3:** Tier 2 = 30 days advance to PPF, retire by 63; Tier 3 = no notice required, retire by 62
- **LEOSA:** Requires qualification within 12 months, 20+ years service or LOD injury, good standing
- **Resignation:** Bars reinstatement except Police Commissioner within 1 year; firearms to borough Property Clerk ONLY
- **Property accountability:** Benefits not returned until ALL property accounted for
chapters.keyTerms:
# Section 329 — Key Terms and Definitions

## Retirement Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Terminal Leave** | Leave with pay computed at PPF; 1 calendar month per 10 years + 3 days per year beyond 20 + 1 day per 4 months |
| **LEOSA Certification Card** | Wallet-sized card exempting retired LEOs from local/state concealed carry prohibitions; must accompany retired ID card |
| **Vested Interest** | Discontinuance of police service with pension benefit eligibility but not full retirement |
| **Resignation** | Voluntary separation other than retirement/vested interest |
| **Dismissal Probation** | Period included in negotiated penalties for alcohol-related motor vehicle incidents |
| **NICS Check** | National Instant Criminal Background Check — required to retain post-1/15/13 Department-issued firearms |

## Key Forms

| Form | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| **PROPERTY RECEIPT - DISCONTINUANCE OF SERVICE (PD520-013)** | Property return documentation for retiring/resigning members |
| **PENSION APPLICATION (PD440-045)** | Grants terminal leave |
| **RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW (PD452-151)** | Completed and signed by resigning member |
| **PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE (PD521-141)** | Lists firearms safeguarded |
| **ACQUISITION OR DISPOSITION OF FIREARMS (PD424-150)** | Report to NY State Police for each firearm |
| **HANDGUN LICENSE APPLICATION (PD643-041)** | Typed and notarized; for NYC residents applying for handgun license upon retirement |

## Key Timeframes

| Item | Timeframe |
|------|-----------|
| Tier 2 reporting to PPF | At least **30 calendar days** in advance |
| Tier 3 reporting to PPF | May report **without notice** |
| Terminal leave PPF reporting | Requested **10 days** prior (not required) |
| LEOSA card expiration | **12 months** from last range requalification |
| NICS check | Within **30 days** prior to separation |
| RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW distribution | Within **5 business days** |
| Reinstatement after resignation | Only by **Police Commissioner** within **1 year** |
| Tier 2 mandatory retirement age | **63** |
| Tier 3 mandatory retirement age | **62** |
| Tier 3 retired ID/LEOSA issuance | **30 days** after discontinuance |
| Terminal leave discontinuance — vacation eligibility | Not for **30 days** after |

## Terminal Leave Restrictions

| Restriction |
|------------|
| No leaves of any kind after terminal leave begins |
| No sick report (unless LOD action while on terminal leave) |
| Excluded from excusal limitations |
| Available only for **emergency situations** |
| Cash overtime only if ordered to work |
| No programmatic overtime, fill details, or non-emergency duties (without bureau chief/DC approval) |

## Resignation Key Rules

| Rule |
|------|
| Bars reinstatement except by Police Commissioner within 1 year |
| Benefits/pension contributions NOT returned until ALL property accounted for |
| Police Commissioner decides if resignation is "without permission" |
| Firearms delivered to **borough Property Clerk's Office ONLY** |
| Report to PPF on last working day in **civilian clothes** |
| Sergeant and clerical member assigned to open locker of absent resignee |

## Memory Aids

> **Memory Aid — TERMINAL LEAVE MATH:** **1 month per 10 years** + **3 days per year beyond 20** + **1 day per 4 months** partial year. Example: 23 years = 2 months (20 years) + 9 days (3 × 3 years) = 2 months + 9 days terminal leave.

> **Memory Aid — TIER 2 vs TIER 3 RETIREMENT AGE:** **Tier 2 = 63rd birthday**, **Tier 3 = 62nd birthday.** Think: "**2** has **3** digits (63), **3** has **2** digits (62)" — or: Tier **2** gets the **higher** age (63), Tier **3** gets the **lower** age (62).

> **Memory Aid — TIER 2 vs TIER 3 PPF NOTICE:** **Tier 2 = 30 days advance notice** ("Tier Two = Takes Time, needs 30 days"). **Tier 3 = No notice required** ("Three = Free, no notice").

> **Memory Aid — RESIGNATION REINSTATEMENT:** Only **Police Commissioner** can reinstate, and only within **1 year.** Think: **PC** = **P**olice **C**ommissioner = **P**erson who has **C**omplete authority.

> **Memory Aid — FIREARMS UPON RESIGNATION:** Deliver to **borough Property Clerk's Office ONLY** ("Resigning = **B**orough **P**roperty **C**lerk, not Manhattan" — actually both Manhattan AND borough PC deliver, but the key exam point is borough PC is where resignations go, not the member's choice). LEOSA holders don't deliver firearms.

questions.text: Terminal leave for a uniformed member with 20 years of service is computed as:
options: B) One calendar month per 10 years of service
questions.answerFull: B) One calendar month (28, 30, or 31 days depending on the month) of leave is granted for each 10 years of service. Beyond 20 years, 3 days per completed year and 1 day per completed 4 months. (A.G. 329-06)
questions.explanation: One calendar month (28, 30, or 31 days depending on the month) of leave is granted for each 10 years of service. Beyond 20 years, 3 days per completed year and 1 day per completed 4 months. (A.G. 329-06)

questions.text: A Tier 2 uniformed member must report to the Police Pension Fund:
options: A) At least 10 calendar days in advance
B) At least 30 calendar days in advance
D) At least 60 calendar days in advance
questions.answerFull: B) Tier 2 members must report at least 30 calendar days in advance of discontinuance of service. Tier 3 members may report without notice. (A.G. 329-06)
questions.explanation: Tier 2 members must report at least 30 calendar days in advance of discontinuance of service. Tier 3 members may report without notice. (A.G. 329-06)

questions.text: A LEOSA Certification Card expires:
options: A) 6 months from date of last range requalification
B) 12 months from date of last range requalification
C) 24 months from date of retirement
questions.answerFull: B) The LEOSA Certification Card will expire 12 months from the date of the member's last range requalification. The member must have qualified during a regular Department range cycle within the past 12 months. (A.G. 329-06)
questions.explanation: The LEOSA Certification Card will expire 12 months from the date of the member's last range requalification. The member must have qualified during a regular Department range cycle within the past 12 months. (A.G. 329-06)

questions.text: After commencement of terminal leave, which of the following may occur?
options: A) Member may be granted vacation
B) Member may be placed on sick report for any illness
C) No leaves of any kind may be granted
D) Member may request comp time
questions.answerFull: C) No leaves of any kind may be granted after commencement of terminal leave, nor may the member be placed on sick report, unless such sick report results from action taken in the line of duty while on terminal leave. (A.G. 329-06)
questions.explanation: No leaves of any kind may be granted after commencement of terminal leave, nor may the member be placed on sick report, unless such sick report results from action taken in the line of duty while on terminal leave. (A.G. 329-06)

questions.text: Resignation bars reinstatement EXCEPT if approved by:
options: A) The Chief of Department within 6 months
B) The Police Commissioner within one year
C) The First Deputy Commissioner within 2 years
D) The Chief of Personnel within 90 days
questions.answerFull: B) Resignation bars reinstatement except if approved by the Police Commissioner within one year of date of resignation. (A.G. 329-07)
questions.explanation: Resignation bars reinstatement except if approved by the Police Commissioner within one year of date of resignation. (A.G. 329-07)

questions.text: When a UMOS resigns, the RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW form must be distributed within:
questions.answerFull: C) The commanding officer signs and distributes the RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW within five business days. (A.G. 329-07)
questions.explanation: The commanding officer signs and distributes the RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW within five business days. (A.G. 329-07)

questions.text: A resigning member's firearms must be delivered to:
options: C) Borough Property Clerk's Office only
questions.answerFull: C) The resigning member must deliver firearms with PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE and ACQUISITION OR DISPOSITION OF FIREARMS report to the borough Property Clerk's Office ONLY. (A.G. 329-07)
questions.explanation: The resigning member must deliver firearms with PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE and ACQUISITION OR DISPOSITION OF FIREARMS report to the borough Property Clerk's Office ONLY. (A.G. 329-07)

questions.text: Tier 2 uniformed members must retire no later than midnight of the eve of their:
questions.answerFull: C) Tier 2 members are required by law to retire no later than midnight of the eve of their 63rd birthday. Tier 3 members must retire no later than midnight of the eve of their 62nd birthday. (A.G. 329-06)
questions.explanation: Tier 2 members are required by law to retire no later than midnight of the eve of their 63rd birthday. Tier 3 members must retire no later than midnight of the eve of their 62nd birthday. (A.G. 329-06)

questions.text: Members on terminal leave are available for duty only during:
questions.answerFull: B) Members on terminal leave, or using accrued time attached to terminal leave, are excluded from excusal limitations and are only available for duty during emergency situations (natural disaster, etc.) or court must-appear notifications. (A.G. 329-06)
questions.explanation: Members on terminal leave, or using accrued time attached to terminal leave, are excluded from excusal limitations and are only available for duty during emergency situations (natural disaster, etc.) or court must-appear notifications. (A.G. 329-06)

questions.text: A UMOS wishing to retain a Department-issued service firearm (issued after 1/15/13) must obtain a NICS check within how many days prior to separation?
questions.answerFull: B) Members must obtain a NICS check through a Federal Firearms Licensee (FFL) no more than 30 days prior to separation from the Department. (A.G. 329-06)
questions.explanation: Members must obtain a NICS check through a Federal Firearms Licensee (FFL) no more than 30 days prior to separation from the Department. (A.G. 329-06)

questions.text: If a UMOS resigns other than in person, who is assigned to open the member's locker?
options: B) A sergeant and clerical member of the service
questions.answerFull: B) The desk officer assigns a sergeant and clerical member of the service to open the member's locker and obtain Department property. (A.G. 329-07)
questions.explanation: The desk officer assigns a sergeant and clerical member of the service to open the member's locker and obtain Department property. (A.G. 329-07)

questions.text: Upon learning of a member's resignation, the Operations Unit notifies all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.answerFull: D) Operations Unit notifies: Communications Section (FINEST Message), Internal Affairs Bureau, Payroll Section, Police Pension Fund, and Human Resources Division. The union representative is not on this notification list. (A.G. 329-07)
questions.explanation: Operations Unit notifies: Communications Section (FINEST Message), Internal Affairs Bureau, Payroll Section, Police Pension Fund, and Human Resources Division. The union representative is not on this notification list. (A.G. 329-07)

sergeantFocus.text: Know the property return matrix. Tier 2 mandatory retirement age is **63**; Tier 3 is **62**. Members on terminal leave are only available for **emergencies**. No leaves after terminal leave starts.

chapters.title: Medical, Health and Wellness
chapters.readme:
# Section 330 — Medical, Health and Wellness

## Source
- **Administrative Guide Section:** 330
- **Source PDFs:** A.G. 330-03, A.G. 330-07, A.G. 330-09; P.G. 330-01, P.G. 330-02, P.G. 330-04, P.G. 330-16
- **Priority:** Medium

## Learning Objectives
- Know the complete line of duty injury investigation and reporting process
- Understand Sick Desk notification triggers for suspicious LOD claims
- Master the hospital privacy rules for injured members
- Know the Trauma Counseling Program scope, timeline, and confidentiality rules
- Understand infectious disease exposure procedures, universal precautions, and decontamination
- Know needle handling procedures and bleach solution protocols
- Understand Regular Sick reporting (Sick Desk call, sick serial number, confinement rules)
- Know Administrative Sick eligibility, 2-day limit, and LIMB LSS MD exclusions
- Know court notification procedures when a member reports sick (Command Diary, Roll Call Clerk)
- Understand Counseling Unit confidentiality exceptions, non-disciplinary referral protections, and EAU conferral requirements

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| A.G. 330-03 | Line of Duty Injury or Death | completed |
| A.G. 330-07 | Trauma Counseling Program | completed |
| A.G. 330-09 | Exposure to Infectious Diseases or Hazardous Materials | completed |
| P.G. 330-01 | Regular Sick | completed |
| P.G. 330-02 | Administrative Sick | completed |
| P.G. 330-04 | Notifying Court When Reporting Sick | completed |
| P.G. 330-16 | Counseling Unit | completed |

## Chapter Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
|  | A.G. 330-03: LOD injury investigation, IS rank requirements, 3 reports (PAR/AIDED/LODI), Sick Desk triggers, hospital privacy, MOS DOA/SILD permitted visitors (5-2-2-2), FECA |
|  | A.G. 330-07: Referral triggers, Trauma Team response, 48-hour follow-up, confidentiality, CO duties |
|  | A.G. 330-09: Universal precautions, exposure procedures, bleach solution ratio, needle handling, Ryan White Act |
|  | P.G. 330-01: Sick Desk call, 3 items received/1 given to D.O., 5 District Surgeon exceptions, confinement rules, travel restrictions, routine visit exemptions, malingering |
|  | P.G. 330-02: UMOS only, 2-day max, LIMB LSS MD exclusions, Admin vs Regular first-call distinction |
|  | P.G. 330-04: Command Diary entries, Roll Call Clerk duties, Operations Coordinator/PRAA duties, condition changes |
|  | P.G. 330-16: Confidentiality exceptions, non-disciplinary referral, misconduct not exempt, EAU conferral for alcohol/DV cases |
|  | LOD, trauma, infectious disease, regular sick, admin sick, court notification, counseling unit definitions; key forms; timeframes; bleach/needle rules |
|  | 25 multiple-choice questions covering all seven procedures |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- **LOD Injury Report:** Sign off electronically before end of tour; include BWC/video findings
- **IS and 3 Reports:** IS always prepares PAR, never prepares AIDED, sometimes prepares LODI
- **Out-of-City LOD:** Investigating supervisor must be captain or above
- **Sick Desk triggers:** Not witnessed, in/near police facility, first 2 hours of tour, off-duty request
- **Hospital privacy:** Only Medical Division address in hospital records — never member's personal info
- **MOS DOA/SILD visitors:** 5-2-2-2 (5 supervisors, 2 investigators, 2 fraternal/religious, 2 conditional)
- **Trauma Counseling:** 48-hour follow-up, strictly confidential, captain+ can request response
- **Infectious disease:** Assign officer who already had contact; patrol supervisor coordinates for multiple exposures
- **Decontamination:** Bleach ratio 1:10, freshly mixed, sink disposal only; one-handed needle scoop technique
- **Regular Sick:** UMOS gets 3 items from Sick Desk but gives D.O. only the sick serial number
- **Admin Sick:** UMOS only (no CMOS); max 2 days; call command first (not Sick Desk); LIMB LSS MD exclusions
- **Court notification:** Command Diary under appearance date AND 2 days prior (excl. weekends/holidays)
- **Counseling Unit:** Supervisors are vital link in detection/referral; non-disciplinary referral = no report, no promo impact; EAU conferral required before adjudication of alcohol/DV misconduct
chapters.keyTerms:
# Section 330 — Key Terms and Definitions

## Line of Duty Injury

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Investigating Supervisor** | Sergeant or above from injured member's command; if unavailable, from precinct of occurrence |
| **Out of City LOD** | Investigating supervisor must be **captain or above** |
| **Line of Duty Injury Report** | Prepared via LOD Injury Reporting System (intranet or smartphone) |
| **FECA** | Federal Employees Compensation Act — benefits when Federal statute violated during apprehension |

## Trauma Counseling

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Trauma Counseling Team** | Staffed by Chief Surgeon, Director of Behavioral Care and Support Section, and licensed professionals of Medical Division |
| **Behavioral Care and Support Section** | Location for 48-hour follow-up meeting |
| **POPPA** | Police Organization Providing Peer Assistance — separate from Trauma Counseling |
| **Employee Assistance Programs** | Multiple programs available; Trauma Counseling does not prevent voluntary use of any |

## Infectious Disease/Hazmat

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Infectious Disease** | Diseases transmitted by contact with blood, body fluids, animal saliva, or CNS tissue |
| **Hazardous Material** | Any chemical, biological, or radiological health hazard |
| **Universal Precautions** | Treat all blood and body fluids as infectious for HIV, HBV, and other bloodborne pathogens |
| **Designated Officer** | Individual designated under Ryan White Act (42 U.S.C. 300ff-86) for exposure determinations |

## Key Forms

| Form | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| **WITNESS STATEMENT - INJURY TO MEMBER (PD429-065)** | Non-Department witness statement for LOD injury |
| **Police Accident Report (MV104AN)** | If vehicle collision involved in LOD injury |
| **AIDED REPORT (PD304-152)** | If no vehicle involved in LOD injury |
| **UNUSUAL OCCURRENCE REPORT (PD370-152)** | For LOD death/serious injury; also for Trauma Counseling incidents |
| **SCOC M187** | Report of Inmate Death to NYS Commission of Correction |
| **Biohazard Label** | Affixed to contaminated property, evidence, or waste |

## Key Timeframes

| Item | Timeframe |
|------|-----------|
| LOD Injury Report sign-off by investigating supervisor | **Before end of tour** |
| Trauma Counseling follow-up meeting | **48 hours** after event |
| Additional trauma counseling sessions | **1 to 6 weeks** after incident |
| FECA documentation forwarding | Within **90 days** of occurrence |
| Delayed LOD designation report | Within **60 days** (then every 60 days) |
| Exposure report number | Obtained via LOD Injury Reporting System |

## Sick Desk Notification Triggers (LOD Injury)

| Trigger |
|---------|
| Incident NOT specifically witnessed |
| Injury in or near any **police facility** |
| Injury during **first 2 hours** of tour |
| Off-duty LOD designation request |

## Bleach Solution for Blood/Body Fluid Spills

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Ratio | **1 part bleach : 10 parts water** |
| Preparation | FRESHLY MIXED for each use |
| Do NOT mix with | Any solution other than water |
| Do NOT use on | Uniforms (causes damage) or hands |
| Disposal | In sink only (not urinal/toilet — chemical deodorizers cause toxic gas) |

## Needle Handling

| Rule |
|------|
| One-handed "scoop" technique |
| Move needle AWAY from yourself |
| NEVER remove, dislodge, or bend a needle |
| Cap container if possible; tape if syringe extends |

## Regular Sick (P.G. 330-01)

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Regular Sick** | Member not eligible for or declines Admin Sick; requires visit to Department Surgeon |
| **District Surgeon** | Report at next office hours; 5 exceptions (Chronic B, Fri/Sat, unable to travel, dental, Medical Division directs) |
| **Sick Serial Number** | Obtained from Sick Desk; the ONLY item UMOS passes to D.O. |
| **Absence Control and Investigations Unit** | Medical Division unit notified by D.O. when malingering is suspected |
| **Sick Reporting System** | Queried by D.O. and Operations Coordinator to verify sick status |
| **Limited Capacity** | Must visit Surgeon to request; exception: pregnant members requesting position limited — no visit required |

## Administrative Sick (P.G. 330-02)

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Admin Sick** | UMOS-only (no CMOS); max 2 days; call D.O. at least 2 hours pre-tour; no Surgeon visit |
| **LIMB LSS MD** | Mnemonic for when Admin Sick is NOT authorized: Line of duty, Injury, Mutual, Chronic B, Late, Stress, Supervisor deems inappropriate, Major illness, Dental |
| **One Sick Event** | Days 1-3 (Admin + Regular) consolidate into a single sick event |

## Notifying Court When Sick (P.G. 330-04)

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Command Diary** | Entry made under appearance date AND 2 days prior (excluding Sat/Sun/Holidays) |
| **Roll Call Clerk** | Notifies court of inability to attend; requests adjournment 2 days before; notifies on morning of appearance (except Criminal Court) |
| **Operations Coordinator / PRAA** | Calls District Surgeon 2 days prior to determine if member can appear |

## Counseling Unit (P.G. 330-16)

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Counseling Unit** | Assists MOS with alcohol, prescription drug, gambling, or financial difficulties; return to full duty |
| **Non-Disciplinary Referral** | No report made; promotions NOT jeopardized; assignment NOT ordinarily changed |
| **Confidentiality Exceptions** | Written consent, valid subpoena, U.S. District Court Order, suspected child abuse, danger to self/others |
| **EAU Conferral** | Required before final adjudication of ALL misconduct involving alcohol or domestic violence |

## Key Timeframes (Updated)

| Item | Timeframe |
|------|-----------|
| LOD Injury Report sign-off by investigating supervisor | **Before end of tour** |
| Trauma Counseling follow-up meeting | **48 hours** after event |
| Additional trauma counseling sessions | **1 to 6 weeks** after incident |
| FECA documentation forwarding | Within **90 days** of occurrence |
| Delayed LOD designation report | Within **60 days** (then every 60 days) |
| Admin Sick notification to D.O. | At least **2 hours** before start of tour |
| Admin Sick maximum duration | **2 days** (3rd day becomes Regular Sick) |
| Court notification — Command Diary | Appearance date AND **2 days prior** (excl. weekends/holidays) |
| UMOS out sick outside resident counties | Contacted **weekly** by District Surgeon |

questions.text: The investigating supervisor for a line of duty injury must sign off on the LOD Injury Report:
options: C) Prior to completion of tour
D) By end of the following tour
questions.answerFull: C) The investigating supervisor reviews for completeness and accuracy, and signs off electronically prior to completion of tour, absent exceptional circumstances. (A.G. 330-03)
questions.explanation: The investigating supervisor reviews for completeness and accuracy, and signs off electronically prior to completion of tour, absent exceptional circumstances. (A.G. 330-03)

questions.text: For a line of duty injury occurring outside of New York City, the investigating supervisor must be in the rank of:
questions.answerFull: C) The investigating supervisor for out-of-City line of duty injuries must be in the rank of captain or above. (A.G. 330-03)
questions.explanation: The investigating supervisor for out-of-City line of duty injuries must be in the rank of captain or above. (A.G. 330-03)

questions.text: The desk officer must notify the Sick Desk supervisor for a LOD injury when:
options: A) The injury occurred during any part of the member's tour
B) The injury occurred during the first two hours of a tour
C) The member was treated and released from the hospital
D) The injury was witnessed by multiple members
questions.answerFull: B) Sick Desk notification is required when: incident was not specifically witnessed, injury in/near police facility, injury during first 2 hours of tour, or off-duty LOD designation request. (A.G. 330-03)
questions.explanation: Sick Desk notification is required when: incident was not specifically witnessed, injury in/near police facility, injury during first 2 hours of tour, or off-duty LOD designation request. (A.G. 330-03)

questions.text: When a UMOS is treated at a hospital for a LOD injury, which address should be entered in hospital records?
options: B) The precinct of occurrence address
C) The Medical Division, 1 Lefrak City Plaza address
D) The member's permanent command address
questions.answerFull: C) Only the Medical Division address (1 Lefrak City Plaza, 59-17 Junction Boulevard, Corona, NY 11368) may be entered in hospital records. Member's command, home address, and telephone number will not be disclosed. (A.G. 330-03)
questions.explanation: Only the Medical Division address (1 Lefrak City Plaza, 59-17 Junction Boulevard, Corona, NY 11368) may be entered in hospital records. Member's command, home address, and telephone number will not be disclosed. (A.G. 330-03)

questions.text: When a UMOS is struck by a bullet on a protective vest and is apparently uninjured:
options: A) No further action is required
B) The member may continue duty without medical evaluation
C) The member must be examined by a doctor for blunt body trauma or internal injury
D) The member is placed on sick report for 24 hours
questions.answerFull: C) When an injured member is struck by a bullet on a protective vest and is apparently uninjured, the member must be examined by a doctor to ascertain if a blunt body trauma or internal injury has occurred. (A.G. 330-03)
questions.explanation: When an injured member is struck by a bullet on a protective vest and is apparently uninjured, the member must be examined by a doctor to ascertain if a blunt body trauma or internal injury has occurred. (A.G. 330-03)

questions.text: The Trauma Counseling follow-up meeting should occur:
options: B) 48 hours after the event or initial contact
C) One week after the event
questions.answerFull: B) The member will be contacted for a follow-up meeting 48 hours after the event or initial contact. Additional counseling/interview sessions are scheduled within 1 to 6 weeks after the incident. (A.G. 330-07)
questions.explanation: The member will be contacted for a follow-up meeting 48 hours after the event or initial contact. Additional counseling/interview sessions are scheduled within 1 to 6 weeks after the incident. (A.G. 330-07)

questions.text: All conversations with the Trauma Counseling Team are:
options: A) Reported to the commanding officer
B) Recorded in the Command Log
D) Shared with Internal Affairs Bureau
questions.answerFull: C) ALL conversation with the Trauma Counseling Team by members of the service (uniformed and civilian) will be STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL. (A.G. 330-07)
questions.explanation: ALL conversation with the Trauma Counseling Team by members of the service (uniformed and civilian) will be STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL. (A.G. 330-07)

questions.text: The Trauma Counseling Team responds to all incidents involving a member being shot, killed, or seriously injured in the line of duty, AND:
options: A) Any incident at the discretion of the desk officer
B) Any incident pursuant to a request from a captain or above
C) Only when directed by Internal Affairs Bureau
questions.answerFull: B) The Trauma Counseling Team will respond to all incidents involving MOS shot/killed/seriously injured in LOD, and any incident pursuant to a request from a captain or above. (A.G. 330-07)
questions.explanation: The Trauma Counseling Team will respond to all incidents involving MOS shot/killed/seriously injured in LOD, and any incident pursuant to a request from a captain or above. (A.G. 330-07)

questions.text: The correct ratio for the bleach solution used to clean blood or body fluid spills is:
options: A) 1 part bleach to 5 parts water
B) 1 part bleach to 10 parts water
C) 1 part bleach to 20 parts water
questions.answerFull: B) Accidental spills may be cleaned by applying a FRESHLY MIXED solution of 1 part household bleach to 10 parts water. The solution must be freshly mixed for each use and must not be mixed with any solution other than water. (A.G. 330-09)
questions.explanation: Accidental spills may be cleaned by applying a FRESHLY MIXED solution of 1 part household bleach to 10 parts water. The solution must be freshly mixed for each use and must not be mixed with any solution other than water. (A.G. 330-09)

questions.text: When handling hypodermic needles, members should use the:
questions.answerFull: B) Members use a one-handed "scoop" technique, using the needle itself to pick up the container, moving the needle away from themselves. Members should NOT hold the container and move their hand toward the needle. Under no circumstances should a member attempt to remove, dislodge, or bend a needle. (A.G. 330-09)
questions.explanation: Members use a one-handed "scoop" technique, using the needle itself to pick up the container, moving the needle away from themselves. Members should NOT hold the container and move their hand toward the needle. Under no circumstances should a member attempt to remove, dislodge, or bend a needle. (A.G. 330-09)

questions.text: When multiple individuals are exposed to an infectious disease at a scene, who coordinates documentation?
options: B) The patrol supervisor at the scene
C) The first member to arrive
questions.answerFull: B) If there are numerous individuals involved, the patrol supervisor at the scene will be responsible for the coordination of information and documenting the names of the members of the service involved. (A.G. 330-09)
questions.explanation: If there are numerous individuals involved, the patrol supervisor at the scene will be responsible for the coordination of information and documenting the names of the members of the service involved. (A.G. 330-09)

questions.text: Under A.G. 330-09, to avoid unnecessary exposure at a scene, control of an aided victim suspected of having an infectious disease should be assigned to:
options: C) An officer who has already had contact with the person
questions.answerFull: C) To avoid unnecessary exposure, control of the aided/collision victim or prisoner will be assigned to an officer at the scene who has already had contact with a person suspected of having an infectious disease. (A.G. 330-09)
questions.explanation: To avoid unnecessary exposure, control of the aided/collision victim or prisoner will be assigned to an officer at the scene who has already had contact with a person suspected of having an infectious disease. (A.G. 330-09)

questions.text: If a LOD designation cannot be made expeditiously, the ranking officer must forward a Typed Letterhead to Medical Division within:
questions.answerFull: B) When a LOD designation cannot be made expeditiously, a report on Typed Letterhead indicating reasons for the delay must be forwarded to Medical Division within 60 days. Subsequent reports are forwarded for each additional 60-day period. (A.G. 330-03)
questions.explanation: When a LOD designation cannot be made expeditiously, a report on Typed Letterhead indicating reasons for the delay must be forwarded to Medical Division within 60 days. Subsequent reports are forwarded for each additional 60-day period. (A.G. 330-03)

questions.text: If an injury comes under the Federal Employees Compensation Act (FECA), the CO must forward documentation to Medical Division within:
questions.answerFull: C) If the injury comes under FECA, the member's CO will forward items (LOD Injury Report, witness statements, arrest history, criminal court complaint, firearms trace, completed DOL form BEC-721a) to Medical Division, Federal Compensation Liaison Officer, within 90 days of occurrence. (A.G. 330-03)
questions.explanation: If the injury comes under FECA, the member's CO will forward items (LOD Injury Report, witness statements, arrest history, criminal court complaint, firearms trace, completed DOL form BEC-721a) to Medical Division, Federal Compensation Liaison Officer, within 90 days of occurrence. (A.G. 330-03)

questions.text: The bleach solution for cleaning blood spills should be disposed of:
options: B) In a urinal or toilet
D) In a hazardous waste container
questions.answerFull: C) Disposal of bleach or bleach dilutions should be performed only in a sink (not a urinal or toilet, since they sometimes contain chemical deodorizers that may cause toxic gas when mixed with bleach). (A.G. 330-09)
questions.explanation: Disposal of bleach or bleach dilutions should be performed only in a sink (not a urinal or toilet, since they sometimes contain chemical deodorizers that may cause toxic gas when mixed with bleach). (A.G. 330-09)

questions.text: A UMOS going out on regular sick obtains three items from the Sick Desk. Which of the following should the UMOS provide to the desk officer at the permanent command?
options: A) Name of Sick Desk member notified
B) Time Sick Desk was notified
D) Change of address/location or phone number
questions.answerFull: C) The UMOS receives 3 items from the Sick Desk (sick serial number, Sick Desk name, and time notified), but only provides the **sick serial number** to the D.O./Supervisor. (P.G. 330-01)
questions.explanation: The UMOS receives 3 items from the Sick Desk (sick serial number, Sick Desk name, and time notified), but only provides the **sick serial number** to the D.O./Supervisor. (P.G. 330-01)

questions.text: Administrative Sick is available to:
options: A) All members of the service
B) Uniformed members of the service only — CMOS cannot go Admin Sick
C) Civilian members of the service only
D) Members with more than 5 years of service
questions.answerFull: B) Administrative Sick is for UMOS only. Civilian members of the service cannot go Administrative Sick. (P.G. 330-02)
questions.explanation: Administrative Sick is for UMOS only. Civilian members of the service cannot go Administrative Sick. (P.G. 330-02)

questions.text: On the 3rd consecutive day of Administrative Sick, the member must:
options: A) Continue on Admin Sick with no changes
B) Report Regular Sick and be guided by Sick Desk regarding District Surgeon visit
D) Report directly to the District Surgeon without calling anyone
questions.answerFull: B) Admin Sick lasts a maximum of 2 days. On the 3rd day, it converts to Regular Sick. The member is guided by the Sick Desk regarding the Medical Division visit to the District Surgeon. All three days consolidate into 1 sick event. (P.G. 330-02)
questions.explanation: Admin Sick lasts a maximum of 2 days. On the 3rd day, it converts to Regular Sick. The member is guided by the Sick Desk regarding the Medical Division visit to the District Surgeon. All three days consolidate into 1 sick event. (P.G. 330-02)

questions.text: For Administrative Sick, the UMOS first calls:
options: A) The Sick Desk, then the command
B) The D.O./Supervisor at their command — not the Sick Desk
questions.answerFull: B) For Admin Sick, the UMOS calls the command (D.O./Supervisor) at least 2 hours pre-tour. For Regular Sick, the UMOS calls the Sick Desk first, then the command. This is a key distinction. (P.G. 330-02)
questions.explanation: For Admin Sick, the UMOS calls the command (D.O./Supervisor) at least 2 hours pre-tour. For Regular Sick, the UMOS calls the Sick Desk first, then the command. This is a key distinction. (P.G. 330-02)

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a reason Admin Sick would be denied (per the LIMB LSS MD mnemonic)?
questions.answerFull: B) The LIMB LSS MD mnemonic lists: Line of duty, Injury, Mutual, Chronic B, Late (less than 2 hrs pre-tour), Stress/psychological, Supervisor deems inappropriate, Major illness, Dental. A simple headache is not on the exclusion list. (P.G. 330-02)
questions.explanation: The LIMB LSS MD mnemonic lists: Line of duty, Injury, Mutual, Chronic B, Late (less than 2 hrs pre-tour), Stress/psychological, Supervisor deems inappropriate, Major illness, Dental. A simple headache is not on the exclusion list. (P.G. 330-02)

questions.text: When a member is out sick and has a pending court appearance, the desk officer makes a Command Diary entry under:
options: A) The date of appearance only
B) The date of appearance and 1 day prior
C) The date of appearance and 2 days prior (not counting Sat/Sun/Holidays)
D) The date of appearance and 3 days prior
questions.answerFull: C) The D.O. makes a Command Diary entry under both the date of appearance AND 2 days prior, not counting Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays. (P.G. 330-04)
questions.explanation: The D.O. makes a Command Diary entry under both the date of appearance AND 2 days prior, not counting Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays. (P.G. 330-04)

questions.text: The Counseling Unit provides assistance to MOS experiencing difficulties with all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.answerFull: D) The Counseling Unit's primary function is assisting MOS with difficulties involving alcohol, prescription drugs, gambling, or finances. Marital counseling is not listed as a primary function. (P.G. 330-16)
questions.explanation: The Counseling Unit's primary function is assisting MOS with difficulties involving alcohol, prescription drugs, gambling, or finances. Marital counseling is not listed as a primary function. (P.G. 330-16)

questions.text: A non-disciplinary referral to the Counseling Unit results in:
options: A) A report being filed with IAB
C) No report, no jeopardized promotions, and assignment not ordinarily changed
D) Automatic transfer to a different command
questions.answerFull: C) A non-disciplinary referral produces no report, does not jeopardize promotions, and the member's current assignment is not ordinarily changed (though it may be if in best interest of all). (P.G. 330-16)
questions.explanation: A non-disciplinary referral produces no report, does not jeopardize promotions, and the member's current assignment is not ordinarily changed (though it may be if in best interest of all). (P.G. 330-16)

questions.text: The investigating supervisor for a LOD injury within the city must be:
options: B) A sergeant or above from the injured member's command
D) Any available supervisor regardless of command
questions.answerFull: B) Within the city, the investigating supervisor is a sergeant or above from the injured member's command. If unavailable, a supervisor from the precinct of occurrence. Out of city requires captain or above. (A.G. 330-03)
questions.explanation: Within the city, the investigating supervisor is a sergeant or above from the injured member's command. If unavailable, a supervisor from the precinct of occurrence. Out of city requires captain or above. (A.G. 330-03)

questions.text: Regarding the investigating supervisor and the three LOD reports (PAR, AIDED, LODI), which statement is correct?
options: A) IS always prepares all three reports
B) IS always prepares the PAR, never prepares the AIDED, and sometimes prepares the LODI
C) IS never prepares any of the three reports
D) IS always prepares the AIDED and the LODI
questions.answerFull: B) The Investigating Supervisor always prepares the Police Accident Report. The IS never prepares the AIDED (directs injured MOS to prepare it, or assigns another member if incapacitated). The IS sometimes prepares the LODI (only when the injured member is incapacitated). (A.G. 330-03)
questions.explanation: The Investigating Supervisor always prepares the Police Accident Report. The IS never prepares the AIDED (directs injured MOS to prepare it, or assigns another member if incapacitated). The IS sometimes prepares the LODI (only when the injured member is incapacitated). (A.G. 330-03)

sergeantFocus.text: Coordinate documentation of all involved MOS names when numerous individuals are exposed. Ensure exposure report number obtained via LOD Injury Reporting System. If actual injury → comply with A.G. 330-03. Know the bleach ratio and needle handling rules for roll call training.

Sign off LOD Injury Report electronically **before end of tour**. Include BWC/video review findings. If member dies or likely to die, ensure firearms and Department property are safeguarded. Comply with P.G. 221-06 if any force was used. FECA indicator required on LOD report.

Ensure involved members are available for Trauma Team interview. Verify members have been contacted in person. Prepare UOR listing contacted members. Captain or above can request Trauma Team response to any incident.

chapters.title: Evaluations
chapters.readme:
# Section 331 — Evaluations

## Source
- **Admin Guide Section:** 331
- **Source PDF:** Public Admin Guide2.pdf (pages 213-229)
- **Priority:** Medium

## Overview

Section 331 governs the performance evaluation process for all uniformed and civilian members of the NYPD. It covers six procedures addressing evaluations by rank — from probationary police officers through deputy chiefs — including the annual evaluation cycle, probationary evaluation schedules, the PERF system for non-investigative personnel, and the Management By Objectives (MBO) system for captains and above. Critical for the Sergeant exam: understanding probationary evaluation timelines, Chronic Absent impact on ratings, reviewer rank requirements, and the PERF scoring scale.

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Pages | Status |
|-----------|-------|-------|--------|
| A.G. 331-01 | Evaluations — General — Members of the Service | 6 | Complete |
| A.G. 331-02 | Evaluation of Probationary Officers | 2 | Complete |
| A.G. 331-03 | Evaluation of POs/Detectives — Investigative Duties | 1 | Complete |
| A.G. 331-04 | Evaluations of POs/Detectives — Non-Investigative Duties | 4 | Complete |
| A.G. 331-05 | Evaluations of Sergeants/Lieutenants | 2 | Complete |
| A.G. 331-06 | Evaluation of Captain Through Deputy Chief | 2 | Complete |

## Priority Study Topics

1. **Probationary evaluation schedules** — PO (10/16/22), Sgt/Lt (4/10), Captain (4/10/16), Investigative (8/15)
2. **PERF scoring scale** — 90-100 Exceptional, 77-89 Exceeds Expectations, 68-76 Meets Standards, 60-67 Needs Improvement, 59 or below Unsatisfactory
3. **Chronic Absent impact** — Category A (consider attendance), Category B (consider Very Low/Unsatisfactory)
4. **Reviewer rank requirements** — below competent or extremely competent: captain or above; Performance Monitoring Program: commanding officer
5. **22nd month recommendation** — Permanent Appointment, Extension of Probation, or Termination of Services
6. **MBO system** — captains through deputy chiefs, five Key Result Areas (KRAs)
7. **Military absence rule** — average of 3 prior evals, minimum satisfactory (3.0)

## Study Tips

- Focus on the **probationary evaluation month numbers** — these are prime exam material
- The **PERF score-to-rating conversion** table is highly testable
- Know the difference between the **Online Evaluation System** (most ranks) and the **PERF System** (non-investigative POs/detectives)
- Understand when the **commanding officer** must serve as reviewer vs. when a **captain or above** is required
- Detective squad supervisor evaluations require **conferral with the precinct CO** — a frequent exam topic
chapters.keyTerms:
# Section 331 — Key Terms & Critical Numbers

## Evaluation Systems by Rank

| Rank/Title | Evaluation System | Form/Method |
|------------|------------------|-------------|
| Probationary Police Officer | Online Evaluation System | Recruit Evaluation (PD439-1412) + Online |
| Police Officer/Detective (non-investigative) | PERF System | Performance Evaluation (PERF) |
| Police Officer (investigative/Detective Track) | Online Evaluation System | Performance Evaluation — Detectives/Detective Track |
| Sergeant (probationary) | Online Evaluation System | Performance Evaluation Sergeant |
| Sergeant (permanent) | Online Evaluation System | Performance Evaluation Reports |
| Lieutenant (probationary/permanent) | Online Evaluation System | Performance Evaluation Lieutenant |
| Captain through Deputy Chief | Online Evaluation System | Management By Objectives (MBO) — 5 KRAs |
| Assistant Chief and above/Civilian Managers | Managerial Performance Evaluation Form | Managerial Performance Evaluation |

## Probationary Evaluation Schedules

| Rank | Evaluation Months | Notes |
|------|------------------|-------|
| Probationary Police Officer | 10th, 16th, 22nd | 22nd month: recommend Permanent/Extension/Termination |
| Probationary Sergeant | 4th, 10th (16th if extended) | 12-month probation, extendable 6 months |
| Probationary Lieutenant | 4th, 10th (16th if extended) | 12-month probation, extendable 6 months |
| Probationary Captain | 4th, 10th, 16th | 12-month probation, extendable 6 months |
| Investigative Track PO | 8th, 15th | Then annually after 15 months |

## Performance Evaluation Rating Schedule (Appendix "A")

| Rank/Title | Rating Period | Due Date |
|------------|-------------|----------|
| Police Officer/Detective (non-investigative) | Jan 1 - Dec 31 | January 31st |
| PO assigned to investigative track | End of 8th and 15th month | Immediately after rating period |
| Detective (investigative) | May 16 - May 15 | June 15th |
| Sergeant | Jan 16 - Jan 15 | February 15th |
| Lieutenant | Jan 16 - Jan 15 | February 15th |
| Captain through Deputy Chief | June 30 - July 1 | August 1st |
| Assistant Chief and above | Jan 1 - Dec 31 | January 31st |
| Civilian Sub-managerial | Sep 16 - Sep 15 | October 15th |
| School Safety Agents | July 1 - June 30 | July 30th |

## PERF System Annual Score Conversion (Non-Investigative)

| Total Annual Score | Overall Evaluation |
|---|---|
| **90-100** | Exceptional |
| **77-89** | Exceeds Expectations |
| **68-76** | Meets Standards |
| **60-67** | Needs Improvement |
| **59 or below** | Unsatisfactory |

## Critical Reviewer Requirements

| Condition | Required Reviewer Rank |
|-----------|----------------------|
| Below competent (2.5 or lower) — 331-01 | Captain or above |
| Extremely competent (5.0) — 331-01 | Captain or above |
| Needs improvement/unsatisfactory (67 or lower) — 331-04 | Captain or above |
| Exceptional (90 or higher) — 331-04 | Captain or above |
| Performance Monitoring Program member | Commanding Officer |

## Chronic Absent Impact on Evaluations

| Category | Evaluation Guidance |
|----------|-------------------|
| Chronic Absent - Category A | Give consideration to attendance record in determining overall rating |
| Chronic Absent - Category B | Consider evaluating "Very Low" (331-01) or "Unsatisfactory" (331-04) |
| Category B (investigative) | Presumed substandard; if rated higher than "Low," rater must justify |

## Key Forms

| Form | Number | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| Force Record | PD406-143 | Reviewed/updated during evaluation interview |
| Recruit Evaluation | PD439-1412 | Probationary PO evaluation (2 copies from Academy) |
| Absence and Tardiness Record | PD433-145 | Supports below-standards rating |
| Civilian Record of Supervisory Conferences | PD433-1411 | Supports below-standards rating |
| Civilian Lateness/Warning Notification | PD449-122 | Supports below-standards rating |
| Civilian Unsatisfactory Performance Warning | PD439-012 | Supports below-standards rating |

## Military Absence Rule

- Member still entitled to a Performance Evaluation
- Rating = **average of 3 prior evaluations**
- Credited rating must not be less than **satisfactory (3.0)** or less than the rating immediately prior to military absence
- Rater notes military duty period in "Overall Rater's Comments"

## Key Distinctions

| Concept | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| Appeal rights | Probationary members and civilians with < 2 years provisional status **cannot appeal** |
| Probation extension request | Made after **6 months** by CO, with **4th month evaluation**, forwarded to Deputy Commissioner, Risk Management |
| Evaluation prepared in error | Submit **Typed Letterhead** to Performance Analysis Unit to void/delete |
| Precinct detective squad supervisor eval | Rater must **confer with precinct CO** and note in Rater Comments |
| Transfer evaluation | Must be prepared; forwarded to **new command** |
| Rated by command on | **Last day of rating period** |

questions.text: How often must each uniformed and civilian member of the service be evaluated?
options: B) At least once each year
questions.answerFull: B) Each uniformed and civilian member of the service must be evaluated at least once each year. (A.G. 331-01, Purpose)
questions.explanation: Each uniformed and civilian member of the service must be evaluated at least once each year. (A.G. 331-01, Purpose)

questions.text: When a rater evaluates a member and rates any Performance Area/Behavioral Dimension below competent, the rater must:
options: C) Comment on all areas rated below competent (rated 2 or 1)
D) Forward the evaluation to the Chief of Department
questions.answerFull: C) The rater must comment on all Performance Areas/Behavioral Dimensions rated below competent (i.e., rated either 2 or 1). (A.G. 331-01, step #5)
questions.explanation: The rater must comment on all Performance Areas/Behavioral Dimensions rated below competent (i.e., rated either 2 or 1). (A.G. 331-01, step #5)

questions.text: If a ratee receives an overall evaluation of below competent (2.5 or lower) or is rated extremely competent (5.0), the reviewer must be in the rank of:
questions.answerFull: C) The reviewer must be in the rank of captain or above, or selected civilian managerial titles. (A.G. 331-01, step #24a)
questions.explanation: The reviewer must be in the rank of captain or above, or selected civilian managerial titles. (A.G. 331-01, step #24a)

questions.text: A probationary police officer is evaluated via the Online Evaluation System how many times during the probationary period?
questions.answerFull: B) Probationary police officers are evaluated three times during their probationary period: the tenth, sixteenth, and twenty-second month. (A.G. 331-02, step #5)
questions.explanation: Probationary police officers are evaluated three times during their probationary period: the tenth, sixteenth, and twenty-second month. (A.G. 331-02, step #5)

questions.text: The twenty-second month evaluation of a probationary police officer must specifically recommend which of the following?
options: B) Permanent Appointment, Extension of Probation, or Termination of Services
C) Competent, Below Competent, or Highly Competent
D) Continue Probation or End Probation
questions.answerFull: B) The twenty-second month evaluation must specifically recommend "Permanent Appointment," "Extension of Probation" or "Termination of Services" in the reviewer's comments area. (A.G. 331-02, step #6)
